questions.question: The potential difference applied to an X-ray tube is decreased. As a result, in the emitted radiation,
questions.solution:
Minimum wavelength for X-ray is given by

$${\lambda _{\min }} = {{12375} \over V}\mathop A\limits^o $$

As the accelerating voltage V is decreased, $$\lambda$$min increases. The intensity of emitted radiation is determined by the number of electrons bombarding the target.

$$\therefore$$ Accelerating voltage does not change the intensity of X-rays emitted.

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2021 Paper 2 Online
questions.question: In a photoemission experiment, the maximum kinetic energies of photoelectrons from metals P, Q and R are E<sub>P</sub>, E<sub>Q</sub> and E<sub>R</sub>, respectively, and they are related by E<sub>P</sub> = 2E<sub>Q</sub> = 2E<sub>R</sub>. In this experiment, the same source of monochromatic light is used for metals P and Q while a different source of monochromatic light is used for the metal R. The work functions for metals P, Q and R are 4.0 eV, 4.5 eV and 5.5 eV, respectively. The energy of the incident photon used for metal R, in eV, is ___________.
questions.solution:
EP = 2EQ = 2ER = E(assume)

For metal P and Q,

E1 - 4 = EP = E ……..(1)

E1 - 4.5 = EA = E/2 ……..(2)

Subtracting equation (2) from (1), we get

E/2 = 0.5

For metal R

E2 - 5.5 = ER = E/2 = 0.5

$$ \Rightarrow $$ E2 = 6

questions.title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
questions.question: As per the data up to November, 2020, released by the Union Finance Ministry, which one of the following countries ranks 1 in terms of ODI (Outward Direct Investment) for the year 2020-21?
questions.solution: Singapore was ranked first in terms of Outward Direct Investment (ODI) for the year 2020-21 as per the data released by the Union Finance Ministry.

questions.title: BITSAT 2021
questions.question: For a constant hydraulic stress on an object, the fractional change in the object's volume $$\left( {{{\Delta V} \over V}} \right)$$ and its bulk modulus B are related as
questions.solution:
Bulk modulus $$ = {{Hydraulic\,stress} \over {Volumetric\,strain}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow B = {{Hydraulic\,stress} \over {{{\Delta V} \over V}}}$$

or, $${{\Delta V} \over V} = $$ Hydraulic stress $$ \times {1 \over B}$$

$$\therefore$$ For constant hydraulic stress, $${{\Delta V} \over V} \propto {1 \over B}$$

questions.title: BITSAT 2021
questions.question: The molar conductivities of KCl, NaCl and KNO3 are 152, 128 and 111 S cm2 mol$$-$$1 respectively. What is the molar conductivity of NaNO3?
questions.solution:
NaCl + KNO3 $$\to$$ NaNO3 + KCl

Sum of molar conductivity of reactant = Sum of molar conductivity of products

$$\Lambda ^\circ (NaN{O_3}) = \Lambda _m^o(NaCl) + \Lambda _m^o(KN{O_3}) - \Lambda _m^o(KCl)$$

$$KCl - NaCl = - \Lambda _m^oKCl - \Lambda _m^o(NaCl) = 24$$

Molar conductivities of KNO3 = 111

Molar conductivities of NaNO3 = 111 $$-$$ 24 = 87 S cm2 mol$$-$$1

questions.title: BITSAT 2021
questions.question: The approximate time duration in hours to electroplate 30 g of calcium from molten calcium chloride using a current of 5 A is (Atomic mass of Ca = 40)
questions.solution:
Ca2+ + 2e$$-$$ $$\to$$ Ca (at cathode)

1 mole CaCl2 = 2F

From Q = it $$\Rightarrow$$ n . 2F = it

$$ \Rightarrow t = {{n\,.\,2F} \over i} = {{30} \over {40}} \times {{2 \times 96500} \over 5}$$

= 28950 s $$ = {{28960} \over {60 \times 60}} = 8.04$$ = 8 h

questions.title: BITSAT 2021
questions.question:
Given, the reduction potential of Na+, Mg2+, Al3+ and Ag+ as $$E_{N{a^ + }/Na}^o$$ = $$-$$ 2.17 V; $$E_{M{g^{2 + }}/Mg}^o$$ = $$-$$ 2.37 V; $$E_{A{g^ + }/Ag}^o$$ = $$-$$ 0.08 V.

The least stable oxide is
questions.solution:
As the value of reduction potential of metal ion increases, the tendency of metal oxide to get reduce into metal increases.

Since, reduction potential of only Ag is positive among the given, thus Ag2O readily gets reduced to Ag metal. In other words, it can be said that Ag2O is the least stable oxide among the given.

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2021 Paper 2 Online
questions.question:
Some standard electrode potentials at 298 K are given below :

Pb2+ /Pb = $$- $$0.13 V

Ni2+ /Ni = $$-$$ 0.24 V

Cd2+ /Cd = $$-$$ 0.40 V

Fe2+ /Fe = $$-$$ 0.44 V

To a solution containing 0.001 M of X2+ and 0.1 M of Y2+, the metal rods X and Y are inserted (at 298 K) and connected by a conducting wire. This resulted in dissolution of X. The correct combination(s) of X and Y, respectively, is(are)

(Given : Gas constant, R = 8.314 J K$$-$$ mol$$-$$1, Faraday constant, F = 96500 C mol$$-$$1)
questions.solution:
Given,

At anode : $$X(s) \to \mathop {{X^{2 + }}(aq)}\limits_{(0.001\,M)} + 2{e^ - }$$

At cathode : $$\mathop {{Y^{2 + }}(aq)}\limits_{(0.1\,M)} + 2{e^ - } \to Y(s)$$

Overall reaction $$X(s) + {Y^{2 + }}(aq) \to {X^{2 + }}(aq) + Y(s)$$

Nernst equation,

$${E_{cell}} = E_{cell}^o - {{0.06} \over 2}\log {{[{X^{2 + }}]} \over {[{Y^{2 + }}]}}$$

$${E_{cell}} = E_{cell}^o - 0.03\log {{0.001} \over {0.1}}$$

$${E_{cell}} = E_{cell}^o + 0.06$$

Ecell should be positive for a reaction to be spontaneous.

(a) $$E_{C{d^{2 + }}/Cd}^o = - 0.40V,E_{N{i^{2 + }}/Ni}^o = - 0.24V$$

$$E_{cell}^o = E_{cathode}^o - E_{anode}^o = - 0.24 - ( - 0.40)$$

$$E_{cell}^o = 0.16$$

$$E_{cell}^{} = E_{cell}^o + 0.06 = 0.16 + 0.06 = 0.22V$$

Reaction is spontaneous.

(b) $$E_{C{d^{2 + }}Cd}^o = - 0.40V$$ $$E_{F{e^{2 + }}/Fe}^o = - 0.44V$$

$$E_{cell}^o = - 0.44 + 0.40 = - 0.04V$$

$$E_{cell}^{} = - 0.04 + 0.06 = 0.02V$$

Reaction is spontaneous.

(c) $$E_{N{i^{2 + }}/Ni}^o = - 0.24V$$

$$E_{P{b^{2 + }}/Pb}^o = - 0.13V$$

$$E_{cell}^o = - 0.13 + 0.24 = 0.11V$$

$$E_{cell}^{} = 0.11 + 0.06 = 0.17V$$

Reaction is spontaneous.

(d) $$E_{N{i^{2 + }}/Ni}^o = - 0.24V$$

$$E_{F{e^{2 + }}/Fe}^o = - 0.44V$$

$$E_{cell}^o = - 0.44 + 0.24 = - 0.20V$$

$$E_{cell}^o = - 0.20 + 0.06 = - 0.14V$$

Reaction is non-spontaneous.

Therefore, the correct combinations of X and Y are (a), (b) and (c).

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2021 Paper 2 Online
questions.question:
At 298 K, the limiting molar conductivity of a weak monobasic acid is 4 $$\times$$ 102 S cm2 mol$$-$$1. At 298 K, for an aqueous solution of the acid the degree of dissociation is $$\alpha$$ and the molar conductivity is y $$\times$$ 102 S cm2 mol$$-$$1. At 298 K, upon 20 times dilution with water, the molar conductivity of the solution becomes 3y $$\times$$ 102 S cm2 mol$$-$$1.

The value of $$\alpha$$ is __________.
questions.solution:
Degree of dissociation = $$\alpha$$

Limiting molar conductivity, $$\Lambda _m^o$$ = 4 $$\times$$ 102 S cm2 mol$$-$$1

Molar conductivity, $$\Lambda _m$$ = y $$\times$$ 102 S cm2 mol$$-$$1

Molar conductivity of dilution, $$\Lambda _m^o$$ = 3y $$\times$$ 102 S cm2 mol$$-$$1

Concentration before dilution = C

Concentration after dilution = $${C \over {20}}$$

Using relation, $$\alpha = {{\Lambda _m^{}} \over {\Lambda _m^o}}$$ .... (i)

Dissociation constant, $${K_a} = {{C{\alpha ^2}} \over {(1 - \alpha )}}$$

Putting Eq. (i),

$${K_a} = {{C\Lambda _m^2} \over {\Lambda _m^{o2}\left( {1 - {{\Lambda _m^{}} \over {\Lambda _m^o}}} \right)}} = {{C\Lambda _m^2} \over {\Lambda _m^o(\Lambda _m^o - \Lambda _m^{})}}$$

Dissociation constant before dillution,

$${K_a} = {{C{{(y \times {{10}^2})}^2}} \over {(4 \times {{10}^2})(4 \times {{10}^2} - y \times {{10}^2})}}$$ ..... (ii)

Dissociation constant after dilution,

$${K_a} = {{{C \over {20}}{{(3y \times {{10}^2})}^2}} \over {(4 \times {{10}^2})(4 \times {{10}^2} - 3y \times {{10}^2})}}$$ ..... (iii)

Comparing Eqs. (ii) and (iii),

$${{C{{(y \times {{10}^2})}^2}} \over {(4 \times {{10}^2})(4 \times {{10}^2} - y \times {{10}^2})}} = {{C{{(3y \times {{10}^2})}^2}} \over {20(4 \times {{10}^2})(4 \times {{10}^2} - 3y \times {{10}^2})}}$$

$${{{y^2} \times {{10}^4}} \over {{{10}^2}(4 - y)}} = {{9{y^2} \times {{10}^4}} \over {{{10}^2} \times 20(4 - 3y)}}$$

$${1 \over {(4 - y)}} = {9 \over {20(4 - 3y)}}$$

$$80 - 60y = 36 - 9y$$

$$80 - 36 = 60y - 9y$$

$$44 = 51y$$

$$ \Rightarrow y = {{44} \over {51}}$$ .... (iv)

Putting in Eq. (i),

$$\alpha = {{\Lambda _m^{}} \over {\Lambda _m^o}} = {{y \times {{10}^2}} \over {4 \times {{10}^2}}}$$

$$\alpha = {{44} \over {51 \times 4}} = {{11} \over {51}}$$

$$\alpha = 0.22$$

The value of $$\alpha$$ is 0.22.

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2021 Paper 2 Online
questions.question:
At 298 K, the limiting molar conductivity of a weak monobasic acid is 4 $$\times$$ 102 S cm2 mol$$-$$1. At 298 K, for an aqueous solution of the acid the degree of dissociation is $$\alpha$$ and the molar conductivity is y $$\times$$ 102 S cm2 mol$$-$$1. At 298 K, upon 20 times dilution with water, the molar conductivity of the solution becomes 3y $$\times$$ 102 S cm2 mol$$-$$1.

The value of y is __________.
questions.solution:
Degree of dissociation = $$\alpha$$

Limiting molar conductivity, $$\Lambda _m^o$$ = 4 $$\times$$ 102 S cm2 mol$$-$$1

Molar conductivity, $$\Lambda _m$$ = y $$\times$$ 102 S cm2 mol$$-$$1

Molar conductivity of dilution, $$\Lambda _m^o$$ = 3y $$\times$$ 102 S cm2 mol$$-$$1

Concentration before dilution = C

Concentration after dilution = $${C \over {20}}$$

Using relation, $$\alpha = {{\Lambda _m^{}} \over {\Lambda _m^o}}$$ .... (i)

Dissociation constant, $${K_a} = {{C{\alpha ^2}} \over {(1 - \alpha )}}$$

Putting Eq. (i),

$${K_a} = {{C\Lambda _m^2} \over {\Lambda _m^{o2}\left( {1 - {{\Lambda _m^{}} \over {\Lambda _m^o}}} \right)}} = {{C\Lambda _m^2} \over {\Lambda _m^o(\Lambda _m^o - \Lambda _m^{})}}$$

Dissociation constant before dillution,

$${K_a} = {{C{{(y \times {{10}^2})}^2}} \over {(4 \times {{10}^2})(4 \times {{10}^2} - y \times {{10}^2})}}$$ ..... (ii)

Dissociation constant after dilution,

$${K_a} = {{{C \over {20}}{{(3y \times {{10}^2})}^2}} \over {(4 \times {{10}^2})(4 \times {{10}^2} - 3y \times {{10}^2})}}$$ ..... (iii)

Comparing Eqs. (ii) and (iii),

$${{C{{(y \times {{10}^2})}^2}} \over {(4 \times {{10}^2})(4 \times {{10}^2} - y \times {{10}^2})}} = {{C{{(3y \times {{10}^2})}^2}} \over {20(4 \times {{10}^2})(4 \times {{10}^2} - 3y \times {{10}^2})}}$$

$${{{y^2} \times {{10}^4}} \over {{{10}^2}(4 - y)}} = {{9{y^2} \times {{10}^4}} \over {{{10}^2} \times 20(4 - 3y)}}$$

$${1 \over {(4 - y)}} = {9 \over {20(4 - 3y)}}$$

$$80 - 60y = 36 - 9y$$

$$80 - 36 = 60y - 9y$$

$$44 = 51y$$

$$ \Rightarrow y = {{44} \over {51}}$$ = 0.86

questions.title: BITSAT 2021
questions.question: Two coils A and B have a mutual inductance 0.001 H. The current changes in the first coil according to the equation $$i = {i_0}\sin \omega t$$, where i0 = 10A and $$\omega$$ = 10$$\pi$$ rads$$-$$1. The maximum value of emf in the second coil is
questions.solution:
Induced emf, $$\varepsilon = - M{{di} \over {dt}}$$

where, i is given as, $$i = {i_0}\sin \omega t$$

$$ \Rightarrow \varepsilon = - M{d \over {dt}}[{i_0}\sin \omega t]$$

$$ = M\,{i_0}\omega \cos \omega t$$

For maximum value of emf in second coil, cos $$\omega$$t is maximum, i.e., 1.

$${E_{\max }} = M\,{i_0}\omega $$ [$$\because$$ $$\cos \omega t = 1$$]

$$ \Rightarrow 0.001 \times 10 \times 10\pi $$

$$ \Rightarrow {E_{\max }} = 0.1$$ $$\pi$$V

Hence, maximum emf value induced in second coil is 0.1 $$\pi$$V.

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics (Term 1) Paper 2021-22
questions.question: The self-inductance of a solenoid of 600 turns is 108 mH. The self-inductance of a coil having 500 turns with the same length, the same radius and the same medium will be
questions.solution:
To solve this problem, we need to understand the relationship between the self-inductance of a solenoid and the number of turns. The self-inductance $ L $ of a solenoid is given by the formula:

$$ L = \mu_0 \mu_r \frac{N^2 A}{l} $$

where:

  • $ \mu_0 $ is the permeability of free space (a constant).
  • $ \mu_r $ is the relative permeability of the core material.
  • $ N $ is the number of turns.
  • $ A $ is the cross-sectional area of the solenoid.
  • $ l $ is the length of the solenoid.

In this problem, we are given the self-inductance $ L_1 $ of a solenoid with 600 turns as 108 mH. We need to find the self-inductance $ L_2 $ for a solenoid with 500 turns, but with the same length, radius, and medium. The formula can be written for both solenoids:

$$ L_1 = \mu_0 \mu_r \frac{N_1^2 A}{l} $$

$$ L_2 = \mu_0 \mu_r \frac{N_2^2 A}{l} $$

Given that the length, area, and the medium are the same, we can divide the two equations:

$$ \frac{L_1}{L_2} = \frac{N_1^2}{N_2^2} $$

Substitute the known values $ L_1 = 108 \, mH $, $ N_1 = 600 $, and $ N_2 = 500 $ into the equation:

$$ \frac{108}{L_2} = \frac{600^2}{500^2} $$

Simplify the right-hand side:

$$ \frac{108}{L_2} = \frac{360000}{250000} $$

$$ \frac{108}{L_2} = 1.44 $$

Rearrange to solve for $ L_2 $:

$$ L_2 = \frac{108}{1.44} $$

$$ L_2 = 75 \, mH $$

Therefore, the self-inductance of the coil with 500 turns is 75 mH. The correct option is:

Option D: 75 mH

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics (Term 1) Paper 2021-22
questions.question: The current in the primary coil of a pair of coils changes from 7 A to 3 A in 0.04 s. The mutual inductance between the two coils is 0.5 H. The induced emf in the secondary coil is -
questions.solution:
The induced emf in the secondary coil is given by the formula:

$$ε = -M \frac{dI}{dt}$$

where:

ε is the induced emf

M is the mutual inductance

dI is the change in current

dt is the time taken for the change in current

In this case, we have:

M = 0.5 H

dI = 7 A - 3 A = 4 A

dt = 0.04 s

Substituting these values into the formula, we get:

$$ε = -0.5 H \times \frac{4 A}{0.04 s} = -50 V$$

Therefore, the induced emf in the secondary coil is -50 V. The negative sign indicates that the induced emf is in the opposite direction to the change in current in the primary coil.

So the answer is Option A: 50 V.

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics (Term 1) Paper 2021-22
questions.question: A coil of area 100 cm<sup>2</sup> is kept at an angle of 30$$^\circ$$ with a magnetic field of 10<sup>$$-$$1</sup> T. The magnetic field is reduced to zero in 10<sup>$$-$$4</sup> s. The induced emf in the coil is -
questions.solution:
The induced emf in a coil can be determined using Faraday's Law of Electromagnetic Induction, which states that the induced emf is given by the rate of change of the magnetic flux through the coil.

The formula for Faraday's Law of Induction is:

$$ \text{emf} = - \frac{d\Phi_B}{dt} $$

where:

- $ \text{emf} $ is the induced emf in volts (V),

- $ \Phi_B $ is the magnetic flux, and

- $ t $ is time.

The magnetic flux $ \Phi_B $ is given by:

$$ \Phi_B = B \cdot A \cdot \cos(\theta) $$

where:

- $ B $ is the magnetic field strength (in teslas, T),

- $ A $ is the area of the coil (in square meters, m²), and

- $ \theta $ is the angle between the magnetic field and the normal to the surface of the coil.

Given data:

  • Area $ A = 100 \, \text{cm}^2 = 100 \times 10^{-4} \, \text{m}^2 = 10^{-2} \, \text{m}^2 $
  • Angle $ \theta = 30^\circ $
  • Initial magnetic field $ B_i = 10^{-1} \, \text{T} $
  • Final magnetic field $ B_f = 0 \, \text{T} $
  • Time $ t = 10^{-4} \, \text{s} $

Calculate the initial magnetic flux $ \Phi_{B_i} $:

$$ \Phi_{B_i} = B_i \cdot A \cdot \cos(30^\circ) $$

$$ \Phi_{B_i} = 10^{-1} \, \text{T} \cdot 10^{-2} \, \text{m}^2 \cdot \cos(30^\circ) $$

$$ \Phi_{B_i} = 10^{-1} \cdot 10^{-2} \cdot \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} $$

$$ \Phi_{B_i} = \frac{10^{-3} \sqrt{3}}{2} \, \text{T} \cdot \text{m}^2 $$

Since the final magnetic field $ B_f $ is zero, the final magnetic flux $ \Phi_{B_f} $ is also zero:

$$ \Phi_{B_f} = 0 $$

Now, calculate the change in magnetic flux $ \Delta \Phi_B $:

$$ \Delta \Phi_B = \Phi_{B_f} - \Phi_{B_i} $$

$$ \Delta \Phi_B = 0 - \frac{10^{-3} \sqrt{3}}{2} $$

$$ \Delta \Phi_B = - \frac{10^{-3} \sqrt{3}}{2} $$

Finally, calculate the induced emf:

$$ \text{emf} = - \frac{d\Phi_B}{dt} $$

$$ \text{emf} = - \frac{- \frac{10^{-3} \sqrt{3}}{2}}{10^{-4} \, \text{s}} $$

$$ \text{emf} = \frac{10^{-3} \sqrt{3}}{2 \times 10^{-4}} $$

$$ \text{emf} = \frac{10^{-3} \sqrt{3}}{2 \times 10^{-4}} $$

$$ \text{emf} = 5 \sqrt{3} \, \text{V} $$

Therefore, the induced emf in the coil is:

Option A: $ 5\sqrt{3} \, \text{V} $

questions.title: BITSAT 2021
questions.question: Choose the correct statement.
options: The speed of light in the meta-material is $$v = c|n|$$.
The speed of light in the meta-material is $$v = {c \over {|n|}}$$.
The speed of light in the meta-material is v = c.
The wavelength of the light in the meta-material ($$\lambda$$<sub>m</sub>) is given by $$\lambda$$<sub>m</sub> = $$\lambda$$<sub>air</sub> | n |.
questions.solution:
When light travel in meta-material, physical characteristics remain unchanged.

$$\therefore$$ $$v = {c \over {|n|}}$$

where, | n | is the relative refractive index of meta-material.

As, $$n = {c \over v} \Rightarrow |n| = {{{n_{med}}} \over {{n_{air}}}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow \left| n \right| = {{{\lambda _{air}}} \over {{\lambda _{med}}}} \Rightarrow {\lambda _{med}} = {{{\lambda _{air}}} \over {\left| n \right|}}$$

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics (Term 1) Paper 2021-22
questions.question: Which of the following has negative temperature coefficient of resistivity?
questions.solution:
The temperature coefficient of resistivity is a parameter that indicates how the resistivity of a material changes with temperature. Let's analyze the given options:

Option A: Metal

Metals typically have a positive temperature coefficient of resistivity. This means that as the temperature increases, the resistivity of metals also increases. This happens because the increased thermal energy causes more collisions between the conduction electrons and the lattice ions. Therefore, metals do not have a negative temperature coefficient of resistivity.

Option B: Metal and Semiconductor

As we established, metals have a positive temperature coefficient of resistivity. Semiconductors, on the other hand, usually have a negative temperature coefficient of resistivity. As temperature increases, more electrons gain enough energy to jump into the conduction band, thereby decreasing the resistivity. Hence, combining both metals and semiconductors together in this option is not accurate for describing a negative temperature coefficient of resistivity.

Option C: Semiconductor

Semiconductors indeed have a negative temperature coefficient of resistivity. When temperature increases, more charge carriers (electrons and holes) are excited into the conduction band, leading to a decrease in resistivity. Therefore, semiconductors are characterized by a negative temperature coefficient of resistivity:

$$ \frac{d\rho}{dT} < 0 $$

where $$\rho$$ is the resistivity, and $$T$$ is the temperature.

Option D: Metal and Alloy

Alloys, like metals, typically have a positive temperature coefficient of resistivity. Similar to pure metals, the resistivity of alloys increases with rising temperature due to enhanced scattering mechanisms that impede the flow of electrons. Therefore, this option is incorrect as well.

Considering all the points above, the correct answer is:

Option C: Semiconductor

questions.title: BITSAT 2021
questions.question: Four charges equal to + Q are placed at the four corners of a square and a charge ($$-$$q) is at its centre. If the system is in equilibrium, then the value of $$-$$q is
questions.solution:
Consider the equilibrium of charge Q at corner A

$${F_B} = {F_D} = {1 \over {4\pi {\varepsilon _0}}}{{{Q^2}} \over {{a^2}}} = {{k{Q^2}} \over {{a^2}}}$$ [$$\because$$ $${1 \over {4\pi {\varepsilon _0}}} = k$$]

$${F_C} = {{k{Q^2}} \over {{{(\sqrt 2 a)}^2}}}$$ [$$\because$$ $$AC = \sqrt 2 a$$]

$$ = {{k{Q^2}} \over {2{a^2}}}$$

$${F_O} = {{k\,Qq} \over {{{\left( {{a \over {\sqrt 2 }}} \right)}^2}}} = {{k\,2Qq} \over {{a^2}}}$$ [$$\because$$ $$OC = {a \over {\sqrt 2 }}$$]

For equilibrium of charge Q at corner A, the net force on this charge along AC must be zero.

$$\therefore$$ $${F_O} = {F_C} + {F_B}\cos 45^\circ + {F_D}\cos 45^\circ $$

$$ = {F_C} + 2{F_B}\cos 45^\circ $$ [$$\because$$ $${F_B} = {F_D}$$]

$$ = {F_C} + 2{F_B} \times {1 \over {\sqrt 2 }}$$ [$$\because$$ $$\cos 45^\circ = {1 \over {\sqrt 2 }}$$]

$$ \Rightarrow {{k\,2Qq} \over {{a^2}}} = {{k\,{Q^2}} \over {2{a^2}}} + {{2 \times k{Q^2}} \over {{a^2}}} \times {1 \over {\sqrt 2 }}$$

$$ \Rightarrow 2Qq = {{{Q^2}} \over 2} + {{{Q^2}} \over {\sqrt 2 }} \times 2$$

$$ \Rightarrow 2q = {Q \over 2} + Q\sqrt 2 $$

$$ \Rightarrow q = {Q \over 4}(1 + 2\sqrt 2 )$$

questions.title: BITSAT 2021
questions.question: An electric dipole is placed at an angle of 30$$^\circ$$ in a non-uniform electric field. The dipole will experience
options: translational force only in the direction of the field
translational force only in a direction normal to the direction of the field
torque as well as a translational force
questions.solution:
When a dipole is placed in a non-uniform electric field, opposite charges of the dipole experiences a force due to E. It is as shown in the figure below

These forces will act in opposite direction. Now, since the electric field is not uniform, so the force experienced by the charges will be unequal.

Hence, their will be a net force acting on the dipole, as they do not cancel each other.

Also, forces on the charges are not linear as shown above. So, they will experience a not non-zero torque. Hence, the dipole will experience both translational force and torque in a non-uniform electric field.

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics (Term 1) Paper 2021-22
questions.question: A negatively charged object X is repelled by another charged object Y. However an object Z is attracted to object Y. Which of the following is the most possibility for the object Z?
questions.solution:
In this scenario, we have three objects: X, Y, and Z. Let's analyze the interactions between these objects to determine the most likely nature of object Z.

1. Object X is negatively charged and is repelled by object Y. This means that object Y must also carry a negative charge, because like charges repel each other.

2. Object Z is attracted to object Y. Since object Y is negatively charged (as established above), object Z can either be positively charged (as opposite charges attract) or neutral (since a neutral object can be attracted to a charged object due to polarization effects).

Given these relationships, the most likely possibilities for object Z are that it is either neutrally charged or positively charged. Therefore, the correct option is:

Option C: neutral or positively charged

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics (Term 1) Paper 2021-22
questions.question: In an experiment three microscopic latex spheres are sprayed into a chamber and became charged with charges +3e, +5e and $$-$$3e respectively. All the three spheres came in contact simultaneously for a moment and got separated. Which one of the following are possible values for the final charge on the spheres?
questions.solution:
When three spheres are brought in contact the net charge will be $-3 e+3 e+5 e=5 e$ When separated this $5 e$ charge will be distributed.

In option (A), Total charge is 6 e . So, this option is not correct.

In option (B), total charge is $5 e$. So, this option is correct.

In option (C), Total charge is 5 e . But this violates quantization of charge principle. Hence this option is not correct.

In option (D), total charge is -10 e , Hence this option is not correct.

So, (B) is the correct option.

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics (Term 1) Paper 2021-22
questions.question: An object has charge of 1 C and gains 5.0 $$\times$$ 10<sup>18</sup> electrons. The net charge on the object becomes -
questions.solution:
To determine the net charge on the object, we need to calculate the total charge change due to the gained electrons and then add this change to the initial charge of the object.

First, recall that the charge of one electron is approximately $$-1.6 \times 10^{-19}$$ coulombs. If the object gains $$5.0 \times 10^{18}$$ electrons, the total charge contributed by these electrons is:

$$ \Delta Q = 5.0 \times 10^{18} \times (-1.6 \times 10^{-19}) \text{ C} $$

Calculating the total charge change:

$$ \Delta Q = -8.0 \times 10^{-1} \text{ C} = -0.80 \text{ C} $$

Since the initial charge of the object was +1 C, the net charge becomes:

$$ Q_{\text{net}} = 1 \text{ C} + (-0.80 \text{ C}) = 0.20 \text{ C} $$

Therefore, the net charge on the object becomes +0.20 C.

Hence, the correct answer is:

Option D: + 0.20 C

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics (Term 1) Paper 2021-22
questions.question: The electric potential V at any point (x, y, z) is given by V = 3x<sup>2</sup> where x is in metres and V in volts. The electric field at the point (1 m, 0, 2m) is -
questions.solution:
To find the electric field from the electric potential, we use the relationship between the electric field $\vec{E}$ and the electric potential $V$. The electric field is the negative gradient of the electric potential:

$$\vec{E} = -\nabla V$$

Given the electric potential $V = 3x^2$, we need to compute the partial derivatives with respect to $x$, $y$, and $z$ to find the components of the electric field.

Since $V = 3x^2$, $V$ does not depend on $y$ or $z$. Therefore, the partial derivatives with respect to $y$ and $z$ are zero:

$$\frac{\partial V}{\partial y} = 0$$

$$\frac{\partial V}{\partial z} = 0$$

Now, we find the partial derivative with respect to $x$:

$$\frac{\partial V}{\partial x} = \frac{\partial (3x^2)}{\partial x} = 6x$$

Thus, the electric field components are:

$$E_x = -\frac{\partial V}{\partial x} = -6x$$

$$E_y = -\frac{\partial V}{\partial y} = 0$$

$$E_z = -\frac{\partial V}{\partial z} = 0$$

At the point (1 m, 0, 2 m), we substitute $x = 1$, $y = 0$, and $z = 2$ into the expressions for the electric field components:

$$E_x = -6 \cdot 1 = -6 \, \text{V/m}$$

$$E_y = 0 \, \text{V/m}$$

$$E_z = 0 \, \text{V/m}$$

The electric field at the point (1 m, 0, 2 m) is $-6 \, \text{V/m}$ along the x-axis. Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option A: 6 V/m along -x-axis

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics (Term 1) Paper 2021-22
questions.question: Which of the diagrams correctly represents the electric field between two charged plates if a neutral conductor is placed in between the plates?
questions.solution: Upper side of the neutral conductor will be negatively charged. Lower side of the neutral conductor will be positively charged. Then the field lines will be from negative to positive as shown in the diagram.

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics (Term 1) Paper 2021-22
questions.question: The magnitude of electric field due to a point charge 2q, at distance r is E. Then the magnitude of electric field due to a uniformly charged thin spherical shell of radius R with total charge q at a distance $${r \over 2}(r >  > R)$$ will be
questions.solution:
The electric field due to a point charge is given by Coulomb's law:

$$E = \frac{kQ}{r^2}$$

where:

  • E is the electric field strength
  • k is Coulomb's constant ($8.99 \times 10^9 N m^2/C^2$)
  • Q is the magnitude of the charge
  • r is the distance from the charge

We are given that the electric field due to a point charge 2q at a distance r is E. Therefore:

$$E = \frac{k(2q)}{r^2}$$

Now, we need to find the electric field due to a uniformly charged thin spherical shell of radius R with total charge q at a distance $${r \over 2}(r > > R)$$.

Since r >> R, we can treat the spherical shell as a point charge located at its center. This is because the distance from the observation point to the shell is much larger than the radius of the shell. Therefore, the electric field due to the spherical shell will be the same as the electric field due to a point charge q located at a distance $${r \over 2}$$.

Using Coulomb's law again:

$$E' = \frac{kq}{(r/2)^2} = \frac{4kq}{r^2}$$

Comparing this with the electric field due to the point charge 2q, we see that:

$$E' = 2E$$

Therefore, the magnitude of the electric field due to the uniformly charged thin spherical shell is 2E. So the correct answer is Option C.

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics (Term 1) Paper 2021-22
questions.question: A square sheet of side 'a' is lying parallel to XY plate at z = a. The electric field in the region is $$\overrightarrow E  = c{z^2}\widehat k$$. The electric flux through the sheet is
questions.solution:
To determine the electric flux through the square sheet, we need to apply Gauss's law. The electric flux through a surface is given by:

$$\Phi = \int \overrightarrow{E} \cdot d\overrightarrow{A}$$

Where $ \overrightarrow{E} $ is the electric field and $ d\overrightarrow{A} $ is the differential area vector. Since the sheet is lying parallel to the XY plane at $ z = a $, the area vector $ d\overrightarrow{A} $ will be in the direction of $ \widehat{k} $.

The electric field in the region is given by:

$$\overrightarrow{E} = c z^2 \widehat{k}$$

At $ z = a $, the electric field $ \overrightarrow{E} $ becomes:

$$\overrightarrow{E} = c a^2 \widehat{k}$$

Since the area vector $ d\overrightarrow{A} $ for the entire sheet is $ a^2 \widehat{k} $, we can compute the flux as follows:

$$\Phi = \overrightarrow{E} \cdot \overrightarrow{A} = (c a^2 \widehat{k}) \cdot (a^2 \widehat{k}) = c a^2 \cdot a^2 = c a^4$$

Thus, the electric flux through the sheet is:

$$\Phi = c a^4$$

Therefore, the correct option is:

Option A $ a^4 c $

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics (Term 1) Paper 2021-22
questions.question: Three charges q, $$-$$q and q0 are placed as shown in figure. The magnitude of the net force on the charge q0 at point O is $$\left[ {k = {1 \over {(4\pi { \in _0})}}} \right]$$
questions.solution:
Positions of $q_0,-q$ and $q$ are shown.

Both $-q$ and $q$ charges are equidistant from $q_0$.

So, the magnitude of both the forces on $q_0$ will be equal. The angle between the forces will be $90^{\circ}$ as shown in the diagram.

Hence the resultant force

$$ \begin{aligned} & =\sqrt{\mathrm{F}^2+\mathrm{F}^2+2 \mathrm{~F} \times \mathrm{F} \times \cos 90^{\circ}} \\\\ & =\sqrt{2} \mathrm{~F}=\sqrt{2} \times \frac{k q q_0}{a^2}=\sqrt{2} \times\left(\frac{1}{4 \pi \varepsilon_0}\right) \times\left(\frac{q q_0}{a^2}\right) \end{aligned} $$

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics (Term 1) Paper 2021-22
questions.question: A + 3.0 nC charge Q is initially at rest at a distance of r<sub>1</sub> = 10 cm from a + 5.0 nC charge q fixed at the origin. The charge Q is moved away from q to a new position at r<sub>2</sub> = 15 cm. In this process work done by the field is
questions.solution:
The work done by the electric field in moving a charge from one point to another is equal to the negative of the change in potential energy of the charge.

The potential energy of a charge Q at a distance r from a charge q is given by:

$$U = \frac{kqQ}{r}$$

where k is Coulomb's constant (8.99 × 109 N·m2/C2).

Therefore, the change in potential energy of the charge Q as it is moved from r1 to r2 is:

$$ \Delta U = U_2 - U_1$$

$$= \frac{kqQ}{r_2} - \frac{kqQ}{r_1}$$

$$= kqQ \left( \frac{1}{r_2} - \frac{1}{r_1} \right)$$

The work done by the electric field is then:

$$W = -\Delta U$$

$$= -kqQ \left( \frac{1}{r_2} - \frac{1}{r_1} \right)$$

Substituting the given values, we get:

$$W = - (8.99 \times 10^9 \text{ N} \cdot \text{m}^2/\text{C}^2)(5.0 \times 10^{-9} \text{ C})(3.0 \times 10^{-9} \text{ C}) \left( \frac{1}{0.15 \text{ m}} - \frac{1}{0.10 \text{ m}} \right)$$

$$W = 4.5 \times 10^{-7} \text{ J}$$

Therefore, the correct answer is Option D: 4.5 × 10−7 J.

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics (Term 1) Paper 2021-22
questions.question: Four objects W, X, Y and Z, each with charge +q are held fixed at four points of a square of side d as shown in the figure. Objects X and Z are on the midpoints of the sides of the square. The electrostatic force exerted by object W on object X is F. Then the magnitude of the force exerted by object W on Z is
questions.solution:
Force on X by W is $F=\frac{k q^2}{(d / 2)^2}=\frac{4 k q^2}{d^2}$

$\begin{aligned} W Z & =\sqrt{d^2+(d / 2)^2} \\\\ & =\sqrt{\frac{5 d^2}{4}}\end{aligned}$

$$ \begin{aligned} \text { Force on }\mathrm{Z} \text { by } \mathrm{W} \text { is } F^{\prime} & =\frac{k q^2}{\frac{5 d^2}{4}} \\\\ & =\frac{4 k q^2}{5 d^2} \\\\ & =\frac{F}{5} \end{aligned} $$

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics (Term 1) Paper 2021-22
questions.question: Two charges 14 $$\mu$$C and $$-$$4 $$\mu$$C are placed at ($$-$$12 cm, 0, 0) and (12 cm, 0, 0) in an external electric field $$E = \left( {{B \over {{r^2}}}} \right)$$, where B = 1.2 $$\times$$ 10<sup>6</sup> N/(cm<sup>2</sup>) and r is in metres. The electrostatic potential energy of the configuration is
questions.solution:
The electrostatic potential energy of a system of charges is given by the equation:

$$U = \frac{1}{4 \pi \epsilon_0} \sum\limits_{i \neq j} \frac{q_i q_j}{r_{ij}}$$

In this problem, we have two charges, 14 $$\mu$$C and $$-$$4 $$\mu$$C, placed at coordinates ($$-$$12 cm, 0, 0) and (12 cm, 0, 0) respectively. We need to convert the distances into meters for consistency in SI units.

$$r_{ij} = 12 \, \text{cm} + 12 \, \text{cm} = 24 \, \text{cm} = 0.24 \, \text{m}$$

Now we can calculate the electrostatic potential energy. The charges need to be in Coulombs:

$$q_1 = 14 \times 10^{-6} \, \text{C}$$


$$q_2 = -4 \times 10^{-6} \, \text{C}$$

Using the formula for the potential energy of a two-charge system and the Coulomb constant $$k = \frac{1}{4 \pi \epsilon_0} = 8.99 \times 10^9 \, \text{N} \cdot \text{m}^2/\text{C}^2$$, we have:

$$U = k \frac{q_1 q_2}{r}$$

Substitute the values:

$$U = 8.99 \times 10^9 \times \frac{(14 \times 10^{-6}) \times (-4 \times 10^{-6})}{0.24}$$

Calculate the product of the charges:

$$q_1 q_2 = 14 \times (-4) \times 10^{-12} = -56 \times 10^{-12}$$

Now substitute this into the equation:

$$U = 8.99 \times 10^9 \times \frac{-56 \times 10^{-12}}{0.24}$$

Simplify the expression:

$$U = 8.99 \times 10^9 \times -233.33 \times 10^{-12}$$

Finally, evaluate the expression:

$$U = -2098.67 \times 10^{-3} \, \text{J}$$

Convert to a simpler form:

$$U = -97.9 \, \text{J}$$

Therefore, the electrostatic potential energy of the configuration is:

$$\boxed{-97.9 \, \text{J}}$$

Hence, the correct option is:

Option D.

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics (Term 1) Paper 2021-22
questions.question:
Assertion (A) : A negative charge in an electric field moves along the direction of the electric field.

Reason (R) : On a negative charge a force acts in the direction of the electric field.
options: Both (A) & (R) are true and (R) is correct explanation of (A).
Both (A) & (R) are true and (R) is not correct explanation of (A).
(A) is false and (R) is also false.
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

Option D

Assertion (A) : A negative charge in an electric field moves along the direction of the electric field. This statement is false. A negative charge in an electric field actually moves in the opposite direction of the electric field. The electric field direction is defined as the direction in which a positive test charge would move. Thus, a negative charge would experience a force in the direction opposite to the electric field.

Reason (R) : On a negative charge a force acts in the direction of the electric field. This statement is also false. The force on a negative charge in an electric field is opposite to the direction of the electric field. The force exerted on the charge by the electric field is given by Coulomb's law:

$$\vec{F} = q \vec{E}$$

where $ q $ is the charge and $ \vec{E} $ is the electric field. For a negative charge $ q $, the force $ \vec{F} $ will be in the opposite direction of $ \vec{E} $ because the charge $ q $ is negative.

Therefore, both (A) and (R) are false, making Option D the correct choice.

questions.title: CBSE 12th Physics (Term 1) Paper 2021-22
questions.question: Equipotentials at a large distance from a collection of charges whose total sum is not zero are -
questions.solution:
To understand the nature of equipotentials at a large distance from a collection of charges whose total charge is not zero, we need to delve into the concept of electric potential due to multiple charges.

At a large distance from the charge distribution, the system of charges can be approximated as a point charge whose value is equal to the total charge of the system. This is because, at large distances, higher-order multipole moments (such as dipole or quadrupole moments) contribute much less to the potential compared to the monopole moment (total charge).

For a point charge, the equipotential surfaces are spherical in nature, as they depend only on the distance from the charge. The electric potential $V$ at a distance $r$ from a point charge $Q$ is given by:

$$ V = \frac{1}{4\pi \epsilon_0} \cdot \frac{Q}{r} $$

This implies that the potential is the same at any point lying on a sphere of radius $r$ centered at the location of the charge. Therefore, as we move farther away from a collection of charges with a non-zero total charge, the equipotential surfaces approach those of a point charge, which are spherical.

Hence, the correct option is:

Option A: spheres

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2021 Paper 1 Online
questions.question:
Two point charges $$-$$Q and +Q/$$\sqrt 3 $$ are placed in the xy-plane at the origin (0, 0) and a point (2, 0), respectively, as shown in the figure. This results in an equipotential circle of radius R and potential V = 0 in the xy-plane with its center at (b, 0). All lengths are measured in meters.



The value of R is __________ meter.
questions.solution:
V at B is zero if

$${{kQ} \over {(2 + R + x)}} = {{{{kQ} \over {\sqrt 3 }}} \over {x + R}}$$ ($$\because$$ $$k = {1 \over {4\pi {\varepsilon _0}}}$$)

$$\sqrt 3 (x + R) = 2 + R + x$$

$$(\sqrt 3 - 1)x + (\sqrt 3 - 1)R = 2$$ .....(i)

V at A is zero if

$${{kQ} \over {2 - x'}} = {{{{kQ} \over {\sqrt 3 }}} \over {x'}}$$

$$\sqrt 3 x' = 2 - x'$$

$$x' = {2 \over {\sqrt 3 + 1}}$$

$$x' + x = R$$

$${2 \over {\sqrt 3 + 1}} + x = R$$

$$2 + (\sqrt 3 + 1)x = (\sqrt 3 + 1)R$$

$$x = {{(\sqrt 3 + 1)R - 2} \over {\sqrt 3 + 1}}$$

$$(\sqrt 3 + 1)R - (\sqrt 3 + 1)x = 2$$ .....(ii)

Using Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get

$$R = \sqrt 3 m = 1.73m$$

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2021 Paper 1 Online
questions.question:
Two point charges $$-$$Q and +Q/$$\sqrt 3 $$ are placed in the xy-plane at the origin (0, 0) and a point (2, 0), respectively, as shown in the figure. This results in an equipotential circle of radius R and potential V = 0 in the xy-plane with its center at (b, 0). All lengths are measured in meters.



The value of b is __________ meter.
questions.solution:
V at B is zero if

$${{kQ} \over {(2 + R + x)}} = {{{{kQ} \over {\sqrt 3 }}} \over {x + R}}$$ ($$\because$$ $$k = {1 \over {4\pi {\varepsilon _0}}}$$)

$$\sqrt 3 (x + R) = 2 + R + x$$

$$(\sqrt 3 - 1)x + (\sqrt 3 - 1)R = 2$$ .....(i)

V at A is zero if

$${{kQ} \over {2 - x'}} = {{{{kQ} \over {\sqrt 3 }}} \over {x'}}$$

$$\sqrt 3 x' = 2 - x'$$

$$x' = {2 \over {\sqrt 3 + 1}}$$

$$x' + x = R$$

$${2 \over {\sqrt 3 + 1}} + x = R$$

$$2 + (\sqrt 3 + 1)x = (\sqrt 3 + 1)R$$

$$x = {{(\sqrt 3 + 1)R - 2} \over {\sqrt 3 + 1}}$$

$$(\sqrt 3 + 1)R - (\sqrt 3 + 1)x = 2$$ .....(ii)

Using Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get

$$R = \sqrt 3 m = 1.73m$$

$$x = {{(\sqrt 3 + 1)\sqrt 3 - 2} \over {\sqrt 3 + 1}} = {{\sqrt 3 + 1} \over {\sqrt 3 + 1}} = 1$$ m

Hence, the centre of circle is having x-coordinate

= b = 2 + x = 3.00 m.

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2021 Paper 2 Online
questions.question: Let E be the ellipse $${{{x^2}} \over {16}} + {{{y^2}} \over 9} = 1$$. For any three distinct points P, Q and Q' on E, let M(P, Q) be the mid-point of the line segment joining P and Q, and M(P, Q') be the mid-point of the line segment joining P and Q'. Then the maximum possible value of the distance between M(P, Q) and M(P, Q'), as P, Q and Q' vary on E, is _______.
questions.solution:
As we know that, in a triangle, sides joining the mid-points of two sides is half and parallel to the third side.

$$\therefore$$ $${M_1}{M_2} = {1 \over 2}QQ'$$


Maximum value of QQ' is AA'

Hence, maximum value of $${M_1}{M_2} = {1 \over 2}AA' = 4$$

questions.title: BITSAT 2021
questions.question: Freedom and quality are the ................ rights of every human.
questions.solution:
The word "inalienable" means unable to be taken away or denied. When we say that freedom and quality are inalienable rights of every human, we mean that these rights are inherent to all people and should not be denied or taken away. This is a common phrase often used in discussions about human rights.

In contrast, "inscrutable" means hard to interpret or understand, "incalculable" means too great to be calculated or estimated, and "institutional" refers to something that is established as a norm or convention in an organization or culture. None of these words make sense in the context of your sentence about human rights.

questions.title: BITSAT 2021
questions.question: Pradeep's face spoke .................. of the happiness he was feeling.
questions.solution:
Option C : volumes

Explanation :

The expression "speak volumes" is an idiom in English, meaning to provide a lot of information, especially in an indirect or unspoken way. In the context of this sentence, "Pradeep's face spoke volumes of the happiness he was feeling" means that Pradeep's facial expressions gave away a great deal about his inner happiness, even if he didn't verbally express it.

Looking at the other options :

Option A : Elegantly - While one could argue that a face could "speak elegantly," this phrase is not a common idiom or phrase in English, and it doesn't quite capture the intended meaning of conveying a lot of information.

Option B : Tons - The word "tons" is used informally to mean a large amount, but it's typically used with countable or quantifiable things. It's not typically used in the context of someone's facial expressions communicating an emotion.

Option D: Much - While "much" does mean a large amount, it doesn't quite fit into this sentence naturally. It's not a commonly used phrase to say a face "spoke much" of an emotion.

Therefore, Option C, "volumes," is the best fit for this sentence.

questions.title: BITSAT 2021
questions.question: His speech was disappointing : it ................ all the major issues.
questions.solution:
Option A : "Projected"

"Projected" typically refers to the act of planning, estimating, or forecasting something for the future. It's often used in terms of estimating future performance or outcomes based on current data or trends. However, in the context of the sentence, "projected" doesn't fit because we're discussing a speech's handling of issues, not forecasting them.

Option B : "Revealed"

"Revealed" means to make known, disclose, or uncover something that was previously hidden or unknown. If the sentence read, "His speech was enlightening: it revealed all the major issues," then "revealed" would make sense. However, the sentence is about the speech being disappointing, and revealing issues would typically be seen as a positive thing.

Option C : "Skirted"

"Skirted" means to evade or avoid dealing with something. In the context of a speech, if someone "skirts" an issue, it means they are intentionally avoiding addressing it. Given that the sentence states the speech was disappointing, "skirted" is a fitting choice because a speech that avoids major issues can indeed be disappointing.

Option D : "Analysed"

"Analysed" means to examine in detail the structure of something, typically for purposes of interpretation or explanation. If a speech analysed all the major issues, it would typically be seen as thorough or comprehensive, not disappointing. Therefore, this word does not fit well in the given sentence.

So, considering the context of the sentence and the meaning of the word "disappointing," the word "skirted" fits best.

questions.title: BITSAT 2021
questions.question: Two drops of equal radius (R) coalesce to form a bigger drop. What is the ratio of surface energy of bigger drop to smaller one?
questions.solution:
Volume of bigger drop = Volume of two smaller drops

$$ \Rightarrow {4 \over 3}\pi R{'^3} = 2 \times {4 \over 3}\pi {R^3}$$

where, R' is the radius of bigger drop.

$$ \Rightarrow R{'^3} = 2{R^3}$$

$$ \Rightarrow R' = {2^{1/3}}\,R$$

Initial surface energy of two small drops, $${U_1} = 8\pi {R^2}\sigma $$ ..... (i)

Final surface energy of big drop, $${U_2} = 4\pi R{'^2}\sigma $$

$$ = 4\pi \times {2^{2/3}}{R^2}\sigma $$ ..... (ii)

On dividing Eq. (ii) by Eq. (i), we get

$${{{U_2}} \over {{U_1}}} = {{4\pi \times {2^{2/3}}\,{R^2}\sigma } \over {8\pi {R^2}\sigma }} = {{{2^{2/3}}} \over 2} = {{{2^{ - 1/3}}} \over 1}$$

Ratio of surface energy of bigger drop to smaller one is 2$$-$$1/3 : 1.

questions.title: BITSAT 2021
questions.question: The maximum value of the function y = x(x $$-$$ 1)2, is
questions.solution:
We have, $$y = x{(x - 1)^2}$$

$$\therefore$$ $${{dy} \over {dx}} = {(x - 1)^2}\,.\,1 + 2x(x - 1) = 3{x^2} - 4x + 1 = (x - 1)(3x - 1)$$

For maximum or minimum, $${{dy} \over {dx}} = 0$$

$$ \Rightarrow (x - 1)(3x - 1) = 0 \Rightarrow x = {1 \over 3},1$$

Now, $${{{d^2}y} \over {d{x^2}}} = 6x - 4 \Rightarrow {\left[ {{{{d^2}y} \over {d{x^2}}}} \right]_{x = {1 \over 3}}} = - 2 < 0$$

$$\therefore$$ y is maximum when $$x = {1 \over 3}$$ and maximum value is

$${[y]_{x = {1 \over 3}}} = {4 \over {27}}$$

Also, $${\left[ {{{{d^2}y} \over {d{x^2}}}} \right]_{x = 1}} = 2 > 0$$

$$\therefore$$ y is minimum when x = 1.

questions.title: BITSAT 2021
questions.question: Find the area enclosed by the loop in the curve 4y2 = 4x2 $$-$$ x3.
questions.solution:
Substitute 0 for y in the equation 4y2 = 4x2 $$-$$ x3,

0 = x2(4 $$-$$ x) $$\Rightarrow$$ x = 0, x = 4

It means curve makes the loop symmetric about X-axis between 0 and 4.

Area $$ = 2\int_0^4 {ydx = {2 \over 2}\int_0^4 {\sqrt {4{x^2} - {x^3}} dx = \int_0^4 {x\sqrt {4 - x} \,dx} } } $$

Let $$4 - x = t \Rightarrow - dx = dt$$

Area $$ = - \int_4^0 {(4 - t)\sqrt t \,dt} $$

$$ = \int_0^4 {(4\sqrt t - t\sqrt t )dt = \left[ {4 \times {2 \over 3}{t^{3/2}} - {2 \over 5}{t^{5/2}}} \right]_0^4} $$

$$ = {8 \over 3} \times 8 - {2 \over 5} \times 32 = {{64} \over 3} - {{64} \over 5} = 64 \times {2 \over {15}} = {{128} \over {15}}$$

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14 November 2021
questions.question: What is the period of the function f(x) = ln(2 + sin<sup>2</sup>x)?
questions.solution:
To determine the period of the function $$f(x) = \ln(2 + \sin^2 x),$$ follow these steps:

$$\sin^2 x$$ is the key part affecting the periodicity. Although $$\sin x$$ has a period of $$2\pi$$, its square, $$\sin^2 x$$, actually has a period of $$\pi$$ because:

$$\sin^2(x + \pi) = \sin^2 x.$$

Since the logarithm function, $$\ln(\cdot)$$, is simply a transformation, the overall period of $$f(x)$$ is determined by the period of $$\sin^2 x$$.

After verifying that there is no smaller period that satisfies the function's repetition, we conclude that:

$$f(x + \pi) = \ln(2 + \sin^2(x + \pi)) = \ln(2 + \sin^2 x) = f(x).$$

Thus, the period of $$f(x)$$ is $$\pi$$, which corresponds to Option B.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14 November 2021
questions.question: What is the range of the function f(x) = 1 - sinx defined on entire real line?
questions.solution:
Given the function $ f(x) = 1 - \sin x $.

The domain of $ f(x) $ is $ (-\infty, \infty) $.

Within this domain, the range of $\sin x$ is $[-1, 1]$.

Therefore, we calculate the range of $ f(x) $ as follows:

$ f(x) = 1 - \sin x $

If $\sin x$ ranges from $[-1, 1]$, then:

$ f(x) = 1 - [-1, 1] = [0, 2] $

Thus, the range of $ f(x) $ is $[0, 2]$.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 18 April 2021
questions.question:
Consider the following statements:

1. A function f : Z → Z, defined by f(x) = x + 1, is one-one as well as onto.

2. A function f : N → N, defined by f(x) = x + 1, is one-one but not onto.

Which of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.solution:
To determine the correctness of the statements provided, let's analyze them one by one:

Statement 1: Function $ f : \mathbb{Z} \to \mathbb{Z} $ defined by $ f(x) = x + 1 $.

One-to-One (Injective): A function is one-to-one if $ f(a) = f(b) $ implies $ a = b $ for any $ a, b \in \mathbb{Z} $. For this function, $ f(a) = a + 1 $ and $ f(b) = b + 1 $. If $ a + 1 = b + 1 $, then $ a = b $. Therefore, the function is one-to-one.

Onto (Surjective): A function is onto if for every $ y \in \mathbb{Z} $, there exists an $ x \in \mathbb{Z} $ such that $ f(x) = y $. For any integer $ y $, we can find $ x = y - 1 $ since $ f(y - 1) = (y - 1) + 1 = y $. Therefore, the function is onto.

Thus, the function $ f(x) = x + 1 $ is both one-to-one and onto from $\mathbb{Z}$ to $\mathbb{Z}$.

Statement 2: Function $ f : \mathbb{N} \to \mathbb{N} $ defined by $ f(x) = x + 1 $.

One-to-One (Injective): Similarly, this function is one-to-one because if $ f(a) = f(b) $ implies $ a + 1 = b + 1 $, which implies $ a = b $.

Onto (Surjective): This function is not onto because not every natural number can be expressed as $ x + 1 $ for some natural number $ x $. Specifically, 1 cannot be expressed as $ x + 1 $ for any $ x \in \mathbb{N} $ since $ x + 1 $ starts from 2 (the range of $ f $ is $\{2, 3, 4, \ldots\}$).

Therefore, the function $ f(x) = x + 1 $ is one-to-one but not onto from $\mathbb{N}$ to $\mathbb{N}$.

Given this analysis, the correct statements are:

Statement 1 is correct.

Statement 2 is also correct.

Correct Option: C (Both 1 and 2)

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 18 April 2021
questions.question:
If 

$f(x)=\left|\begin{array}{ccc}1 & 1 & x+1 \\ 2 x & x(x-1) & x(x+1) \\ 3 x(x-1) & 2(x-1)(x-2) & x(x+1)(x-1)\end{array}\right|$ 

then what is f(-1) + f(0) + f(1) equal to?
questions.solution:
Let the given determinant be

$$ f(x) = \begin{vmatrix} 1 & 1 & x+1 \\ 2x & x(x-1) & x(x+1) \\ 3x(x-1) & 2(x-1)(x-2) & x(x+1)(x-1) \end{vmatrix} $$

We want to find $f(-1) + f(0) + f(1)$. Let's evaluate $f(x)$ for $x=-1, 0, 1$.

For $x = -1$:

$$ f(-1) = \begin{vmatrix} 1 & 1 & 0 \\ -2 & 2 & 0 \\ 6 & 12 & 0 \end{vmatrix} = 0 $$

since the third column is all zeros.

For $x = 0$:

$$ f(0) = \begin{vmatrix} 1 & 1 & 1 \\ 0 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 2(-1)(-2) & 0 \end{vmatrix} = 0 $$

since the second row is all zeros.

For $x = 1$:

$$ f(1) = \begin{vmatrix} 1 & 1 & 2 \\ 2 & 0 & 2 \\ 0 & 0 & 0 \end{vmatrix} = 0 $$

since the third row is all zeros.

Therefore, $f(-1) + f(0) + f(1) = 0 + 0 + 0 = 0$.

Thus,

$$ f(-1) + f(0) + f(1) = 0 $$

Final Answer: The final answer is $\boxed{A}$

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 18 April 2021
questions.question: What is the domain of the function f(x) = 3<sup>x</sup>?
questions.solution:
The function $ f(x) = 3^x $ is an exponential function.

The domain of an exponential function, such as $ 3^x $, is the set of all real numbers since there are no restrictions on the values that $ x $ can take for the function to be defined. In other words, you can input any real number into the function, and it will produce a valid output.

Therefore, the domain of $ f(x) = 3^x $ is:

Option A: $ (-\infty, \infty) $

questions.title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
questions.question: The Headquarters of South-Eastern Railway is located at
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option C: Kolkata.

The South Eastern Railway (SER) zone is one of the 18 zones of Indian Railways. Its headquarters are located in Kolkata, West Bengal. The SER zone is a significant part of the Indian railway network, responsible for a large area encompassing parts of West Bengal, Odisha, Jharkhand, and Chhattisgarh. While other options mention important railway hubs in India (Bilaspur, Secunderabad, and Bhubaneswar), they are not the headquarters of the South Eastern Railway. Kolkata's central location within the SER zone's operational area makes it a strategically suitable location for the administrative and operational control of the railway network.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
questions.question: Bharatmala Pariyojana is related to
options: Interlinking of Northern and Southern Indian rivers in a garland shape.
Networks of National Highways in India.
Interlinking of all cities of India through Railways.
Interlinking of all industrial regions of India through pipelines.
questions.solution:
Bharatmala Pariyojana is related to the Networks of National Highways in India.

This ambitious infrastructure project aims to improve road connectivity across the nation through the development and expansion of national highways. It involves the construction, upgradation, and expansion of highway networks to enhance economic development by facilitating smoother transportation routes for goods and people, reducing travel time and cost, and connecting remote areas to larger markets.

questions.question: Tableau of which one of the following States/Union Territory was adjudged best in the Republic Day Parade, 2021?

questions.question: In the recently concluded elections in December 2020, Faustin-Archange Touadera has won a second term in office as President of one of the following countries. Identify the country.

Correct Answer: Option A – Central African Republic

questions.title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
questions.question: Which one of the following statements is not correct in respect of the 'Legion of Merit' award?
options: This award is conferred by the President of the United States of America.
This award was conferred to the Prime Minister of India in December, 2020.
This was also awarded to the Prime Minister of Australia, Scott Morrison.
It is the highest civilian award of the United States of America.
questions.solution:
Option D is not correct.

The "Legion of Merit" is not the highest civilian award of the United States. It is a military award given for exceptionally meritorious conduct in the performance of outstanding services and achievements. The highest civilian honor in the United States is the Presidential Medal of Freedom.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
questions.question: Which one of the following statements is most appropriate about 'Exercise Kavach'?
options: It is a military exercise of the Indian Army only.
It is a joint military exercise involving the Indian Army and the Indian Navy only.
It is a joint military exercise involving the Indian Army, the Indian Navy and the Indian Air Force only.
It is a joint military exercise involving the Indian Army, the Indian Navy, the Indian Air Force and also the Indian Coast Guard.
questions.solution: Option D is the most appropriate statement. "Exercise Kavach" is a joint military exercise involving the Indian Army, the Indian Navy, the Indian Air Force, and also the Indian Coast Guard. This exercise is aimed at improving the coordination and operational effectiveness among these forces to enhance national security and resilience against threats.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
questions.question: Recently a state of emergency has been declared in which one of the following countries?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option D: Myanmar.

A state of emergency was declared in Myanmar following the military coup in February 2021. The declaration allowed the military to assume control of the government and significantly curtail civil liberties. This declaration led to widespread protests, crackdowns, and a humanitarian crisis within the country. While other countries in the region may face challenges, the specific declaration of a state of emergency, with the resulting implications for governance and human rights, was most prominent in Myanmar at that time. It's important to note that geopolitical situations are fluid, and the information provided reflects the situation at the time of this response's creation. Always consult up-to-date news sources for the most current information on the status of any given country.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
questions.question: Which country has replaced Maldives to host the 2023 Indian Ocean Island Games?
questions.solution:
Madagascar has replaced the Maldives as the host of the 2023 Indian Ocean Island Games.

Hosting Change: Madagascar took over from the Maldives as the venue for the 2023 Indian Ocean Island Games.

Previous Decision: The event was initially awarded to the Maldives last year.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
questions.question: Identify the correct reason, out of the following, about Claire Polosak for being in the news:
options: She has been honoured with the Pulitzer Prize.
She recently won a Grand Slam championship.
She became the first female match official to conduct a men's cricket test match.
She has been conferred with the Gandhi Peace Prize.
questions.solution:
The correct answer is C.

Claire Polosak achieved significant recognition by becoming the first female match official to umpire a men's cricket Test match. This groundbreaking achievement made headlines worldwide and highlighted progress towards gender equality in officiating major sporting events. Options A, B, and D are incorrect; Claire Polosak's career is focused on cricket officiating, not journalism (Pulitzer Prize), professional athletic competition (Grand Slam), or peace activism (Gandhi Peace Prize). Her role as a Test match umpire represents a milestone in the history of cricket and sports officiating in general. It's important to note that while umpiring isn't directly "playing" the game, it is a crucial and highly skilled component of the sport, requiring extensive knowledge, judgment, and the ability to handle pressure at the highest level. Polosak's achievement is a testament to her dedication and expertise, opening doors for future female officials in cricket and other sports.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
questions.question: Which one of the following is the correct meaning of ziyarat?
options: Pilgrimate to the tombs of sufi saints
The death anniversary of a sufi shaikh
questions.solution: Ziyarat refers to the pilgrimage to the tombs of Sufi saints. This practice is an important aspect of Sufism, where devotees travel to the tombs or shrines of revered Sufi saints to seek spiritual blessings, make supplications, and show their devotion. It is Option A.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
questions.question: Which one of the following planets has the highest density?
questions.solution:
Option D: Earth

Earth has the highest density among the planets in our solar system. The average density of Earth is approximately $$5.52 \, \text{g/cm}^3$$. This high density is due to its metallic core and abundant silicate materials. To compare, here are the average densities of the other planets:

Mercury: $$5.43 \, \text{g/cm}^3$$

Venus: $$5.24 \, \text{g/cm}^3$$

Jupiter: $$1.33 \, \text{g/cm}^3$$

Jupiter, despite being the largest planet, has a much lower density because it is primarily composed of hydrogen and helium, making it a gas giant. Earth’s composition with heavier elements like iron gives it a greater density than these other planets.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
questions.question: Which one of the following Oil Refineries is not located in Assam?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is A. Tatipaka.

Tatipaka Oil Refinery is located in Andhra Pradesh, not Assam. Numaligarh, Bongaigaon, and Digboi refineries are all situated in Assam, India. These refineries play a crucial role in India's petroleum sector, processing crude oil into various petroleum products like petrol, diesel, kerosene, and other petrochemicals that are vital for the country's energy needs. The geographical location of these refineries in Assam is strategically significant, given the presence of oil and gas resources in the northeastern region of India.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
questions.question: Which one of the following is not a bio-mass energy source?
questions.solution:
The source that is not considered a biomass energy source is:

Option B: Nuclear reactor

Biomass energy sources are typically derived from organic materials. They commonly include plant-based materials like wood, agricultural residues, and gases produced from biological processes (such as gobar gas, also known as biogas). Coal, while also originally derived from biological materials, is considered a fossil fuel due to its ancient origin and different energy characteristics compared to typical biomass resources. In contrast, nuclear reactors generate energy from nuclear fission processes and are not related to biological matter.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
questions.question: Which one of the following is not a form of condensation?
questions.solution:
Sleet is not a form of condensation.

In meteorological terms, condensation refers to the process by which water vapor in the air is changed into liquid water. This process is responsible for the formation of different weather phenomena, such as:

Dew: Forms when water vapor condenses onto cool surfaces, typically overnight.

Fog: Consists of tiny water droplets suspended in the air near the ground, resulting from the condensation of water vapor.

Frost: Occurs when water vapor sublimates directly into ice crystals on surfaces, usually seen during cold conditions.

Sleet, on the other hand, is a type of precipitation composed of ice pellets formed when raindrops freeze before reaching the ground, not through condensation. It involves a different process where precipitation passes through a layer of air that is below freezing, causing the raindrops to freeze into sleet.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
questions.question: Which one of the following clouds is a rain-bearing cloud?
questions.solution:
Nimbus clouds are typically rain-bearing clouds. The term "nimbus" comes from the Latin word for "rainstorm" or "cloud," and it specifically refers to clouds that have precipitation. These clouds, which can include both nimbostratus and cumulonimbus clouds, are dense and often have a dark appearance, signaling rain or storms.

In contrast:

Cumulus clouds are generally associated with fair weather, though they can develop into larger storm clouds under the right conditions.

Stratus clouds usually bring light rain or drizzle, but they are not always associated with heavy precipitation.

Cirrus clouds are high-altitude clouds that generally indicate fair weather, although they can also signify that a change in the weather is coming, such as a warm front bringing rain.

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option C

Nimbus cloud

questions.title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
questions.question: In which one of the following countries is intensive subsistence agriculture not predominantly practiced?
questions.solution:
Intensive subsistence agriculture is not predominantly practiced in Option C: Canada.

Canada has a vast land area with diverse climatic conditions, most of which are not suitable for subsistence farming. Instead, Canada is known for its large-scale commercial agriculture, utilizing advanced technologies and machinery due to the country’s emphasis on exporting agricultural products and its relatively lower population density compared to countries where subsistence farming is more common. In contrast, countries like India, Japan, and Indonesia have regions where intensive subsistence agriculture is a significant part of the rural lifestyle, primarily due to historical, cultural, and economic factors, along with the necessity to sustain dense populations on smaller parcels of land.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
questions.question: The natural vegetation which covers the maximum geographical areas of India is
questions.solution:
The natural vegetation that covers the maximum geographical area of India is the Tropical Deciduous Forests. These forests, often referred to as monsoon forests, are predominant in India and adapt to the seasonal monsoon weather conditions.

Characteristics of Tropical Deciduous Forests:

Deciduous Nature: As the name suggests, the trees in these forests shed their leaves for about 6-8 weeks during the dry summer to conserve water.

Biodiversity: These forests are rich in biodiversity, home to a variety of flora and fauna.

Division: They are further divided into

Moist Deciduous Forests: Primarily found in regions with moderate rainfall (100-200 cm) including the northeastern states, along the foothills of the Himalayas, Jharkhand, Odisha, and parts of the Western Ghats.

Dry Deciduous Forests: Found in areas with lesser rainfall (70-100 cm) like the central part of the country, Madhya Pradesh, Gujarat, Andhra Pradesh, Karnataka, and Tamil Nadu.

Common Trees: Some of the dominant species include teak, sal, sandalwood, and bamboo.

These forests are economically significant due to the commercial value of the timber they provide and are crucial for maintaining regional ecological balance.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
questions.question: River Beas, flowing from Himachal and Punjab, joins the river
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option B: Satluj.

The Beas River, originating in the Himalayas and flowing through Himachal Pradesh and Punjab, is a major tributary of the Satluj River. The Satluj itself is one of the five rivers that make up the Punjab region of India and Pakistan, the others being the Beas, Ravi, Chenab, and Jhelum. These rivers ultimately merge to form the Indus River. Therefore, while the Beas indirectly contributes to the Indus River system, its direct confluence is with the Satluj River.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
questions.question: Which one of the following rivers is not a tributary of river Brahmaputra?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option C: River Mahananda.

The Mahananda River is a tributary of the Ganges River, not the Brahmaputra River. Let's look at the other options:

River Manas: This river originates in Bhutan and flows through Assam before joining the Brahmaputra. It's a significant tributary known for its biodiversity.

River Kameng: Also originating in the Himalayas, the Kameng River is a major tributary of the Brahmaputra, contributing significantly to its water volume.

River Subansiri: This major river, originating in the Himalayas, is a large tributary of the Brahmaputra River, known for its hydropower potential.

In summary, the Manas, Kameng, and Subansiri rivers are all tributaries of the Brahmaputra River system, while the Mahananda River flows into the Ganges River system. The Brahmaputra and Ganges are distinct major river systems in the Indian subcontinent, though they share some geographical proximity.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
questions.question: Which one of the following is not a minor plate?
questions.solution:
Option D: Antarctic Plate

The Antarctic Plate is not considered a minor plate. It is one of the major tectonic plates that cover a significant portion of the Earth's surface. In contrast, the Cocos Plate, Nazca Plate, and Caroline Plate are considered minor plates due to their smaller size and more localized impact on Earth's tectonic activities.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
questions.question:
Advantage(s) of tectonic activity in Iceland include(s):

1. Source of natural geothermal energy

2. Creation of new land

3. Attraction of tourists

Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.solution:
Advantage(s) of Tectonic Activity in Iceland:

Key Points:

Tectonic Plate Definition: A tectonic plate is a large, irregularly shaped slab of solid rock, typically comprising both continental and oceanic lithosphere. These are often referred to as lithospheric plates.

Classification of Plates:

Oceanic Plate: If a significant portion of the plate is under the ocean, it is termed an oceanic plate.

Continental Plate: If a significant portion of the plate lies under a continent, it is termed a continental plate.

Causes of Earthquakes: Tectonic plate activity is among the most common causes of earthquakes.

Geothermal and Volcanic Activity in Iceland: Iceland's tectonic plates contribute significantly to its geothermal and volcanic activity, making these areas energy-rich.

Tourism: Volcanic regions worldwide are often popular tourist destinations, attracting visitors for their geological features and natural beauty.

Additional Information:

Major and Minor Plates:

The Earth's lithosphere is divided into seven major plates and some minor plates.

Major Plates:

Antarctica and surrounding oceanic plates

North American Plate

South American Plate

Pacific Plate

India-Australia-New Zealand Plate

African Plate with the eastern Atlantic floor

Eurasian Plate and adjacent oceanic plate

Minor Plates:

Cocos Plate: Located between Central America and the Pacific Plate

Nazca Plate: Located between South America and the Pacific Plate

Arabian Plate: Found in the Saudi Arabian landmass

Philippine Plate: Situated between the Asiatic and Pacific Plate

Caroline Plate: Located between the Philippine and Indian Plate, north of Guinea

Fuji Plate: Found in the northeast of Australia

questions.title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
questions.question: Which one of the following is the longest Latitude?
questions.solution:
The length of a circle of latitude depends on its distance from the equator. The circle of latitude at the equator (0.0°) is the largest because it has the maximum radius (equal to the Earth's radius). As you move away from the equator toward the poles, the radius of the circle of latitude decreases (by a factor of $\cos(\theta)$, where $\theta$ is the latitude), and hence the circle becomes shorter.

Let's review the options:

90° Latitude: This is at the pole, which is just a point.

23.5° Latitude: This is the Tropic of Cancer or Capricorn, but its circle is shorter than the equator.

0.0° Latitude: This is the equator, the longest circle of latitude.

66.5° Latitude: This is the Arctic or Antarctic Circle, which is even shorter.

Thus, the longest circle of latitude is at 0.0° Latitude.

Correct Answer: Option C – 0.0 degree Latitude

questions.title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
questions.question: If it is 12.00 Noon in India, on which meridian will it be 7.00 am of the same day?
questions.solution:
When it is 12:00 Noon in India (which is on the 82.5° E longitude used as its standard meridian), 7:00 AM of the same day would occur at a position 5 hours earlier in time.

The Earth rotates at a rate of 15 degrees per hour (since 360 degrees corresponds to 24 hours). To find how many degrees west we need to move to achieve a 5-hour time difference, we calculate:

$$ 5 \, \text{hours} \times 15 \, \text{degrees/hour} = 75 \, \text{degrees} $$

Since we need to move westward to go backward in time, the meridian at which it is 7:00 AM when it is 12:00 Noon on the 82.5° E longitude would be:

$$ 82.5^\circ \, E - 75^\circ = 7.5^\circ \, E $$

Thus, the correct option is:

Option A: 7.5 degree E. longitude

questions.title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
questions.question: Basket-of-eggs topography is related to
questions.solution:
Basket-of-eggs topography is related to Option A: Drumlins.

Drumlins are streamlined, elongated hills formed by glacial ice acting on underlying unconsolidated till or ground moraine. They occur in groups and often resemble an overturned basket of eggs, hence the name "basket-of-eggs topography." Drumlins have a characteristic shape where one end is steep (the stoss end) and the other end tapers off gradually (the lee side), indicating the direction of glacier movement. They can vary in length from a few hundred meters to several kilometers and are typically found in fields, known as drumlin fields, indicating past glacial activity.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
questions.question: The maximum depth of Lithosphere is found in the
questions.solution:
The maximum depth of the Lithosphere is typically found in subduction zones, which are regions where one tectonic plate is being forced beneath another. These zones are often located beneath oceanic trenches, making the correct option:

Option A: Pacific Ocean

In these subduction zones, such as the Mariana Trench in the Pacific Ocean, the lithospheric plates can reach significant depths. This is due to the oceanic lithosphere being relatively thin and dense, allowing it to subduct deeply beneath the overriding plate.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
questions.question: A large body of magmatic material that cools in the deeper depth of the Earth's crust and develops in the form of large domes is known as
questions.solution:
The answer is A. Batholiths.

A batholith is a large mass of intrusive igneous rock (that is, rock formed from cooled magma deep beneath the Earth's surface) that forms from the cooling and solidification of a large volume of magma. They are typically exposed at the Earth's surface only after significant erosion of the overlying rock layers. Batholiths are characterized by their immense size; they can extend for hundreds of kilometers and have a depth that is often unknown, as their lower boundaries are generally not exposed. Their shapes are often irregular and dome-like due to the way magma intrudes and pushes aside the surrounding rock.

Let's briefly contrast batholiths with the other options:

Lacoliths: These are similar to batholiths but are smaller and lens-shaped. They form when magma intrudes between layers of sedimentary rock, forcing the overlying layers upward to create a dome-like structure. The shape is more defined and less extensive than a batholith.

Lopoliths: These are large, saucer-shaped intrusions of igneous rock that form when magma accumulates in a synclinal depression (a downward fold in rock layers). They are relatively flat compared to the dome-like shape of batholiths and laccoliths.

Phacoliths: These are relatively small, lens-shaped intrusions of igneous rock that typically occur along the crests of anticlines (upward folds in rock layers) or within the troughs of synclines. They are much smaller in scale than batholiths.

In summary, while all these terms describe intrusive igneous rock formations, batholiths are distinguished by their enormous size and dome-like shape resulting from the cooling of massive volumes of magma deep within the Earth's crust.

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2021 Paper 1 Online
questions.question: An extended object is placed at point O, 10 cm in front of a convex lens L1 and a concave lens L2 is placed 10 cm behind it, as shown in the figure. The radii of curvature of all the curved surfaces in both the lenses ae 20 cm. The refractive index of both the lenses is 1.5. The total magnification of this lens system is
