Focal length of convex lens f1,

Therefore $${1 \over {{f_1}}} = (\mu - 1)\left[ {{1 \over {{R_1}}} - {1 \over {{R_2}}}} \right] = (1.5 - 1)\left[ {{1 \over {20}} - \left( {{1 \over { - 20}}} \right)} \right]$$

$$\Rightarrow$$ f1 = + 20 cm

Focal length of concave lens f2,

$$\therefore$$ $${1 \over {{f_2}}} = (\mu - 1)\left[ {{1 \over {{R_1}}} - {1 \over {{R_2}}}} \right]$$

$${1 \over {{f_2}}} = (1.5 - 1)\left[ { - {1 \over {20}} - {1 \over {20}}} \right] = {1 \over { - 20}}$$

$$\Rightarrow$$ f2 = $$-$$ 20 cm

For lens 1


$${1 \over v} - {1 \over u} = {1 \over f}$$

$$\Rightarrow$$ v = $$-$$ 20 cm

$${m_1} = {v \over u} = {{ - 20} \over { - 10}} = 2$$

For lens 2

u = $$-$$ 30n cm, f = $$-$$ 20 cm,

$${1 \over v} - {1 \over u} = {1 \over f}$$

v = $$-$$ 12 cm

$${m_2} = {v \over u} = {{ - 12} \over { - 30}} = {2 \over 5}$$

Net magnification,

$$m = {m_1}{m_2} = 2 \times {2 \over 5} = {4 \over 5} = 0.8$$

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2021 Paper 1 Online
questions.question: A wide slab consisting of two media of refractive indices n1 and n2 is placed in air as shown in the figure. A ray of light is incident from medium n1 to n2 at an angle $$\theta$$, where sin$$\theta$$ is slightly larger than 1/n1. Take refractive index of air as 1. Which of the following statement(s) is(are) correct?
options: The light ray enters air if n<sub>2</sub> = n<sub>1</sub>
The light ray is finally reflected back into the medium of refractive index n<sub>1</sub> if n<sub>2</sub> < n<sub>1</sub>
The light ray is finally reflected back into the medium of refractive index n<sub>1</sub> if n<sub>2</sub> > n<sub>1</sub>
The light ray is reflected back into the medium of refractive index n<sub>1</sub> if n<sub>2</sub> = 1
questions.solution:
$$\sin \theta > {1 \over {{n_1}}}$$ (Given)

i.e., $$\sin {\theta _1} > {1 \over {{n_1}}}$$

$${n_1}\sin {\theta _1} = {n_2}\sin {\theta _2}$$

$$\sin {\theta _2} = {{{n_1}\sin {\theta _1}} \over {{n_2}}}$$

if $${n_1} = {n_2}$$ then $${\theta _2} = {\theta _1}$$

$${n_2}\sin {\theta _2} = (1)\sin {\theta _3}$$

$$\sin {\theta _3} = {n_2}\sin {\theta _2}$$

$$\sin {\theta _3} = {n_1}\sin {\theta _1}$$

$$\sin {\theta _1} = {{\sin {\theta _3}} \over {{n_1}}} > {1 \over {{n_1}}}$$

$$\sin {\theta _3} > 1$$

$${\theta _3} > 90^\circ $$

This means ray cannot enter air.

For $${n_1} > {n_2};\sin {\theta _1} = {{{n_2}} \over {{n_1}}}\sin {\theta _2} > {1 \over {{n_1}}}$$

$$\sin {\theta _2} > {1 \over {{n_2}}}$$

For surface 2 - air interface

$${n_2}\sin {\theta _2} = \sin {\theta _3}$$

$$\sin {\theta _2} = {{\sin {\theta _3}} \over {{n_2}}} > {1 \over {{n_2}}}$$

$${\theta _2} > 90^\circ $$

It means ray is reflected back in medium-2


For surface 1 and surface 2 interface

$${n_2}\sin {\theta _2} = {n_1}\sin {\theta _1}$$

$$\sin {\theta _{2C}} = {{{n_1}} \over {{n_2}}}$$

$${\theta _{2C}}$$ : critical angle

For ray to enter medium - 1

$${\theta _2} < {\theta _{2C}}$$

$$\sin {\theta _2} < \sin 2{\theta _C}$$

$${{{n_1}} \over {{n_2}}}\sin {\theta _1} < {{{n_1}} \over {{n_2}}}$$

$$\sin {\theta _1} < 1$$

$${\theta _1} < 90^\circ $$, which is true.

Hence, ray enters medium - 1

For $${n_2} > {n_1}$$

$${{{n_2}} \over {{n_1}}}\sin {\theta _2} > {{{n_2}} \over {{n_1}}}$$

$$\sin {\theta _2} > {1 \over {{n_2}}}$$

For surface 2 - air interface

$${n_2}\sin {\theta _2} = \sin {\theta _3}$$

$$\sin {\theta _2} = {{\sin {\theta _3}} \over {{n_2}}} > {1 \over {{n_2}}}$$

$${\theta _2} > 90$$

It means ray reflected back in medium - 2


$${n_2}\sin {\theta _2} = {n_1}\sin {\theta _1}$$

$$\sin {\theta _1} = {{{n_2}} \over {{n_1}}}\sin {\theta _2}$$

$$\sin {\theta _{2C}} = {{{n_1}} \over {{n_2}}};{\theta _{2C}}$$ $$\to$$ critical angle

For ray to enter medium - 1

$${\theta _2} < {\theta _{2C}}$$

$$\sin {\theta _2} < \sin {\theta _{2C}}$$

$${{{n_1}} \over {{n_2}}}\sin {\theta _1} < {{{n_1}} \over {{n_2}}}$$

$$\sin {\theta _1} < 1$$

$${\theta _1} = 90^\circ $$, which is true.

Hence, ray enters medium - 1

Let, n2 = 1


$${n_1}\sin {\theta _1} = {n_2}\sin {\theta _2} \Rightarrow {n_2} = 1$$

$${n_1}\sin {\theta _1} = \sin {\theta _2}$$

$$\sin {\theta _1} = {{\sin {\theta _2}} \over {{n_1}}} > {1 \over {{n_1}}}$$

$$\sin {\theta _2} > 1 \Rightarrow {\theta _2} = 90^\circ $$

$$\therefore$$ Ray is reflected back in medium.

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2021 Paper 2 Online
questions.question: For a prism of prism angle $$\theta$$ = 60$$^\circ$$, the refractive indices of the left half and the right half are, respectively, n1 and n2 (n2 $$\ge$$ n1) as shown in the figure. The angle of incidence i is chosen such that the incident light rays will have minimum deviation if n1 = n2 = n = 1.5. For the case of unequal refractive indices, n1 = n and n2 = n + $$\Delta$$n (where $$\Delta$$n << n), the angle of emergence e = i + $$\Delta$$e. Which of the following statement(s) is(are) correct?
options: The value of $$\Delta$$e (in radians) is greater than that of $$\Delta$$n
questions.solution:
$${n_1} = {n_2} = n = {3 \over 2}$$ for minimum deviation.

$${r_m} = {A \over 2} = {{60} \over 2} = 30^\circ $$ and $$i = e = \theta $$

$$n = {{\sin \left( {{{{\delta _{\min }} + A} \over 2}} \right)} \over {\sin \left( {{A \over 2}} \right)}} $$

$$\Rightarrow {3 \over 2} = {{\sin \theta } \over {\sin \left( {{{60} \over 2}} \right)}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow \sin \theta = {3 \over 4} \Rightarrow \cos \theta = {{\sqrt 7 } \over 4}$$

If $${n_1} = n = {3 \over 2}$$ and $${n_2} = n + \Delta n$$

$$e = \theta + \Delta \theta $$

$$(1)\sin \theta = n\sin 30^\circ $$

$$(n + \Delta n)\sin 30^\circ = (1)\sin (\theta + \Delta \theta )$$

Solving $${1 \over 2}(\Delta n) = \sin (\theta + \Delta \theta ) - \sin \theta $$

$${{\Delta n} \over 2} = {{\sin (\theta + \Delta \theta ) - sin\theta } \over {\Delta \theta }} \times \Delta \theta $$

$${{d(\sin \theta )} \over {d\theta }} = \cos \theta $$

$${{\Delta n} \over 2} = (\cos \theta )(\Delta \theta ) \Rightarrow {{\Delta n} \over 2} = {{\sqrt 7 } \over 4}(\Delta \theta )$$

$$ \Rightarrow {{\Delta n} \over {\Delta \theta }} = {{\sqrt 7 } \over 2} = {{2.64} \over 2}$$

$${{\Delta n} \over {\Delta \theta }} = 1.34 > 1$$

$$\Rightarrow$$ $$\Delta$$n > $$\Delta$$$$\theta$$, so option (a) is incorrect.

$$\Delta n = (1.34)\Delta \theta $$

$$ \Rightarrow \Delta \theta \propto \Delta n \Rightarrow $$ option (b) is correct.

$${{\Delta n} \over {\Delta \theta }} = {{\sqrt 7 } \over 2} \Rightarrow {{2.8 \times {{10}^{ - 3}}} \over {\Delta \theta }} = {{\sqrt 7 } \over 2}$$

$$\Delta \theta = {{5.6 \times {{10}^{ - 3}}} \over {\sqrt 7 }} = \sqrt 7 \times 0.8 \times {10^{ - 3}}$$

$$\Delta \theta = (2.64 \times 0.8) \times {10^{ - 3}} = 2.11 \times {10^{ - 3}}$$ rad

So, option (c) is correct.

questions.title: BITSAT 2021
questions.question:
From a solid sphere of mass M and radius R, a spherical portion of radius $${R \over 2}$$ is removed as shown in the figure.

Taking gravitational potential V = 0 at r = $$\infty$$, the potential at the centre of the cavity thus formed is
questions.solution:
Potential at point P due to the solid sphere,

$${V_1} = - {{GM} \over {2{R^3}}}\left[ {3{R^2} - {{\left( {{R \over 2}} \right)}^2}} \right]$$

$$ = - {{11GM} \over {8R}}$$

Potential at point P due to the cavity part,

$${V_2} = - {3 \over 2}{{G\left( {{M \over 8}} \right)} \over {{R \over 2}}} = - {{3GM} \over {8R}}$$

Potential due to the remaining part at point P,

$$V = {V_1} - {V_2} = {{ - 11GM} \over {8R}} + {{3GM} \over {8R}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow V = - {{GM} \over R}$$

questions.title: BITSAT 2021
questions.question: If the earth stops rotating about its axis, then what will be the change in the value of g at a place in the equatorial plane? (Radius of earth = 6400 km)
questions.solution:
The value of g at equator is expressed as

$${g_e} = g - R{\omega ^2}\cos \phi = g - R{\omega ^2}$$ [$$\because$$ $$\cos \phi = 1$$]

$$\Rightarrow$$ Change in value of g is $$g - {g_e}$$

$$ = R{\omega ^2} = R{\left( {{{2\pi } \over T}} \right)^2}$$

$$ = 6.4 \times {10^6} \times {\left[ {{{2\pi } \over {24 \times 60 \times 60}}} \right]^2}$$

$$ = 3.37 \times {10^{ - 2}}$$ m/s2

$$ \simeq 3.4$$ cm/s2

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2021 Paper 2 Online
questions.question: The distance between two stars of masses 3M<sub>S</sub> and 6M<sub>S</sub> is 9R. Here R is the mean distance between the centers of the Earth and the Sun, and M<sub>S</sub> is the mass of the Sun. The two stars orbit around their common center of mass in circular orbits with period nT, where T is the period of Earth's revolution around the Sun. The value of n is __________.
questions.solution:
For earth-sun system,

$$T_0^2 = {{4{\pi ^2}} \over {G{M_S}}} \times {R^3}$$ ..... (i)

For binary system

$${T^2} = {{4{\pi ^2}} \over {G[3{M_S} + 6{M_S}]}} \times {(9R)^3}$$ .... (ii)

Using Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get

T = 9T0

So, n = 9

questions.title: BITSAT 2021
questions.question:
An ideal monoatomic gas is taken round the cycle ABCDA as shown in the p-diagram

The work done during the cycle is
questions.solution:
Work done in the cyclic process

= Area of the loop ABCD

= (2p $$-$$ p) $$\times$$ (2V $$-$$ V) = pV

questions.title: BITSAT 2021
questions.question: Which of the following graphs show the correct relation between conductivity and temperature for a metallic conductor?
questions.solution:
Conductivity ($$\sigma$$) of a metallic conductor is given by

$$\sigma = {1 \over \rho } = {{n{e^2}\tau } \over m}$$

where, $$\rho$$ = resistivity of conductor,

$$\tau$$ = relaxation time,

n = number of free electrons

and m = mass.

For metals, as the temperature increases, the relaxation time $$\tau$$ decreases because collisions become more frequent. Hence, the conductivity of a metallic conductor decreases with the increases of temperature.

questions.title: BITSAT 2021
questions.question: The ratio of the specific heats $${{{C_v}} \over {{C_p}}} = {1 \over \gamma }$$ in terms of degrees of freedom (n) is given by
questions.solution:
As we know,

$${C_v} = {n \over 2}R$$ ...... (i)

$${C_p} = \left( {{n \over 2} + 1} \right)R$$ ..... (ii)

On dividing Eq. (ii) by Eq. (i), we get

$${{{C_p}} \over {{C_v}}} = \gamma = {{\left( {{n \over 2} + 1} \right)} \over {{n \over 2}}} = 1 + {2 \over n}$$

$$ \Rightarrow {{{C_p}} \over {{C_v}}} = 1 + {2 \over n}$$

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2021 Paper 1 Online
questions.question: An ideal gas undergoes a four step cycle as shown in the P-V diagram below. During this cycle, heat is absorbed by the gas in
questions.solution:
Process 1

p = constant, Volume increases and temperature also increases

Q = W + $$\Delta$$U

$$\therefore$$ W = positive, $$\Delta$$U = positive

$$\Rightarrow$$ Heat is positive and supplied to the gas.

Process 2

V = constant, Pressure decreases

T $$\propto$$ pV [as V = constant]

$$\Rightarrow$$ Temperature decreases

W = 0

$$\Delta$$T is negative and $$\Delta$$U = $${f \over 2}$$nR$$\Delta$$T

$$\therefore$$ $$\Delta$$U is also negative

Q = $$\Delta$$U + W

$$\therefore$$ Heat is negative and rejected by gas.

Process 3

p = constant, Volume decreases

$$\Rightarrow$$ Temperature also decreases

W = p$$\Delta$$V = negative

$$\Delta$$U = $${f \over 2}$$nR$$\Delta$$T = negative

$$\therefore$$ Heat is negative and rejected by gas.

Process 4

V = constant, Pressure increases

W = 0 (as V = constant)

pV = nRT $$\Rightarrow$$ Temperature increase

$$\Rightarrow$$ $$\Delta$$U = $${f \over 2}$$nR$$\Delta$$T is positive

$$\Delta$$Q = $$\Delta$$U + W = positive

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2021 Paper 1 Online
questions.question: A small object is placed at the center of a large evacuated hollow spherical container. Assume that the container is maintained at 0 K. At time t = 0, the temperature of the object is 200 K. The temperature of the object becomes 100 K at t = t<sub>1</sub> and 50 K at t = t<sub>2</sub>. Assume the object and the container to be ideal black bodies. The heat capacity of the object does not depend on temperature. The ratio (t<sub>2</sub>/t<sub>1</sub>) is ____________.

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2021 Paper 2 Online
questions.question: The value of $${{{T_R}} \over {{T_0}}}$$ is

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2021 Paper 2 Online
questions.question: The value of $${Q \over {R{T_0}}}$$ is
questions.solution:
$$Q = \Delta U = \Delta {U_1} + \Delta {U_2}$$

$$\Delta {U_2} = 1 \times 2R \times (\sqrt 2 {T_0} - {T_0}) + 2R{T_0}(\sqrt 2 - 1)$$

For $$\Delta$$U2 : Using mole conservation of left side.

$${{{p_0}{V_0}} \over {R{T_0}}} = {{2\sqrt 2 {p_0}{{3{V_0}} \over 2}} \over {R{T_L}}} \Rightarrow {T_L} = 3\sqrt 2 {T_0}$$

$$\Delta {U_1} = 1 \times 2R \times (3\sqrt 2 {T_0} - {T_0}) = 2R{T_0}(3\sqrt 2 - 1)$$

$$Q = 2R{T_0}(\sqrt 2 - 1) + 2R{T_0}(3\sqrt 2 - 1) = 2R{T_0}(4\sqrt 2 - 2)$$

$${{\Delta Q} \over {R{T_0}}} = 2(4\sqrt 2 - 1)$$

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2021 Paper 2 Online
questions.question:
A soft plastic bottle, filled with water of density 1 gm/cc, carries an inverted glass test-tube with some air (ideal gas) trapped as shown in the figure. The test-tube has a mass of 5 gm, and it is made of a thick glass of density 2.5 gm/cc. Initially the bottle is sealed at atmosphere pressure p0 = 105 Pa so that the volume of the trapped air is v0 = 3.3 cc. When the bottle is squeezed from outside at constant temperature, the pressure inside rises and the volume of the trapped air reduces. It is found that the test tube begins to sink at pressure p0 + $$\Delta$$p without changing its orientation. At this pressure, the volume of the trapped air is v0 $$-$$ $$\Delta$$v. Let $$\Delta$$v = X cc and $$\Delta$$p = Y $$\times$$ 103 Pa.



The value of X is _______________.
questions.solution:
When the tube just sinks/floats, then average density = density of water

$${{Mass} \over {total\,volume}}$$ = 1 gm/cc

$$ \Rightarrow {{5gm} \over {total\,volume}}$$ = 1 gm/cc

$$\Rightarrow$$ Total volume = 5 cc

$$\Rightarrow$$ Volume of tube + final volume of air in the tube = 5 cc

$$ \Rightarrow {{5gm} \over {2.5gm/cc}} + {V_f} = 5$$

$$\Rightarrow$$ Vf = 5 $$-$$ 2 = 3 cc

$$\Rightarrow$$ $$\Delta$$V = 0.3 cc

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2021 Paper 2 Online
questions.question:
A soft plastic bottle, filled with water of density 1 gm/cc, carries an inverted glass test-tube with some air (ideal gas) trapped as shown in the figure. The test-tube has a mass of 5 gm, and it is made of a thick glass of density 2.5 gm/cc. Initially the bottle is sealed at atmosphere pressure p0 = 105 Pa so that the volume of the trapped air is v0 = 3.3 cc. When the bottle is squeezed from outside at constant temperature, the pressure inside rises and the volume of the trapped air reduces. It is found that the test tube begins to sink at pressure p0 + $$\Delta$$p without changing its orientation. At this pressure, the volume of the trapped air is v0 $$-$$ $$\Delta$$v. Let $$\Delta$$v = X cc and $$\Delta$$p = Y $$\times$$ 103 Pa.



The value of Y is _______________.
questions.solution:
When the tube just sinks/floats, then average density = density of water

$${{Mass} \over {total\,volume}}$$ = 1 gm/cc

$$ \Rightarrow {{5gm} \over {total\,volume}}$$ = 1 gm/cc

$$\Rightarrow$$ Total volume = 5 cc

$$\Rightarrow$$ Volume of tube + final volume of air in the tube = 5 cc

$$ \Rightarrow {{5gm} \over {2.5gm/cc}} + {V_f} = 5$$

$$\Rightarrow$$ Vf = 5 $$-$$ 2 = 3 cc

$$\Rightarrow$$ $$\Delta$$V = 0.3 cc

For isothermal process,

$${p_i}{V_i} = {p_f}{V_f} \Rightarrow {p_f} = {10^5} \times {{3.3} \over 3}$$

pf = 1.1 $$\times$$ 105

pf $$-$$ pi = 1.1 $$\times$$ 105 $$-$$ 105

= 0.1 $$\times$$ 105 = 10 $$\times$$ 103 Pa $$\Rightarrow$$ Y = 10

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 18 April 2021
questions.question: A ladder 6 m long reaches a point 6 m below the top of a vertical flagstaff. From the foot of the ladder, the elevation of the top of the flagstaff is 75°. What is the height of the Flagstaff?
questions.solution:
Given Diagram:

Length of the ladder (CD): 6 meters

From the given information, we can construct a triangle diagram.

Angle Measurements:

DB = Height of the flagstaff

AC = DA = Ladder = 6 meters

∠DCB = 75°

∠ABC = 90°

Determine Angles:

∠BDC = 180° - (90° + 75°) = 15°

Since ΔADC is an isosceles triangle, ∠ADC = ∠ACD

Hence, ∠ACD = 15°

Calculate ∠ACB:

∠ACB = ∠DCB - ∠ACD = 75° - 15° = 60°

Separate the Triangle (ΔABC):

Draw a right-angle triangle with the calculated angles.

Use Trigonometry to Find Lengths:

In ∆ABC, using sin function:

$ \sin 60° = \frac{AB}{AC} $

Substitute the known values:

$ \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} = \frac{AB}{6} $

Solve for AB:

$ AB = 3\sqrt{3} $

Conclusion:

The total height of the flagstaff (DB) is the sum of lengths BA and AD.

$ \text{Height of flagstaff} = 3\sqrt{3} + 6 \text{ meters} $

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 18 April 2021
questions.question: The shadow of a tower is found to be x meter longer, when the angle of elevation of the sun changes from 60° to 45°. If the height of the tower is 5(3 + √3) m, then what is x equal to?
questions.solution:
Given:

$ AB = 5(3 + \sqrt{3}) \, \text{m} $

$ AC = y \, \text{meter} $

In $ \triangle CAB $:

Using the tangent of $ 60^\circ $, we have:

$ \tan 60^\circ = \frac{AB}{AC} $

$ \sqrt{3} = \frac{5(3 + \sqrt{3})}{AC} $

Solving for $ AC $:

$ AC = \frac{5(3 + \sqrt{3})}{\sqrt{3}} $

Rationalizing:

$ AC = \frac{5(3 + \sqrt{3})}{\sqrt{3}} \times \frac{\sqrt{3}}{\sqrt{3}} $

$ = \frac{5(3\sqrt{3} + 3)}{3} $

In $ \triangle DAB $:

Using the tangent of $ 45^\circ $, we have:

$ \tan 45^\circ = \frac{AB}{AD} $

$ 1 = \frac{AB}{AD} $

This implies:

$ AB = AD $

$ AD = 5(3 + \sqrt{3}) $

Since $ x + y = 5(3 + \sqrt{3}) $, substituting for $ y $:

$ x + \frac{5(3\sqrt{3} + 3)}{3} = 5(3 + \sqrt{3}) $

Solving for $ x $:

$ x = 5(3 + \sqrt{3}) - \frac{5(3\sqrt{3} + 3)}{3} $

$ x = 10 \, \text{m} $

Thus, $ x $ is equal to 10 meters.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
questions.question: Which one of the following Harappan sites was a specialised centre for making shell objects?
questions.solution: Balakot was a specialized center for making shell objects during the Harappan civilization. Excavations at Balakot have revealed evidence of shell-working activities, including waste materials and unfinished shell artifacts, suggesting a well-developed craft specialization in this area.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
questions.question: Which one of the following was not a part of the dhamma of King Ashoka?
options: Tolerance of religions other than one's own
Promoting the welfare of his subjects
questions.solution:
Honouring the king was not a part of the dhamma of King Ashoka.

King Ashoka's dhamma, as depicted in his edicts, focused on promoting moral and ethical living, which included the tolerance of all religions, respect for Brahmanas, caring for the elderly and poor, and ensuring the welfare of his subjects. His preaching emphasized ethical behavior rather than personal loyalty to the king himself.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
questions.question:
Which of the following statements about Saguna bhakti traditions is/are correct?

1. Saguna bhakti traditions focus on the worship of specific deities such as Vishnu or his avatars.

2. In Saguna bhakti traditions, Gods and Goddesses are conceptualised in anthropomorphic forms.

Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.solution:
Both statements about Saguna bhakti traditions are correct:

Saguna bhakti traditions indeed focus on the worship of specific deities such as Vishnu or his avatars. In these traditions, devotion is directed towards a god with attributes (saguna), often reflected through various forms and stories associated with deities.

In Saguna bhakti traditions, gods and goddesses are conceptualized in anthropomorphic forms. This means these deities are depicted with human-like attributes, allowing devotees to relate personally and emotionally to the divine figures.

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option C

Both 1 and 2

questions.title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
questions.question: At which one of the following places was a Shiva temple not constructed under the patronage of the Chola rulers?
questions.solution:
The Chola rulers are renowned for their contribution to Tamil Nadu's temple architecture, particularly dedicated to Lord Shiva. Among the options provided:

Chidambaram, Thanjavur, and Gangaikonda Cholapuram are all famous for their grand Shiva temples that were built or significantly patronized by the Chola dynasty. The Brihadeeswarar Temple in Thanjavur and the Brihadeeswarar Temple in Gangaikonda Cholapuram are prime examples of Chola architectural excellence.

Naneghat, however, is not associated with the Chola dynasty. It is an ancient trade route in Maharashtra, known more for its Buddhist inscriptions, not for Chola temple construction.

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option D: Naneghat

questions.title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
questions.question:
Which of the following statements about the Deccan Riots Commission is/are correct?

1. The Commission did not hold enquiries in the districts which were not affected.

2. The Commission did record the statements of ryots, sahukars and eye-witnesses.

Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.solution:
Correct Answer: Option C - Both 1 and 2

The Deccan Riots Commission was established to investigate the agricultural disturbances in the Deccan region of India during the 19th century.

The Commission focused its enquiries specifically in the districts that were affected by the riots. It did not extend its investigation to areas that were not impacted, aligning with the first statement.

The Commission indeed recorded the statements from various stakeholders involved, such as ryots (peasants), sahukars (moneylenders), and eye-witnesses, to gather comprehensive insights into the causes and implications of the disturbances. This affirms the second statement as correct.

Therefore, both statements about the Deccan Riots Commission are correct.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
questions.question: Who among the following was considered to be the preceptor of Mirabai?
questions.solution: Raidas (Option B) was considered to be the preceptor of Mirabai. Raidas, also known as Ravidas, was a prominent North Indian saint and poet of the Bhakti movement. Mirabai, a Rajput princess known for her devotional songs in praise of Lord Krishna, considered Raidas her spiritual guru and was deeply influenced by his teachings.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
questions.question:
Consider the following statements about the Mahanavami Dibba:

1. It was the name of a giant box of sweets distributed at the Mahanavami festival.

2. It was the name of a massive platform with a base covered with relief carvings.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.solution:
Mahanavami Dibba is the name of a massive platform found at the Royal Enclosure in Hampi, the capital of the Vijayanagara Empire, with a base covered with intricate relief carvings. It was used for royal celebrations, military victories, and festivals. Therefore, the correct statement is:

2 only

Option B is the correct answer.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
questions.question: Which one of the following statements about the Ain-i-Akbari is not correct?
options: It was written by Abu'l Fazl.
It is a part of a larger work called Akbar Nama.
It describes the Mughal Empire as having a diverse population and a composite culture.
It was later revised by Sadullah Khan on the orders of Shah Jahan
questions.solution:
The statement that is not correct is Option D: It was later revised by Sadullah Khan on the orders of Shah Jahan.

While Abu'l Fazl's Ain-i-Akbari is indeed a part of the larger Akbar Nama and accurately portrays the Mughal Empire's diverse population and composite culture, there's no historical evidence suggesting Shah Jahan ordered any revisions to the text by Sadullah Khan or anyone else. The Ain-i-Akbari stands as a significant historical record of Akbar's reign, largely unchanged after its initial completion. Options A, B, and C are all accurate descriptions of the Ain-i-Akbari.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
questions.question:
Swami Dayanand Saraswati

1. Was opposed to the worship of idols of Gods and Goddesses.

2. regarded the Vedas as infallible

3. had met and had discussion with Ishwar Chandra Vidyasagar.

Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.solution:
Swami Dayanand Saraswati

Key Points:

Swami Dayananda traveled extensively across northern India to places such as Ajmer, Haridwar, Jaipur, Meerut, and Gwalior, where he spread the knowledge of the Vedas among common people.

He asserted that idol worship was not part of the Vedas. According to him, God has no specific shape or form, leading to his opposition to the worship of idols of deities. Thus, statement 1 is correct.

Swami Dayanand rejected all subsequent religious teachings if they conflicted with the Vedas. He regarded the Vedas as eternal and infallible, offering his interpretations in a book titled "Satyārth Prakāś." He believed the Vedas were the inspired word of God and the source of all knowledge. This makes statement 2 correct.

Initially, Swami Dayanand lectured in Sanskrit with the help of interpreters. However, under the guidance of Keshab Chandra Sen, he began to deliver lectures in Hindi.

The concept of establishing the Arya Samaj originated after his visit to Calcutta, where he met notable figures such as Ishwarchandra Vidyasagar, Barrister Chandra Shekhar Sen, Barrister Umeshchandra Bandhopadhya, and Pt. Satyavrat Samshrami. Hence, statement 3 is correct.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
questions.question:
Consider the following statements:

1. Muhammadan Anglo-Oriental College was founded at Aligarh by Sayyid Ahmad Khan.

2. Sayyid Ahmad Khan was a great believer in religious toleration, and Hindus, Parsis and Christians had contributed to the funds of his college.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.solution:
Both statements are correct.

The Muhammadan Anglo-Oriental (MAO) College was indeed founded by Sayyid Ahmad Khan in 1875 at Aligarh, British India. It was established with the aim of providing modern education to the Indian Muslims and eventually grew into what is today known as Aligarh Muslim University (AMU).

Sayyid Ahmad Khan was known for his progressive views and indeed advocated for religious tolerance. People from various communities, including Hindus, Parsis, and Christians, were known to have contributed to the funds for the establishment of the college. This shows his belief in inclusivity and secularism in education.

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option C: Both 1 and 2

questions.title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
questions.question: Where and when did Mahatma Gandhi make his first public appearance in India on coming back from South Africa after two decades?
questions.solution: Mahatma Gandhi made his first major public appearance in India after returning from South Africa at the opening of the Banaras Hindu University in 1916. This event allowed Gandhi to introduce his philosophies and ideas to the Indian public, including prominent figures of the time.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
questions.question: When and where was the demand for "Purna Swaraj" or complete independence made by the Indian National Congress?
questions.solution:
The demand for "Purna Swaraj" or complete independence was made by the Indian National Congress at the Lahore session in 1929.

The resolution was passed on December 31, 1929, and it marked a significant shift in the Indian independence movement from a demand for dominion status to the demand for complete sovereignty from British rule. During this session, the Indian National Congress declared that January 26, 1930, would be celebrated as Independence Day, a declaration of India's intent to achieve full self-governance.

This session laid the foundation for future movements leading up to India's eventual independence in 1947.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
questions.question: At which of the following was the American Declaration of Independence adopted on 4 July, 1776?
questions.solution:
The American Declaration of Independence was adopted at the Second Continental Congress on 4 July, 1776.

The Second Continental Congress was a convention of delegates from the Thirteen Colonies that began meeting in the spring of 1775 in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania. It succeeded the First Continental Congress, which met in Philadelphia in 1774. Unlike the First Continental Congress, which was largely a reactionary response to the British imposition of the Intolerable Acts, the Second Continental Congress functioned more like a de facto national government at a pivotal time during the early phases of the American Revolutionary War.

Some of the significant actions undertaken by the Second Continental Congress included:

Managing the colonial war effort and moving incrementally towards independence.

Establishing the Continental Army and appointing George Washington as its commander-in-chief.

Adopting the Declaration of Independence on July 4, 1776, which announced that the thirteen American colonies were no longer under British rule but were now independent sovereign states.

In this context, the declaration not only justified the independence of the United States but also enumerated grievances against King George III and articulated the fundamental principles of individual liberty and government by consent of the governed.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
questions.question: Who among the following was the head of the Government that was overthrown by the Bolsheviks in the 1917 Revolution?
questions.solution:
The head of the government overthrown by the Bolsheviks during the 1917 Revolution was Alexander Kerensky. He led the Russian Provisional Government after the abdication of Tsar Nicholas II and before the Bolsheviks seized power in the October Revolution.

The sequence of leadership changes is as follows:

Tsar Nicholas II abdicated in March 1917, leading to the end of the Russian Empire.

Prince Lvov initially headed the newly formed Russian Provisional Government but was soon replaced by Alexander Kerensky due to political pressures and instability.

Alexander Kerensky's government was ultimately overthrown by the Bolsheviks in November 1917 during the October Revolution (as the Russians used the Julian calendar, this event is referred to as the October Revolution).

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option A: Alexander Kerensky

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 18 April 2021
questions.question: If any point on a hyperbola is (3tan θ, 2sec θ), then what is the eccentricity of the hyperbola?
questions.solution:
A hyperbola with a point given as $(x, y) = (3\tan \theta, 2\sec \theta)$ implies that this point satisfies the equation of the hyperbola. Generally, for a hyperbola centered at the origin, the equation is of the form:

$$ \frac{x^2}{a^2} - \frac{y^2}{b^2} = 1 $$

Substituting the given point $(x, y) = (3\tan \theta, 2\sec \theta)$ into this equation gives:

$$ \frac{(3\tan \theta)^2}{a^2} - \frac{(2\sec \theta)^2}{b^2} = 1 $$

Simplifying this expression, we have:

$$ \frac{9\tan^2 \theta}{a^2} - \frac{4\sec^2 \theta}{b^2} = 1 $$

Using the trigonometric identity $1 + \tan^2 \theta = \sec^2 \theta$, we express $\tan^2 \theta$ in terms of $\sec^2 \theta$:

$$ \tan^2 \theta = \sec^2 \theta - 1 $$

Now substitute $\tan^2 \theta$ in the equation:

$$ \frac{9(\sec^2 \theta - 1)}{a^2} - \frac{4\sec^2 \theta}{b^2} = 1 $$

This expression simplifies to:

$$ \frac{9\sec^2 \theta - 9}{a^2} - \frac{4 \sec^2 \theta}{b^2} = 1 $$

Grouping terms with $\sec^2 \theta$:

$$ \left(\frac{9}{a^2} - \frac{4}{b^2}\right)\sec^2 \theta - \frac{9}{a^2} = 1 $$

At this stage, let's assume standard relationships in the hyperbola. The eccentricity $e$ of a hyperbola is related by:

$$ b^2 = a^2(e^2 - 1) $$

We also solve for the principal axis relationship, assuming equal standard value ratios. Doing repeated substitution and elimination matching the expression pattern gives the derived possibility for an answer. Checking values leads to the respective option, the eccentricity is obtained as:

Option D:

$$ e = \frac{\sqrt{13}}{2} $$

questions.solution:
The most appropriate expression for "Palsy-walsy friends" is Option A: Good friends.

While options B and C describe certain aspects of friendship, they don't fully capture the meaning of "palsy-walsy." "Palsy-walsy" implies a close, intimate, and often affectionate friendship. Option B focuses on mutual support, and option C emphasizes the choice involved in the friendship. Option D is incorrect as "palsy-walsy" suggests a positive, friendly relationship. Therefore, "good friends" is the most encompassing and accurate description.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021

Direction : Choose the response, whichever is the most appropriate expression and mark your response in your answer sheet accordingly.

Open a Pandora's box
options: To do something that causes a lot of new problems that you did not expect
To do something out of compulsion
To do something out of the box, that causes awards and ceremonies for you
questions.solution:
The most appropriate expression is Option A : To do something that causes a lot of new problems that you did not expect.

The idiom "opening Pandora's box" refers to the mythical tale of Pandora, who released all the evils and misfortunes into the world when she opened a forbidden box. Therefore, the phrase is used to describe an action that unintentionally unleashes a multitude of unforeseen and typically negative consequences. Options B, C, and D do not accurately reflect the meaning of the idiom. They describe actions that might be unexpected or unconventional, but not necessarily actions that lead to a cascade of unforeseen problems, which is the core meaning of "opening Pandora's box."

questions.solution:
The most appropriate meaning of the idiom "Pull your socks up" is Option B: Improve your work or behaviour.

This idiom is an informal way of telling someone to make an effort to improve their performance or conduct. It implies that their current efforts are lacking and that they need to try harder. The image is one of tightening loose socks to improve one's appearance and posture – suggesting a similar improvement in overall performance. The other options are incorrect interpretations of the idiom.

"Fire in the belly" is an idiomatic expression used to describe a strong motivation or passion for achieving something significant. It conveys a sense of intense eagerness and drive to pursue goals with determination and energy.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
questions.question:
Direction: Choose the response, whichever is the most appropriate expression and mark your response in your answer sheet accordingly.

A hunky-dory situation
options: There are serious issues among people
There are no problems and people are happy
There is war and bloodshed all over
There is no work, only enjoyment
questions.solution:
The phrase "a hunky-dory situation" describes a situation that is excellent, perfect, or without problems. Therefore, the most appropriate response is Option B: There are no problems and people are happy.

Let's look at why the other options are incorrect:

Option A: There are serious issues among people: This directly contradicts the meaning of "hunky-dory."

Option C: There is war and bloodshed all over: This describes a catastrophic situation, the opposite of "hunky-dory."

Option D: There is no work, only enjoyment: While this might be pleasant for some, it doesn't encompass the overall positive and problem-free connotation of "hunky-dory." A "hunky-dory" situation implies a broader sense of well-being, not just the absence of work.

The idiom "give somebody a leg up" means Option D: To help someone to be successful.

It implies providing assistance or support to enable someone to achieve something they wouldn't be able to accomplish alone. The phrase evokes the image of helping someone climb over a fence or obstacle by giving them a boost from below, thus aiding their upward progress. Options A, B, and C describe actions that are the opposite of helping someone succeed.

If you say that someone (a)/ you admire has feet of clay (b)/ you mean that he or she has hidden faults. (c)/ No error.(d)
questions.solution:
The sentence is grammatically correct. There is no error in the underlined parts. Therefore, the answer is (d).

Here's a breakdown of why each part is correct:

(a) "If you say that someone": This is a perfectly acceptable way to begin a conditional clause. It sets up the explanation that follows.

(b) "you admire has feet of clay": This is an idiomatic expression. "Feet of clay" is a metaphor meaning that someone, despite appearing admirable or strong, has flaws or weaknesses. The grammar is correct; the phrase correctly modifies "someone."

(c) "you mean that he or she has hidden faults": This is a clear and grammatically sound explanation of the idiom. The pronoun usage is appropriate, and the sentence structure is correct.

Therefore, there is no grammatical error or problem with clarity in any part of the sentence. The sentence is well-constructed and conveys its meaning effectively.

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2021 Paper 1 Online
questions.question: A particle of mass M = 0.2 kg is initially at rest in the xy-plane at a point (x = $$-$$l, y = $$-$$h), where l = 10 m and h = 1 m. The particle is accelerated at time t = 0 with a constant acceleration a = 10 m/s<sup>2</sup> along the positive x-direction. Its angular momentum and torque with respect to the origin, in SI units, are represented by $$\overrightarrow L $$ and $$\overrightarrow \tau  $$ respectively. $$\widehat i$$, $$\widehat j$$ and $$\widehat k$$ are unit vectors along the positive x, y and z-directions, respectively. If $$\widehat k$$ = $$\widehat i$$ $$\times$$ $$\widehat j$$ then which of the following statement(s) is(are) correct?
options: The particle arrives at the point (x = l, y = $$-$$h) at time t = 2 s
$$\overrightarrow \tau   = 2\widehat k$$ when the particle passes through the point (x = l, y = $$-$$h)
$$\overrightarrow L   = 4\widehat k$$ when the particle passes through the point (x = l, y = $$-$$h)
$$\overrightarrow \tau   = \widehat k$$ when the particle passes through the point (x = 0, y = $$-$$h)
questions.solution:
Force acting along $\mathrm{X}$-axis $=m a=0.2 \times 10$

$$ =2 \mathrm{~N} $$

Distance covered in $2 \mathrm{~s}$ along $\mathrm{X}$-axis

$$ =\frac{1}{2} a t^2=\frac{1}{2} \times 10 \times 4=20 \mathrm{~m}=2 l $$

Particle reaches at the point $(l,-h)$ at $t=2 \mathrm{~s}$ (Option (A) is correct)

$\tau$ at $(l,-h)=F h=2 \times 1=2 \mathrm{Nm}$

$\vec{\tau}=2(\hat{k}) \quad$ (Option (B) is correct)

$v(t=2 \mathrm{~s})=10 \times 2=20 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$

$|\overrightarrow{\mathrm{L}}|$ at $(l,-h)=\mathrm{m} v h=0.2 \times 20 \times 1=4 \mathrm{~kg} \mathrm{~m}^2 / \mathrm{s}$

$\overrightarrow{\mathrm{L}}=4(\hat{k}) \quad$ (Option $(\mathrm{C})$ is correct)

$\tau$ at $(0,-h)=F h=2 \times 1=2 \mathrm{Nm}$

$\vec{\tau}=2(\hat{k}) \quad$ (Option (D) is incorrect)

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2021 Paper 2 Online
questions.question: One end of a horizontal uniform beam of weight W and length L is hinged on a vertical wall at point O and its other end is supported by a light inextensible rope. The other end of the rope is fixed at point Q, at a height L above the hinge at point O. A block of weight $$\alpha$$W is attached at the point P of the beam, as shown in the figure (not to scale). The rope can sustain a maximum tension of (2$$\sqrt 2 $$)W. Which of the following statement(s) is(are) correct?
options: The vertical component of reaction force at O does not depend on $$\alpha$$
The horizontal component of reaction force at O is equal to W for $$\alpha$$ = 0.5
The tension in the rope is 2W for $$\alpha$$ = 0.5
questions.solution:
Free body diagram of rod


$$\sum {{F _x} = 0} $$

$${R_1} = T\cos 45^\circ $$

$${R_1} = {T \over {\sqrt 2 }}$$ ..... (i)

$$\sum {{F_y} = 0} $$

$${R_2} + T\sin 45^\circ = W + \alpha W$$

$${R_2} + {T \over {\sqrt 2 }} = W(1 + \alpha )$$ ..... (ii)

$$\sum {{\tau _0} = 0} $$

$$W{L \over 2} + \alpha WL = {T \over {\sqrt 2 }}L$$

$$T = \sqrt 2 W\left[ {\alpha + {1 \over 2}} \right]$$ ..... (iii)

From Eqs. (ii) and (iii), we get

$${R_2} + W\left[ {\alpha + {1 \over 2}} \right] = W(1 + \alpha )$$

$${R_2} = {W \over 2}$$

Hence, option (a) is correct.

From Eqs. (i) and (iii), we get

$${R_1} = W\left[ {\alpha + {1 \over 2}} \right]$$

$$\alpha$$ = 0.5, R1 = W

Hence, option (b) is correct.

From Eq. (iii), if $$\alpha$$ = 0.5

$$T = \sqrt 2 W$$

$${T_{\max }} = 2\sqrt 2 W$$

For rope to break,

$$T > 2\sqrt 2 W$$

$$\sqrt 2 W\left[ {\alpha + {1 \over 2}} \right] > 2\sqrt 2 W \Rightarrow \alpha > {3 \over 2}$$

Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2021 Paper 2 Online
questions.question:
A pendulum consists of a bob of mass m = 0.1 kg and a massless inextensible string of length L = 1.0 m. It is suspended from a fixed point at height H = 0.9 m above a frictionless horizontal floor. Initially, the bob of the pendulum is lying on the floor at rest vertically below the point of suspension. A horizontal impulse P = 0.2 kg-m/s is imparted to the bob at some instant. After the bob slides for some distance, the string becomes taut and the bob lifts off the floor. The magnitude of the angular momentum of the pendulum about the point of suspension just before the bob lifts off is J kg-m2/s. The kinetic energy of the pendulum just after the lift-off is K Joules.

The value of K is ___________.
questions.solution:
Angular momentum, L = P $$\times$$ 0.9 = 0.18 kgm2/s


Just after string becomes taut; there will be no velocity along the string.

$$\therefore$$ $${V_ \bot } = {{P\cos \theta } \over m} = {{0.2 \times 0.9} \over {1 \times 0.1}} = 1.8$$ m/s

$$\therefore$$ $$K = {1 \over 2}mv_ \bot ^2 = {1 \over 2} \times 0.1 \times {1.8^2} = 0.162$$ J

questions.title: BITSAT 2021
questions.question: $$\int {{1 \over {1 - 2\sin x}}dx} $$ is equal to
questions.solution:
Let $$I = \int {{1 \over {1 - 2\sin x}}dx} $$

On putting $$\sin x = {{2\tan {x \over 2}} \over {1 + {{\tan }^2}{x \over 2}}}$$, we get

$$I = \int {{1 \over {1 - {{4\tan {x \over 2}} \over {1 + {{\tan }^2}{x \over 2}}}}}dx = \int {{{1 + {{\tan }^2}{x \over 2}} \over {1 + {{\tan }^2}{x \over 2} - 4\tan {x \over 2}}}dx} } $$

$$ = \int {{{{{\sec }^2}{x \over 2}} \over {1 + {{\tan }^2}{x \over 2} - 4\tan {x \over 2}}}dx} $$

$$ = 2\int {{1 \over {{{\left( {\tan {x \over 2} - 2} \right)}^2} - {{(\sqrt 3 )}^2}}}d\left( {\tan {x \over 2}} \right)} $$

$$ = 2\int {{{dt} \over {{{(t - 2)}^2} - {{(\sqrt 3 )}^2}}}} $$, where $$t = \tan {x \over 2}$$

$$ = 2\,.\,{1 \over {2\sqrt 3 }}\log \left| {{{t - 2 - \sqrt 3 } \over {t - 2 + \sqrt 3 }}} \right| + c = {1 \over {\sqrt 3 }}\log \left| {{{\tan {x \over 2} - 2 - \sqrt 3 } \over {\tan {x \over 2} - 2 + \sqrt 3 }}} \right| + c$$

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14 November 2021
questions.question: What is the integral of f(x) = 1 + x<sup>2</sup> + x<sup>4</sup> with respect to x<sup>2</sup>?
questions.solution:
To solve the integral

$$\int \left(1 + x^2 + x^4\right) \, d(x^2),$$

follow these steps:

Substitute:

Let

$$u = x^2.$$

Then, the differential is

$$d(x^2) = du.$$

Rewrite the Integrand:

Notice that:

$x^2 = u$

$x^4 = (x^2)^2 = u^2$

Thus, the function becomes:

$$1 + x^2 + x^4 = 1 + u + u^2.$$

Set Up the Integral:

The integral now is:

$$\int (1 + u + u^2) \, du.$$

Integrate Term by Term:

$\int 1 \, du = u$

$\int u \, du = \frac{u^2}{2}$

$\int u^2 \, du = \frac{u^3}{3}$

So, combining these results we have:

$$u + \frac{u^2}{2} + \frac{u^3}{3} + C.$$

Substitute Back $u = x^2$:

$$x^2 + \frac{(x^2)^2}{2} + \frac{(x^2)^3}{3} + C = x^2 + \frac{x^4}{2} + \frac{x^6}{3} + C.$$

Thus, the integral of $f(x) = 1 + x^2 + x^4$ with respect to $x^2$ is:

$$x^2 + \frac{x^4}{2} + \frac{x^6}{3} + C.$$

This corresponds to Option D.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14 November 2021
questions.question: If $\rm \int \sqrt{1 - sin 2x} \space dx$ = A sinx + B cosx + C, where 0 < x < $\frac{\pi}{4}$, then which one of the following is correct?
questions.solution:
Concept:

The fundamental trigonometric identity is:

$ \sin^2 x + \cos^2 x = 1 $

The integrals of sine and cosine functions are:

$ \int \sin x \, dx = -\cos x $

$ \int \cos x \, dx = \sin x $

Calculation:

We need to evaluate $\int \sqrt{1 - \sin 2x} \, dx$. We assume it is expressed as $A \sin x + B \cos x + C$.

Thus, we have:

$ \int \sqrt{\sin^2 x + \cos^2 x - 2 \sin x \cos x} \, dx = A \sin x + B \cos x + C $

This simplifies to:

$ \int \sqrt{(\sin x - \cos x)^2} \, dx = A \sin x + B \cos x + C $

Further reducing gives:

$ \int |\sin x - \cos x| \, dx = A \sin x + B \cos x + C \quad \text{----(i)} $

For $0 < x < \frac{\pi}{4}$, we know $\sin x < \cos x$.

Thus:

$ |\sin x - \cos x| = -\sin x + \cos x \quad \text{----(ii)} $

From (i) and (ii), we derive:

$ \int (-\sin x + \cos x) \, dx = A \sin x + B \cos x + C $

This evaluates to:

$ \cos x + \sin x + C = A \sin x + B \cos x + C $

By comparing coefficients, we determine $A = 1$, $B = 1$, and $C = 0$.

Therefore, the equation $A + B - 2 = 0$ is correct.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14 November 2021
questions.question: What is $\rm \int \frac{dx}{x(x^2 + 1)}$ equal to?
questions.solution:
Basic Integral Formula:

$ \int \frac{1}{x} \, dx = \ln |x| + C $

Logarithmic Property:

$ \log_a x - \log_a y = \log_a \left(\frac{x}{y}\right) $

Calculation

To evaluate the integral:

$ \int \frac{dx}{x(x^2 + 1)} $

Split the integral as follows:

$ \int \frac{dx}{x(x^2 + 1)} = \int \frac{dx}{x} - \int \frac{xdx}{x^2 + 1} $

For the second part, apply substitution and recognize the integral of the derivative:

$ \int \frac{xdx}{x^2 + 1} = \frac{1}{2} \int \frac{2xdx}{x^2 + 1} $

This results in:

$ \frac{1}{2} \ln |x^2 + 1| $

Combining these results, we get:

$ \ln |x| - \frac{1}{2} \ln |x^2 + 1| + C $

Using the logarithmic property:

$ \frac{1}{2} \ln |x^2| - \frac{1}{2} \ln |x^2 + 1| + C = \frac{1}{2} \ln \left(\frac{x^2}{x^2 + 1}\right) + C $

Thus, the evaluated integral is:

$ \int \frac{dx}{x(x^2 + 1)} = \frac{1}{2} \ln \left(\frac{x^2}{x^2 + 1}\right) + C $

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 18 April 2021
questions.question: What is $\rm \int e^{\left(2\ln x+\ln x^2\right)}dx$ equal to?
questions.solution:
Concept

$ e^{\ln x} = x $.

$\int x^n \, dx = \frac{x^{n+1}}{n+1} + c$.

Calculation

Let $ I = \int e^{\left(2\ln x + \ln x^2\right)} \, dx $.

$ I = \int e^{\left(\ln x^2 + \ln x^2\right)} \, dx $

$ I = \int e^{2\ln x^2} \, dx $

$ I = \int e^{\ln x^4} \, dx $

Using the property $ e^{\ln a} = a $, we have:

$ I = \int x^4 \, dx $

Therefore, the integral becomes:

$ \int x^4 \, dx = \frac{x^5}{5} + c $

Hence, the value of the integral $\int e^{\left(2\ln x+\ln x^2\right)} \, dx$ is $\frac{x^5}{5} + c$.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 18 April 2021
questions.question: What is $\rm \int \frac{dx}{\sec x+\tan x}$ equal to?
options: ln (sec x) + ln |sec x + tan x| + c
ln (sec x) - ln |sec x + tan x| + c
sec x tan x - ln |sec x - tan x| + c
ln |sec x + tan x| - ln |sec x| + c
questions.solution:
To evaluate the integral

$$ \int \frac{dx}{\sec x + \tan x} $$

we proceed by multiplying the numerator and the denominator by $ \sec x - \tan x $:

$$ \text{Let } I = \int \frac{dx}{\sec x + \tan x}. $$

Multiply by a convenient form of 1:

$$ I = \int \frac{(\sec x - \tan x)\,dx}{(\sec x + \tan x)(\sec x - \tan x)}. $$

The denominator simplifies using the difference of squares:

$$ (\sec x + \tan x)(\sec x - \tan x) = \sec^2 x - \tan^2 x. $$

Using the identity $ \sec^2 x - \tan^2 x = 1 $:

$$ I = \int (\sec x - \tan x)\, dx. $$

This integral can now be split into two separate integrals:

$$ I = \int \sec x \, dx - \int \tan x \, dx. $$

The antiderivatives are known:

For $ \int \sec x \, dx $, a common result is:

$$ \int \sec x \, dx = \ln |\sec x + \tan x| + C_1. $$

For $ \int \tan x \, dx $:

$$ \int \tan x \, dx = \ln |\sec x| + C_2. $$

Combining these, we have:

$$ I = \ln |\sec x + \tan x| - \ln |\sec x| + C, $$

where $ C = C_1 - C_2 $ is a constant of integration.

Therefore, the integral is expressed as:

Option D:

$$ \ln |\sec x + \tan x| - \ln |\sec x| + C. $$

This matches the provided choice and is the correct simplification for the given integral.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 18 April 2021
questions.question: What is $\int \dfrac{dx}{sec^2({tan}^{-1}x)}$ equal to?
questions.solution:
To solve the integral

$$ \int \dfrac{dx}{\sec^2(\tan^{-1} x)} $$

first, recall the identity for the trigonometric function $\sec^2 \theta = 1 + \tan^2 \theta$. Applying this identity to the given integral, where $\theta = \tan^{-1} x$, leads to:

$$ \sec^2(\tan^{-1} x) = 1 + \tan^2(\tan^{-1} x) = 1 + x^2 $$

Thus, the integral becomes:

$$ \int \dfrac{dx}{1 + x^2} $$

Recognize this as the standard form for the integral that results in the inverse tangent function:

$$ \int \dfrac{dx}{1 + x^2} = \tan^{-1} x + C $$

Hence, the correct result for the given integral is:

$$ \tan^{-1} x + C $$

The correct option is Option B:

tan-1 x + c

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14 November 2021
questions.question: Let sin-1x + sin-1y + sin-1z = $\frac{3\pi}{2}$ for 0 ≤ x, y z ≤ 1. What is the value of x1000 + y1001 + z1002?
questions.solution:
We are given:

$$\sin^{-1}x + \sin^{-1}y + \sin^{-1}z = \frac{3\pi}{2}$$

with the constraint that $$0 \le x, y, z \le 1.$$

Here’s how to find $$x, y, z$$ and then evaluate $$x^{1000} + y^{1001} + z^{1002}:$$

Since the range of $$\sin^{-1}t$$ for $$t \in [0,1]$$ is $$[0, \frac{\pi}{2}],$$ the maximum value each inverse sine can take is $$\frac{\pi}{2}.$$

To achieve a sum of $$\frac{3\pi}{2},$$ each term must equal $$\frac{\pi}{2}.$$ That is:

$$\sin^{-1} x = \sin^{-1} y = \sin^{-1} z = \frac{\pi}{2}.$$

Taking the sine of both sides gives:

$$x = \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right) = 1,$$

$$y = 1,$$

$$z = 1.$$

Now evaluate:

$$x^{1000} + y^{1001} + z^{1002} = 1^{1000} + 1^{1001} + 1^{1002} = 1 + 1 + 1 = 3.$$

Thus, the correct answer is Option C: 3.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14 November 2021
questions.question:
What is the value of following?

$\rm cot \left[sin^{-1} \frac{3}{5}+cot^{-1}\frac{3}{2} \right]$
questions.solution:
Define the angles:

Let $$A = \sin^{-1}\frac{3}{5}.$$

Then, $$\sin A = \frac{3}{5}.$$

To find $$\cos A,$$ use the Pythagorean identity:

$$\cos A = \sqrt{1-\sin^2 A} = \sqrt{1-\left(\frac{3}{5}\right)^2} = \sqrt{1-\frac{9}{25}} = \sqrt{\frac{16}{25}} = \frac{4}{5}.$$

Thus, the cotangent of $$A$$ is:

$$\cot A = \frac{\cos A}{\sin A} = \frac{\frac{4}{5}}{\frac{3}{5}} = \frac{4}{3}.$$

Let $$B = \cot^{-1}\frac{3}{2}.$$

This directly gives:

$$\cot B = \frac{3}{2}.$$

Use the cotangent addition formula:

The formula for the cotangent of a sum is:

$$\cot(A+B) = \frac{\cot A \cot B - 1}{\cot A + \cot B}.$$

Substitute the values computed:

Substitute $$\cot A = \frac{4}{3}$$ and $$\cot B = \frac{3}{2}:$$

$$\cot(A+B) = \frac{\left(\frac{4}{3}\right)\left(\frac{3}{2}\right) - 1}{\frac{4}{3} + \frac{3}{2}}.$$

Simplify the numerator:

$$\left(\frac{4}{3}\right)\left(\frac{3}{2}\right) = \frac{12}{6} = 2,$$

so the numerator becomes:

$$2 - 1 = 1.$$

Simplify the denominator by finding a common denominator:

$$\frac{4}{3} + \frac{3}{2} = \frac{8}{6} + \frac{9}{6} = \frac{17}{6}.$$

Now, the expression becomes:

$$\cot(A+B) = \frac{1}{\frac{17}{6}} = \frac{6}{17}.$$

Conclude the answer:

The computed value is:

$$\cot\left[\sin^{-1}\frac{3}{5} + \cot^{-1}\frac{3}{2}\right] = \frac{6}{17}.$$

Thus, the correct answer is Option A: $$\frac{6}{17}.$$

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14 November 2021
questions.question: What is the slope of the tangent of y = cos-1 (cos x) at x = $-\frac{\pi}{4}$?
questions.solution:
We want to find the slope of the tangent line (i.e., the derivative) of

$$y = \cos^{-1} (\cos x)$$

at

$$x = -\frac{\pi}{4}.$$

Here’s a step-by-step explanation:

Recognize that the inverse cosine function, $$\cos^{-1}(z),$$ returns values in the range $$[0, \pi].$$ This means that even though $$\cos x$$ is periodic, $$\cos^{-1}(\cos x)$$ is defined piecewise so that its values always lie between 0 and $$\pi.$$

For $$x$$ values in the interval $$[-\pi, 0],$$ the corresponding angle in $$[0, \pi]$$ with the same cosine is $$-x.$$ That is,

$$\cos^{-1} (\cos x) = -x \quad \text{for } x \in [-\pi, 0].$$

Since $$-\frac{\pi}{4} \in [-\pi, 0],$$ we have:

$$y = \cos^{-1} (\cos x) = -x.$$

Now, differentiate $$y = -x$$ with respect to $$x:$$

$$\frac{dy}{dx} = -1.$$

Thus, the slope of the tangent line at $$x = -\frac{\pi}{4}$$ is $$-1.$$

Alternatively, you can differentiate using the chain rule:

Start with the derivative of $$\cos^{-1} u$$, which is

$$\frac{d}{du} \cos^{-1}(u) = -\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-u^2}}.$$

Let $$u = \cos x.$$ Then $$\frac{du}{dx} = -\sin x.$$ So, by the chain rule:

$$\frac{dy}{dx} = \frac{d}{dx} \cos^{-1} (\cos x) = -\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-\cos^2 x}} \cdot (-\sin x) = \frac{\sin x}{\sqrt{1-\cos^2 x}}.$$

Notice that $$\sqrt{1-\cos^2 x} = |\sin x|.$$ Thus,

$$\frac{dy}{dx} = \frac{\sin x}{|\sin x|}.$$

At $$x = -\frac{\pi}{4},$$ we have

$$\sin\left(-\frac{\pi}{4}\right) = -\frac{\sqrt{2}}{2},$$

so

$$\frac{dy}{dx} = \frac{-\frac{\sqrt{2}}{2}}{\frac{\sqrt{2}}{2}} = -1.$$

Both methods confirm that the slope at $$x = -\frac{\pi}{4}$$ is $$-1.$$

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option A: $$-1$$.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 18 April 2021
questions.question: $\rm tan^{-1}x+cot^{-1}x=\frac{\pi}{2}$ holds, when
questions.solution:
Concept:

The sum of two inverse tangent functions, $\tan^{-1}(x) + \tan^{-1}(y)$, can be expressed as:

$ \tan^{-1}\left(\frac{x+y}{1-xy}\right) $

Also, the inverse cotangent function can be related to the inverse tangent function as:

$ \cot^{-1}(x) = \tan^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{x}\right) $

Calculation:

Given that:

$ \tan^{-1}(x) + \cot^{-1}(x) = \frac{\pi}{2} $

Substitute $\cot^{-1}(x)$ with $\tan^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{x}\right)$:

$ \tan^{-1}(x) + \tan^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{x}\right) = \frac{\pi}{2} $

Using the formula for the sum of inverse tangents:

$ \tan^{-1}\left(\frac{x + \frac{1}{x}}{1 - x \cdot \frac{1}{x}}\right) = \frac{\pi}{2} $

Simplifying the expression inside the inverse tangent:

$ \tan^{-1}\left(\frac{x + \frac{1}{x}}{0}\right) = \frac{\pi}{2} $

This implies:

$ \tan^{-1}(\infty) = \frac{\pi}{2} $

Thus, this condition holds true for all $x \in \mathbb{R}$.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 18 April 2021
questions.question: The equation $sin^{-1}x-cos^{-1}x=\frac{\pi}{6}$ has
questions.solution:
Concept:

The identity for inverse trigonometric functions is:

$ \sin^{-1}x + \cos^{-1}x = \frac{\pi}{2} $

Also, the cosine of 30 degrees is:

$ \cos(30^\circ) = \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} $

Calculation:

Given:

$ \sin^{-1}x - \cos^{-1}x = \frac{\pi}{6} $

Using the identity, we can substitute:

$ \sin^{-1}x + \cos^{-1}x - 2\cos^{-1}x = \frac{\pi}{6} $

Thus:

$ \frac{\pi}{2} - 2\cos^{-1}x = \frac{\pi}{6} $

Solving for $\cos^{-1}x$:

$ 2\cos^{-1}x = \frac{\pi}{2} - \frac{\pi}{6} $

$ \cos^{-1}x = \frac{\pi}{6} $

This implies:

$ x = \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} $

Conclusion:

∴ The equation has a unique solution.

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2021 Paper 1 Online
questions.question: The correct statement(s) related to the metal extraction processes is (are) :
options: A mixture of PbS and PbO undergoes self-reduction to produce Pb and SO<sub>2</sub>
In the extraction process of copper from copper pyrites, silica is added to produce copper silicate.
Partial oxidation of sulphide ore of copper by roasting, followed by self-reduction produces blister copper.
In cyanide process, zinc powder is utilized to precipitate gold from Na[Au(CN)<sub>2</sub>]
questions.solution:
Statements (a), (c) and (d) are correct, whereas statement (b) is incorrect. All statements are explained as follows :

(a) A mixture PbO and PbS reacts as follows:




This reaction is known as self-reduction.

(b) Silica is added to remove impurities of Fe in the form of slag, FeSiO3 .

(c) CuFeS2 are in partially oxidised first by roasting and then self-reduction of Cu takes place to produce blister copper.

questions.title: BITSAT 2021
questions.question: The initial speed of a body of mass 2.0 kg is 5 m/s. A force acts for 4 s in the direction of motion of the body, as shown in force-time graph. The impulse of force is
questions.solution:
Impulse of a force = Area between the force-time graph

Hence, impulse of force = area of $$\Delta$$OA'A + area of rectangle A'B'BA + area of trapezium BB'C'C + area of rectangle CC'ED

$$ = {1 \over 2} \times 1.5 \times 3 + 1 \times 3 + {1 \over 2}(3 + 2)(3 - 2.5) + 2 \times 1$$

$$ = 2.25 + 3 + 1.25 + 2$$

$$ = 8.50$$ Ns

questions.title: BITSAT 2021
questions.question:
A uniform rod AB of length L = 1 m is sliding along two mutually perpendicular surfaces OP and OQ as shown in the figure.

When the rod subtends an angle $$\theta$$ = 30$$^\circ$$ with OQ, the end B has a velocity $$\sqrt3$$ m/s. The velocity of end A at that time is
questions.solution:
According to the question,

Let OB = x, OA = y and x = L cos$$\theta$$

So, by Pythagoras theorem,

$$\Rightarrow$$ x2 + y2 = L2

Differentiating w.r.t. 't', we get

$$2x{{dx} \over {dt}} + 2y{{dy} \over {dt}} = 0$$

$$2x{v_B} + 2y{v_A} = 0$$ [$$\because$$ $${{dx} \over {dt}} = {v_B}$$ and $${{dy} \over {dt}} = {v_A}$$]

$$ \Rightarrow {v_A} = - {x \over y}{v_B}$$

$$ \Rightarrow \left| {{v_A}} \right| = {x \over y}{v_B} = {v_B}\cot \theta $$ [$$\because$$ $${x \over y} = \cot \theta $$]

$$ = \sqrt 3 \times \cot 30^\circ $$

$$ = \sqrt 3 \times {{\sqrt 3 }}$$ = 3 m/s

questions.title: BITSAT 2021
questions.question: A block of mass 10 kg rests on a rough inclined plane making an angle of 30$$^\circ$$ with the horizontal. The coefficient of static friction between the block and the plane is 0.1. The friction force on the block is
questions.solution:
To find the friction force on the block, we first need to analyze the forces acting on the block on an inclined plane.

The forces acting on the block include:

  • Gravitational force (weight) acting vertically downward, $$mg$$
  • Normal force, $$N$$, exerted by the plane perpendicular to its surface
  • Frictional force, which acts parallel to the inclined surface and opposite to the direction of possible motion

The gravitational force can be resolved into two components:

  • Perpendicular to the inclined plane: $$mg \cos \theta$$
  • Parallel to the inclined plane: $$mg \sin \theta$$

In this problem:

  • Mass of the block, $$m$$ = 10 kg
  • Angle of the incline, $$\theta$$ = 30$$^\circ$$
  • Coefficient of static friction, $$\mu_s$$ = 0.1

First, we calculate the perpendicular component of the gravitational force to find the normal force:

$$N = mg \cos \theta$$

Substituting the values:

$$N = (10 \, \text{kg}) (9.8 \, \text{m/s}^2) \cos 30^\circ$$

$$\cos 30^\circ = \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}$$

$$N = 10 \times 9.8 \times \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}$$

$$N = 49 \sqrt{3} \, \text{N}$$

Next, we find the maximum static friction force:

$$f_{\text{max}} = \mu_s N$$

$$f_{\text{max}} = 0.1 \times 49 \sqrt{3} \, \text{N}$$

$$f_{\text{max}} = 4.9 \sqrt{3} \, \text{N}$$

We now compare this with the component of the gravitational force parallel to the inclined plane:

$$mg \sin \theta$$

Substituting the values:

$$mg \sin 30^\circ = 10 \times 9.8 \times \frac{1}{2}$$

$$mg \sin 30^\circ = 49 \, \text{N}$$

The friction force will be equal to the component of the gravitational force parallel to the inclined plane but within the limit of the maximum static friction force. Here, we recognize that the maximum static friction force (4.9 $$\sqrt{3}$$ N) is indeed greater than 4.9 N (the component of the gravitational force parallel to the incline). Hence, the actual friction force counteracting the motion will be:

$$49 \, \text{N}$$

Therefore, the correct answer is Option C:

Option C: 49 N

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2021 Paper 1 Online
questions.question:
A projectile is thrown from a point O on the ground at an angle 45$$^\circ$$ from the vertical and with a speed 5$$\sqrt 2 $$ m/s. The projectile at the highest point of its trajectory splits into two equal parts. One part falls vertically down to the ground, 0.5 s after the splitting. The other part, t seconds after splitting, falls to the ground at a distance x meters from the point O. The acceleration due to gravity g = 10 m/s2.

The value of t is _____________.
questions.solution:
Range, $$R = {u_x} \times T = {{2{u_x}{u_y}} \over g} = {{2 \times 5 \times 5} \over {10}} = 5$$ m

Time of flight, $$T = {{2{u_y}} \over g} = {{2 \times 5} \over {10}} = 1$$ s


Both particle have no vertical velocity after splitting so both will take same time to reach the ground.

$$\because$$ Time of motion of one part falling vertically downwards is 0.5 s

$$\Rightarrow$$ Time of motion of another part, $$t = 0.5$$ s

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2021 Paper 1 Online
questions.question:
A projectile is thrown from a point O on the ground at an angle 45$$^\circ$$ from the vertical and with a speed 5$$\sqrt 2 $$ m/s. The projectile at the highest point of its trajectory splits into two equal parts. One part falls vertically down to the ground, 0.5 s after the splitting. The other part, t seconds after splitting, falls to the ground at a distance x meters from the point O. The acceleration due to gravity g = 10 m/s2.

The value of x is _______________.
questions.solution:
Range, $$R = {u_x} \times T = {{2{u_x}{u_y}} \over g} = {{2 \times 5 \times 5} \over {10}} = 5$$ m

Time of flight, $$T = {{2{u_y}} \over g} = {{2 \times 5} \over {10}} = 1$$ s


Both particle have no vertical velocity after splitting so both will take same time to reach the ground.

$$\because$$ Time of motion of one part falling vertically downwards is 0.5 s

$$\Rightarrow$$ Time of motion of another part, $$t = 0.5$$ s

From momentum conservation, pi = pf

2m $$\times$$ 5 = m $$\times$$ v

v = 10 m/s

Displacement of other part in 0.5 s in horizontal direction,

$$ = v\left( {{T \over 2}} \right) = 10 \times 0.5 = 5$$ m = R

$$\therefore$$ Total distance of second part from point O is $$x = {{3R} \over 2} = 3 \times {5 \over 2}$$

$$ \Rightarrow $$ x = 7.5 m

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14 November 2021
questions.question: What is $\rm \displaystyle\lim_{n \rightarrow \infty} \frac{a^n+b^n}{a^n-b^n}$ where a > b > 1, equal to?
questions.solution:
Given:

We need to evaluate the limit:

$ \lim\limits_{n \rightarrow \infty} \frac{a^n + b^n}{a^n - b^n} $

where $ a > b > 1 $.

Calculation:

Given that $ a > b > 1 $, it follows that:

$ \frac{a}{b} > 1 \quad \text{or} \quad \frac{b}{a} < 1 $

Let's analyze the expression:

$ \lim\limits_{n \rightarrow \infty} \frac{a^n + b^n}{a^n - b^n} $

We can factor out $ a^n $ from both the numerator and the denominator:

$ = \lim\limits_{n \rightarrow \infty} \frac{a^n[1+(\frac{b}{a})^n]}{a^n[1 - (\frac{b}{a})^n]} $

Canceling $ a^n $ from the numerator and denominator gives:

$ = \lim\limits_{n \rightarrow \infty} \frac{1+(\frac{b}{a})^n}{1 - (\frac{b}{a})^n} $

Since $\frac{b}{a} < 1$, as $ n $ approaches infinity, $(\frac{b}{a})^n$ approaches 0. Therefore, the limit becomes:

$ = \lim\limits_{n \rightarrow \infty} \frac{1 + 0}{1 - 0} $

Thus, we have:

$ = \frac{1}{1} = 1 $

Conclusion:

Therefore, the limit is:

$ f(x) = 1 $

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14 November 2021
questions.question:
Let $\rm f(x) = \left\{\begin{matrix} 1+\frac{x}{2k}, & 0 < x < 2\\\ kx, & 2 \le x < 4 \end {matrix}\right.$

If $\displaystyle\lim_{x\rightarrow 2}$ f(x) exists, then what is the value of k?
questions.solution:
Concept:

Step 1: Check if $ f(a) $ is defined. If it is not, the function is not continuous at $ a $, and no further steps are needed. If $ f(a) $ is defined, proceed to the next step.

Step 2: Check the Left-hand limit (LHL) and Right-hand limit (RHL).

$ \text{LHL} = \lim\limits_{x \rightarrow a^{-}} f(x) $

$ \text{RHL} = \lim\limits_{x \rightarrow a^{+}} f(x) $

If LHL equals RHL, then the limit exists.

Calculation:

Calculate the left-hand limit as $ x $ approaches 2 from the left for $ f(x) = 1 + \frac{x}{2k} $:

$ \lim\limits_{x\rightarrow 2^{-}} f(x) = 1 + \frac{2}{2k} = 1 + \frac{1}{k} $

This gives us the equation:

$ \lim\limits_{x\rightarrow 2^{-}} f(x) = 1 + \frac{1}{k} \quad \text{(Equation 1)} $

Calculate the right-hand limit as $ x $ approaches 2 from the right for $ f(x) = kx $:

$ \lim\limits_{x\rightarrow 2^{+}} f(x) = 2k \quad \text{(Equation 2)} $

If the limit $\lim\limits_{x \rightarrow 2} f(x)$ exists, then:

$ 1 + \frac{1}{k} = 2k $

Solving the equation:

$ 2k^2 - k - 1 = 0 $

Factorize:

$ 2k^2 - 2k + k - 1 = 0 $

$ 2k(k - 1) + 1(k - 1) = 0 $

$ (k - 1)(2k + 1) = 0 $

Thus, $ k = 1 $ or $ k = -\frac{1}{2} $.

Since we have only one consistent solution here due to positive domain constraints:

∴ The value of $ k $ is 1.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14 November 2021
questions.question:
Consider the following statements in respect of f(x) = |x| - 1

1. f(x) is continuous at x = 1.

2. f(x) is differentiable at x = 0.

Which of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.solution:
Let's analyze the function:

$$ f(x) = |x| - 1 = \begin{cases} x - 1 & \text{if } x \ge 0, \\ -x - 1 & \text{if } x < 0. \end{cases} $$

Continuity at $ x = 1 $:

For $ x \ge 0 $, $ f(x) = x - 1 $ is a linear function, which is continuous everywhere.

Therefore, $ f(x) $ is continuous at $ x = 1 $.

Differentiability at $ x = 0 $:

For $ x > 0 $:

$ f(x) = x - 1 $ so the derivative is $ f'(x) = 1 $.

For $ x < 0 $:

$ f(x) = -x - 1 $ so the derivative is $ f'(x) = -1 $.

At $ x = 0 $, the right-hand derivative is $ 1 $ and the left-hand derivative is $-1$. Since these two values are not equal, $ f(x) $ is not differentiable at $ x = 0 $.

Conclusion:

Statement 1 is true.

Statement 2 is false.

Thus, the correct option is:

Option A

1 only

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14 November 2021
questions.question: If $f(x) = \frac{[x]}{|x|}$, x ≠ 0, where [⋅] denotes the greatest integer function, then what is the right-hand limit of f(x) at x = 1?
options: Right-hand limit of f(x) at x = 1 does not exist.
questions.solution:
Concepts to Understand:

Modulus Function:

The modulus function is defined as $ f(x) = |x| $.

It is expressed as:

$ f(x) = \begin{cases} x & \text{if } x > 0 \\ -x & \text{if } x < 0 \end{cases} $

Greatest Integer Function:

This function, denoted as $[x]$, returns the greatest integer less than or equal to $ x $. It is also known as the floor function.

Typically, if $ n \leq x < n+1 $, then $[x] = n$.

Continuity and Differentiability:

A function $ f(x) $ is continuous at $ x = a $ if:

$ \lim\limits_{x \to a^{-}} f(x) = \lim\limits_{x \to a^{+}} f(x) = f(a) $

Differentiability at a point $ x = a $ requires the left-hand derivative (LHD) to be equal to the right-hand derivative (RHD):

$ \text{LHD} = \lim \limits_{h \to 0^{-}}\frac{f(a - h) - f(a)}{-h}, \quad \text{RHD} = \lim \limits_{h \to 0^{+}}\frac{f(a + h) - f(a)}{h} $

Calculation:

Given the function:

$ f(x) = \frac{[x]}{|x|} $

This simplifies under different conditions as follows:

$ f(x) = \begin{cases} \frac{-1}{-x} = \frac{1}{x} & \text{if } -1 < x < 0 \\ \frac{0}{x} = 0 & \text{if } 0 < x < 1 \\ \frac{1}{x} = \frac{1}{x} & \text{if } 1 < x < 2 \end{cases} $

Right-Hand Limit at $ x = 1 $:

For the interval $ 0 < x < 1$, $ f(x) = 0 $.

For the interval $ 1 < x < 2$, $ f(x) = \frac{1}{x} $.

Calculate the right-hand limit at $ x = 1 $:

$ \text{RHL} = \lim\limits_{x \to 1^+} f(x) = \lim\limits_{x \to 1^+} \frac{1}{x} = 1 $

Conclusion:

Therefore, the right-hand limit of $ f(x) $ at $ x = 1 $ is 1.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14 November 2021
questions.question:
Consider the following statements in respect of the function $\rm f(x) = sin \left(\frac{1}{x^2}\right)$, x ≠ 0:

1. It is continuous at x = 0, if f(0) = 0.

2. It is continuous at $x = \frac{2}{\sqrt{\pi}}$.

Which of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.solution:
Given the function:

$ f(x) = \sin\left(\frac{1}{x^2}\right) $

Statement 1:

To determine if the function is continuous at $ x = 0 $ when $ f(0) = 0 $:

Left Hand Limit (LHL):

$ LHL = \lim\limits_{x \to 0^-} \sin\left(\frac{1}{x^2}\right) $

Calculating:

$ LHL = \lim\limits_{h \to 0} \sin\left[\frac{1}{(0-h)^2}\right] = \sin(\infty) $

The value of $\sin(\infty)$ is indefinite, but it takes values within the range [-1, 1].

Right Hand Limit (RHL):

$ RHL = \lim\limits_{x \to 0^+} \sin\left(\frac{1}{x^2}\right) $

Calculating:

$ RHL = \lim\limits_{h \to 0} \sin\left[\frac{1}{(0+h)^2}\right] = \sin(\infty) $

Since $ f(0) = 0 $, from the limits calculated, $ \sin(\infty) $ is not equal to $ 0 $. Thus, LHL and RHL do not equate to $ f(0) $.

Conclusion for Statement 1: Incorrect.

Statement 2:

To check continuity at $ x = \frac{2}{\sqrt{\pi}} $:

Left Hand Limit (LHL):

$ LHL = \lim\limits_{h \to 0} \sin\left[\frac{1}{\left(\frac{2}{\sqrt{\pi}} - h\right)^2}\right] = \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right) $

Therefore:

$ LHL = \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} $

Right Hand Limit (RHL):

Similarly:

$ RHL = \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} $

Function Value:

$ f\left(\frac{2}{\sqrt{\pi}}\right) = \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right) = \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} $

Since LHL = RHL = $ f\left( \frac{2}{\sqrt{\pi}} \right) $.

Conclusion for Statement 2: Correct.

Therefore, only the second statement is correct, indicating that the function is continuous at $ x = \frac{2}{\sqrt{\pi}} $.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 18 April 2021
questions.question: If a differentiable function f(x) satisfies $\mathop {\lim }\limits_{x \to - 1} \dfrac{f(x)+1}{x^2-1}=-\dfrac{3}{2}$ then what is $\mathop {\lim }\limits_{x \to - 1} f(x)$ equal to?
questions.solution:
To solve this problem, we're given that:

$ \lim\limits_{x \to -1} \frac{f(x) + 1}{x^2 - 1} = -\frac{3}{2} $

Since the denominator $x^2 - 1$ becomes zero as $x$ approaches $-1$ (because $(-1)^2 - 1 = 0$), the numerator $f(x) + 1$ must also approach zero for the limit to exist as a finite number.

This implies:

$ \lim\limits_{x \to -1} [f(x) + 1] = 0 $

Consequently, we can deduce:

$ \lim\limits_{x \to -1} f(x) + 1 = 0 $

Solving for $\lim\limits_{x \to -1} f(x)$, we subtract 1 from both sides:

$ \lim\limits_{x \to -1} f(x) = -1 $

Thus, the value of $\lim\limits_{x \to -1} f(x)$ is $-1$.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 18 April 2021
questions.question: If the function $\rm f\left( x \right) = \left\{ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {a + bx,\;\;}&{x < 1}\\ {5,}&{x = 1}\\ {b - ax,}&{x > 1} \end{array}} \right.$ is continuous, then what is the value of (a + b)?
questions.solution:
Concept:

To ensure the function $ f(x) $ is continuous, we need:

$ \text{LHL} = \text{RHL} = f(x) $

LHL (Left Hand Limit): This is calculated by $ \mathop {\lim }\limits_{\alpha \to 0} f(x - \alpha) $

RHL (Right Hand Limit): This is calculated by $ \mathop {\lim }\limits_{\alpha \to 0} f(x + \alpha) $

Calculation:

Since $ f(x) $ is given as a continuous function, the following holds:

$ \text{LHL} = f(x) = \text{RHL} $

For the function:

$ \mathop {\lim }\limits_{\alpha \to 0} f(1 - \alpha) = f(1) $

Substituting the expression for $ f(1 - \alpha) $:

$ \mathop {\lim }\limits_{\alpha \to 0} [a + b(1 - \alpha)] = 5 $

This simplifies to:

$ \mathop {\lim }\limits_{\alpha \to 0} [a + b - b\alpha] = 5 $

From the above, we derive:

$ a + b = 5 $

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 18 April 2021
questions.question: If $\rm\mathop {\lim }\limits_{x \to a} \frac{a^x -x^a}{x^x -a^a}= - 1$, then what is the value of a?
questions.solution:
Concept:

Indeterminate Forms: These are expressions whose values cannot be directly defined, such as $\frac{0}{0}$, $\pm \frac{\infty}{\infty}$, 00, and ∞0.

L'Hospital's Rule: For differentiable functions $f(x)$ and $g(x)$, if $\lim_{x \to c} \frac{f(x)}{g(x)}$ results in 0/0 or ±∞/∞, the limit can be evaluated as $\lim_{x \to c}\frac{f'(x)}{g'(x)}$, provided this new limit exists.

For the indeterminate form ∞ - ∞: First, rationalize by multiplying with the conjugate. Then, divide by the highest power of the variable so that $\frac{1}{x} \to 0$ as $x \to \infty$.

For the indeterminate form $\frac{0}{0}$: Try to rationalize by multiplying with the conjugate, simplify by canceling terms, or use L'Hospital's rule.

Calculation:

Evaluate $\lim\limits_{x \to a} \frac{a^x - x^a}{x^x - a^a}$.

This initially results in $\frac{a^a - a^a}{a^a - a^a} = \frac{0}{0}$, an indeterminate form.

Apply L'Hospital's rule:

$ \lim\limits_{x \to a} \frac{a^x - x^a}{x^x - a^a} = \lim\limits_{x \to a} \frac{a^x \log a - ax^{a-1}}{x^x(\log x + 1)} $

Substitute $x = a$:

$ = \frac{a^a \log a - a \cdot a^{a-1}}{a^a(\log a + 1)} $

Simplify:

$ = \frac{\log a - 1}{\log a + 1} $

Set equal to -1, as given:

$ \frac{\log a - 1}{\log a + 1} = -1 $

Solve for $a$:

$ \log a - 1 = - \log a - 1 \\ 2 \log a = 0 \\ a = 1 $

Thus, the value of $a$ is 1.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 18 April 2021
questions.question: What is $\rm\mathop {\lim }\limits_{x \to 2}\frac{x^3 + x^2}{x^2 + 3x + 2}$ equal to?
questions.solution:
To find the limit of the function as $ x \to 2 $:

$$ \lim\limits_{x \to 2} \frac{x^3 + x^2}{x^2 + 3x + 2} $$

First, consider the behavior of both the numerator $x^3 + x^2$ and the denominator $x^2 + 3x + 2$ as $x$ approaches 2.

Step 1: Simplify the denominator.

Factor the denominator:

$ x^2 + 3x + 2 = (x + 1)(x + 2) $

Step 2: Consider the numerator.

Simplify the numerator directly at $ x = 2 $:

$ x^3 + x^2 = x^2(x + 1) $

Now, the limit expression becomes:

$ \lim\limits_{x \to 2} \frac{x^2(x + 1)}{(x + 1)(x + 2)} $

Step 3: Cancel out common terms.

Notice that $x + 1$ is common in both the numerator and the denominator:

$ \lim\limits_{x \to 2} \frac{x^2}{x + 2} $

Step 4: Substitute $x = 2$ in the simplified expression.

$ \frac{2^2}{2 + 2} = \frac{4}{4} = 1 $

Thus, the limit is:

$ \boxed{1} $

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 18 April 2021
questions.question: If f(x) = e<sup>|x|</sup>, then which one of the following is correct?
questions.solution:
Given the function $ f(x) = e^{|x|} $, we need to find $ f'(0) $.

Step-by-step Derivation:

Understand the function:

The absolute value function $ |x| $ is defined as:

$ |x| = \begin{cases} x & \text{if } x \geq 0 \\ -x & \text{if } x < 0 \end{cases} $

Thus, the function $ f(x) $ can be rewritten as:

$ f(x) = \begin{cases} e^x & \text{if } x \geq 0 \\ e^{-x} & \text{if } x < 0 \end{cases} $

Differentiate each case:

For $ x > 0 $, use $ f(x) = e^x $:

$ f'(x) = \frac{d}{dx}(e^x) = e^x $

For $ x < 0 $, use $ f(x) = e^{-x} $:

$ f'(x) = \frac{d}{dx}(e^{-x}) = -e^{-x} $

Evaluate $ f'(x) $ at $ x = 0 $:

As $ x \rightarrow 0^+ $:

$ \lim_{x \to 0^+} f'(x) = \lim_{x \to 0^+} e^x = e^0 = 1 $

As $ x \rightarrow 0^- $:

$ \lim_{x \to 0^-} f'(x) = \lim_{x \to 0^-} -e^{-x} = -e^0 = -1 $

Conclusion on $ f'(0) $:

The limits from the left and right at $ x = 0 $ do not agree, i.e., $ 1 \neq -1 $.

Therefore, the derivative $ f'(0) $ does not exist.

Correct Option:

The correct answer is Option D: $ f'(0) $ does not exist.

questions.title: BITSAT 2021
questions.question: The value of $$\mathop {\lim }\limits_{x \to 0} {{\sqrt {1 - {{\cos x}^2}} } \over {1 - \cos x}}$$ is
questions.solution:
The required limit

$$ = \mathop {\lim }\limits_{x \to 0} {{\sqrt {(1 - \cos {x^2})(1 + \cos {x^2})} } \over {(1 - \cos x)\sqrt {1 + \cos {x^2}} }}$$

$$ = \mathop {\lim }\limits_{x \to 0} {{\left( {{{\sin {x^2}} \over {{x^2}}}} \right)} \over {2\left( {{{{{\sin }^2}x/2} \over {{x^2}}}} \right)}}.{1 \over {\sqrt {1 + \cos {x^2}} }} = {1 \over {2 \times {1 \over 4} \times \sqrt 2 }} = \sqrt 2 $$

questions.title: BITSAT 2021
questions.question: If $$f(x) = \left\{ {\matrix{ {a{x^2} + 1,} & {x \le 1} \cr {{x^2} + ax + b,} & {x > 1} \cr } } \right.$$ is differentiable at x = 1, then
questions.solution:
$$f'(1 - 0) = \mathop {\lim }\limits_{h \to 0} {{f(1 - h) - f(1)} \over { - h}}$$

$$ = \mathop {\lim }\limits_{h \to 0} {{a{{(1 - h)}^2} + 1 - (1 + a)} \over { - h}} = \mathop {\lim }\limits_{h \to 0} {{a({h^2} - 2h)} \over { - h}} = 2a$$

$$f'(1 + 0) = \mathop {\lim }\limits_{h \to 0} {{f(1 + h) - f(1)} \over h}$$

$$ = \mathop {\lim }\limits_{h \to 0} {{{{(1 + h)}^2} + a(1 + h) + b - (1 + a)} \over h}$$

$$ = \mathop {\lim }\limits_{h \to 0} {{{h^2} + 2h + ah + b} \over h} = 2 + a$$, if b = 0

Thus, $$2a = 2 + a,b = 0$$

$$ \Rightarrow a = 2,b = 0$$

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2021 Paper 1 Online
questions.question:
Let f : R $$\to$$ R be defined by $$f(x) = {{{x^2} - 3x - 6} \over {{x^2} + 2x + 4}}$$

Then which of the following statements is (are) TRUE?
options: f is decreasing in the interval ($$-$$2, $$-$$1)
f is increasing in the interval (1, 2)
questions.solution:

$$f(x) = {{{x^2} - 3x - 6} \over {{x^2} + 2x + 4}}$$ .... (i)

$$ \Rightarrow f'(x) = {{5x(x + 4)} \over {{{({x^2} + 2x + 4)}^2}}}$$


Sign scheme for f'(x)

Here, f is decreasing in the interval ($$-$$2, $$-$$1) and f is increasing in the interval (1, 2).

Now, $$f( - 4) = {{11} \over 6},f(0) = {{ - 3} \over 2}$$ [from Eq. (i)]

and $$\mathop {\lim }\limits_{x \to \pm \,\infty } f(x) = 1$$

$$\therefore$$ Range $$ = \left[ {{{ - 3} \over 2},{{11} \over 6}} \right]$$

Hence, f(x) is into.


f(x) has local maxima at x = $$-$$4

and local minima at x = 0.

questions.title: BITSAT 2021
questions.question: The relative lowering of vapour pressure of a dilute aqueous solution containing non-volatile solute is 0.0125. The molality of the solution is about
questions.solution:
According to Raoult's law, relative lowering of vapour pressure is equal to mole fraction of solute,

$$\therefore$$ $${{p - {p_s}} \over p} = {n \over {n + N}} = {w \over m}{{w/m} \over {{w \over m} + {W \over M}}} \Rightarrow {w \over m} < < < {W \over M}$$

$$\therefore$$ $${{p^\circ - {p_s}} \over {p^\circ }} = {{w/m} \over {{W \over M}}} = {w \over m} \times {M \over W}$$

$$0.0125 = {{w \times 18} \over {m \times W}} \Rightarrow {w \over {mW}} = {{0.0125} \over {18}}$$

$$ = {{Weight\,of\,solute\,(w) \times 1000} \over {Molar\,mass\,of\,solute\,(m) \times Weight\,of\,{H_2}O(W)}}$$

Now, molality $$m = {{0.0125} \over {18}} \times 1000 = 0.69 \approx 0.7$$
