questions.question:
Direction: Select the option that is nearest in meaning to the underlined word and mark your response in your answer sheet accordingly.

Her smile was contagious.
questions.solution:
The word "contagious" in this sentence means something that spreads easily or has an infectious quality. In the context of a smile, it suggests that her smile makes others smile too.

The option closest in meaning is:

Option D: catching.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
questions.question:
Direction: Select the option that is nearest in meaning to the underlined word and mark your response in your answer sheet accordingly.

Her dynamic nature impressed everyone.
questions.solution:
Let's break down the meaning of "dynamic" in the sentence:

• "Dynamic" in this context refers to someone who is energetic, active, and always changing or evolving.

• Option A, "enduring," means lasting over time, which isn’t a close match.

• Option B, "attentive," involves paying close attention, which is different.

• Option D, "jealous," is unrelated.

• Option C, "evolutionary," relates to change and development. Since "dynamic" conveys a sense of continual progress or change, Option C is the closest in meaning.

Thus, the best choice is Option C: evolutionary.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021

"Bemoaning" also means to express sorrow or regret.

Thus, the option that is nearest in meaning is:

Option D: backer

questions.title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
questions.question:
Direction: Select the option that is nearest in meaning to the underlined word and mark your response in your answer sheet accordingly.

Arrogance is a hallmark of his nature.
questions.solution:
The word "arrogance" means excessive pride and a feeling of superiority. Let's examine the options:

A. Concern: This refers to worry or anxiety, which is not synonymous with arrogance.

B. Unpretentiousness: This means being modest and humble, the opposite of arrogance.

C. Conceit: This means an excessively high opinion of oneself, which is a very close synonym for arrogance.

D. Simplicity: This suggests being straightforward and uncomplicated, again, not related to arrogance.

Therefore, the option nearest in meaning to "arrogance" is C. Conceit. Both terms describe an inflated sense of self-importance.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
questions.question:
Direction : Select the option that is nearest in meaning to the underlined word and mark your response in your answer sheet accordingly.

She undertook a reconnaissance of the entire issue.
questions.solution:
Option C: investigation

"Reconnaissance" refers to a preliminary survey or exploration to gather information, typically about enemy territory or a situation. While "investigation" involves examining something closely to gain knowledge, making it nearest in meaning to "reconnaissance."

questions.solution:
The correct option that is nearest in meaning to the word "replica" is:

A "replica" refers to an exact or close copy of something, which aligns with the meaning of "clone" as both imply being identical or very similar in appearance or characteristics.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
questions.question:
Direction : Select the option that is nearest in meaning to the underlined word and mark your response in your answer sheet accordingly.

The sardonic nature of her stories made her stand out among contemporary writers.
questions.solution:
The word "sardonic" means grimly mocking or cynical. Therefore, the closest option is C, mocking.

Let's examine why the other options are incorrect:

A. Compassionate: Compassionate means feeling or showing sympathy and concern for others. This is the opposite of sardonic.

B. Insightful: Insightful means having or showing an accurate and deep understanding; perceptive. While a sardonic story might be insightful, the core meaning of sardonic is not about insight but about a mocking or cynical tone.

D. Comic: Comic means causing laughter; humorous. Sardonic humor can be funny, but it's often dark and cynical, not purely comedic. The emphasis in "sardonic" is on the mocking and cynical aspects, not the humorous ones.

In short, "sardonic" describes a specific type of humor or tone characterized by its mocking and cynical nature, making "mocking" the most accurate synonym.

questions.question: SI unit of Boltzmann's constant is
questions.solution: The SI unit of Boltzmann's constant is JK$$-$$1

questions.title: BITSAT 2021
questions.question: For the complete combustion of ethanol, $${C_2}{H_5}OH(I) + 3{O_2}(g) \to 2C{O_2}(g) + 3{H_2}O(I)$$, the amount of heat produced as measured in bomb calorimeter is 1364.47 kJ mol$$-$$1 at 25$$^\circ$$C. Assuming ideality the enthalpy of combustion, $$\Delta$$HC, for the reaction will be (R = 8.314 JK$$-$$1 mol$$-$$1)
questions.solution:
$${C_2}{H_5}OH(I) + 3{O_2}(g) \to 2C{O_2}(g) + 3{H_2}O(I)$$

$$\Delta U = - 1364.47$$ kJ/mol,

$$ \Rightarrow \Delta {n_g} = - 1$$

T = 25$$^\circ$$C = 298 K

$$\therefore$$ $$\Delta H = \Delta U + \Delta {n_g}RT$$

$$\Delta H = - 1364.47 + {{ - 1 \times 8314 \times 298} \over {1000}}$$

[Here, value of R in unit of J must be converted into kJ]

$$ = - 1364.47 - 2.4776 = - 1366.95$$ kJ/mol

questions.title: BITSAT 2021
questions.question: The incorrect expression among the following is
questions.solution:
(a) $$\Delta$$G = $$\Delta$$H $$-$$ T $$\Delta$$S

For a system, total entropy change is $$\Delta$$Stotal,

$$\Delta$$Htotal = 0

$$\therefore$$ $$\Delta$$Gsystem = $$-$$T$$\Delta$$Stotal

$$\therefore$$ $${{\Delta {G_{system}}} \over {\Delta {S_{total}}}} = - T$$

Thus, (a) is true,

(b) For isothermal reversible process, $$\Delta$$E = 0.

By first law of thermodynamics,

$$\Delta$$E = q $$\times$$ W

$$\therefore$$ Wreversible = $$-$$q = $$-$$$$\int_{{V_t}}^{{V_f}} {p\,dV} $$

Wreversible = $$ - nRT\ln {{{V_f}} \over {{V_t}}}$$

Thus, (b) is true.

(c) $$\Delta G^\circ = \Delta H^\circ - T\Delta S^\circ $$

$$\Delta G^\circ = - nRT\ln \,K = - RT\ln K$$ (for n = 1)

$$\therefore$$ $$ - RT\ln K = \Delta H^\circ - T\Delta S^\circ $$

$$\therefore$$ $$\ln K = - \left( {{{\Delta H^\circ - T\Delta S^\circ } \over {RT}}} \right)$$

Thus, (c) is incorrect.

(d) $$\Delta G^\circ = - RT\ln K$$

$$\therefore$$ $$\ln K = - {{\Delta G^\circ } \over {RT}}$$ $$\therefore$$ $$K = {e^{ - \Delta G^\circ /RT}}$$

Thus, (d) is also true.

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2021 Paper 1 Online
questions.question:
An ideal gas undergoes a reversible isothermal expansion from state I to state II followed by a reversible adiabatic expansion from state II to state III. The correct plot(s) representing the changes from state I to state III is (are)

(p : pressure, V : volume, T : temperature, H : enthalpy, S : entropy)
questions.solution:
From state I to II Reversible isothermal expansion takes place. So, following changes take place,

● pressure decreases.
● Volume increases.
● Temperature remains constant.
● Enthalpy, H remains constant.
● Entropy, S for expansion increases.

So, all options follows the above mentioned conditions, so all graphs are correct for state I and II.

From state II to III Reversible adiabatic expansion takes place. So, following changes take place.

● pressure decreases.
● Volume increases.
● Temperature decreases.
● Enthalpy, H decreases.
● Entropy, S remains constant.
● H increases instead of decreasing, so only option (c) is incorrect.

All other options, i.e., (a), (b) and (d) follows the above mentioned conditions.

Therefore, correct graphical representations are (a), (b) and (d).

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2021 Paper 1 Online
questions.question: The value of standard enthalpy, $$\Delta$$H<sup>o</sup> (in kJ mol<sup>$$-$$1</sup>) for the given reaction is _______.
questions.solution:
For the given reaction,

$$ \mathrm{X}(\mathrm{s}) \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{X}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{Z}(\mathrm{g}) $$

We have

$$ \frac{d(\ln K)}{d(1 / T)}=\frac{-\Delta H^{\circ}}{R} $$

And $$ K=\frac{p_{z}}{p^{\circ}} $$

Substituting the value of $$K$$ in Eq. (1), we get

$$ \frac{d\left(\ln \frac{p_{z}}{p^{\circ}}\right)}{d\left(\frac{1}{T}\right)}=\frac{-\Delta H^{\circ}}{R} $$

Or

$$ d\left(\ln \frac{p_{z}}{p^{\circ}}\right)=\frac{-\Delta H^{\circ}}{R} d\left(\frac{1}{T}\right) $$

Integrating both sides of Eq. (2), we get

$$ \ln \frac{p_{z}}{p^{\circ}}=\frac{-\Delta H^{\circ}}{R T}+\ln A $$

Where $$\ln A=$$ Integration constant

From the given plot of $$\ln \frac{p_{z}}{p^{\circ}} \mathrm{vs} \frac{10^{4}}{T}$$


The slope of the plot is

$$ \text { Slope }=\frac{-\Delta H^{\circ}}{R}=\frac{y_{2}-y_{1}}{x_{2}-x_{1}}=\frac{-7-(-3)}{\left(\frac{12}{10^{4}}-\frac{10}{10^{4}}\right)} $$

$$\frac{-\Delta H^{\circ}}{R}=\frac{-4}{2} \times 10^{4}$$

$$\Delta H^{\circ}=-2 \times 10^{4} \times R$$

$$\Delta H^{\circ}=2 \times 8.314 \times 10^{4}$$

$$\Delta H^{\circ}=16.628 \times 10^{4} \mathrm{~J} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$$

$$\Delta H^{\circ}=166.28 \mathrm{~kJ} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$$

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2021 Paper 1 Online
questions.question: The value of $$\Delta$$S<sup>$$\theta$$</sup> (in J K<sup>$$-$$1</sup> mol<sup>$$-$$1</sup>) for the given reaction, at 1000 K is _________.
questions.solution:
For the given reaction,

$$ \mathrm{X}(\mathrm{s}) \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{X}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{Z}(\mathrm{g}) $$

We have

$$ \frac{d(\ln K)}{d(1 / T)}=\frac{-\Delta H^{\circ}}{R} $$

And $$ K=\frac{p_{z}}{p^{\circ}} $$

Substituting the value of $$K$$ in Eq. (1), we get

$$ \frac{d\left(\ln \frac{p_{z}}{p^{\circ}}\right)}{d\left(\frac{1}{T}\right)}=\frac{-\Delta H^{\circ}}{R} $$

Or

$$ d\left(\ln \frac{p_{z}}{p^{\circ}}\right)=\frac{-\Delta H^{\circ}}{R} d\left(\frac{1}{T}\right) $$

Integrating both sides of Eq. (2), we get

$$ \ln \frac{p_{z}}{p^{\circ}}=\frac{-\Delta H^{\circ}}{R T}+\ln A $$

Where $$\ln A=$$ Integration constant

We can also calculate the intercept $$(\ln A)$$ from the plot of $$\ln \frac{p_{z}}{p^{\circ}} \mathrm{vs} \frac{1}{T}$$.

Now, from the equation of straight line, we have

or

$$ \begin{aligned} &y=m x+C \\ &C=y-m x \end{aligned} $$

From the plot, we have

$$ m=-2 \times 10^{4} \mathrm{~K}, y=-3, x=\frac{10}{10^{4}} \mathrm{~K} $$

Now, substituting the above values in Eq. (4), we get

$$ \begin{aligned} &C=-3-\left(-2 \times 10^{4} \times \frac{10}{10^{4}}\right) \\ &C=-3+20 \\ &C=17 \end{aligned} $$

$$\Rightarrow C=17$$

This intercept is equal to $$\ln A$$ in Eq. (3), so we have

$$ \ln A=C=17 $$

From the thermodynamic equations, we have

$$ \Delta G^{\circ}=\Delta H^{\circ}-7 \Delta S^{\circ} $$

For any equilibrium reaction, the Gibb's energy is,

$$ \Delta G^{\circ}=-R T \ln K $$

On comparing both the equations, we get

$$ -R T \ln K=\Delta H^{\circ}-T \Delta S^{\circ} $$

Or

$$ \begin{aligned} \ln K &=\frac{-\Delta H^{\circ}}{R T}+\frac{\Delta S^{\circ}}{R} \\ \left(\frac{p_{z}}{p^{\circ}}\right) &=\frac{-\Delta H^{\circ}}{R T}+\frac{\Delta S^{\circ}}{R} \end{aligned} $$

On comparing Eq. (3) and (6), we have

$$ \frac{\Delta S^{\circ}}{R}=\ln A=17 $$

Or

$$ \begin{aligned} \Delta S^{\circ} &=17 R \\ &=17 \times 8.314 \\ &=141.34 \,\mathrm{JK}^{-1} \end{aligned} $$

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2021 Paper 2 Online
questions.question:
One mole of an ideal gas at 900 K, undergoes two reversible processes, I followed by II, as shown below. If the work done by the gas in the two processes are same, the value of $$\ln {{{V_3}} \over {{V_2}}}$$ is _________.


(U : internal energy, S : entropy, p : pressure, V : volume, R : gas constant)

(Given : molar heat capacity at constant volume, CV,m of the gas is $${5 \over 2}$$R)
questions.solution:
For process 1, entropy is constant thus, q is constant

$$\therefore$$ $${W_I} = \Delta U = n{C_{V,m}}\Delta T$$

$$ = - (2250 - 450)R = - 1800R$$ .... (i)

$$\Delta U = n{C_{V,m}}\Delta T$$

$$ - 1800R = 1 \times {{5R} \over 2} \times \Delta T$$

$$ \Rightarrow \Delta T = - 720K$$

$${T_2} - {T_1} = - 720K$$

$${T_2} = - 720K + 900K = 180K$$

For process II,

$${W_{II}} = - nR{T_2}\ln \left( {{{{V_3}} \over {{V_2}}}} \right)$$

$$ = - 1 \times R \times 180\ln \left( {{{{V_3}} \over {{V_2}}}} \right)$$ .... (ii)

As, both work done for process I and II are equal,

Therefore, WI = WII

$$ - 1800R = - R \times 180 \times \ln \left( {{{{V_3}} \over {{V_2}}}} \right)$$

$$\ln \left( {{{{V_3}} \over {{V_2}}}} \right) = {{1800} \over {180}} \Rightarrow \ln \left( {{{{V_3}} \over {{V_2}}}} \right) = 10$$

questions.title: BITSAT 2021
questions.question: Angle between the diagonals of a cube is
questions.solution:
Let each edge of cube be a, then coordinates of the vertices of cube are

O(0, 0, 0), A(a, 0, 0), B(0, a, 0), C(0, 0, a), N(a, a, 0), P(a, a, a), L(0, a, a), M(a, 0, a)

Direction ratios of the diagonals OP, AL, BM and CN are (a, a, a), ($$-$$a, a, a), (a, $$-$$a, a) and (a, a, $$-$$a).

Let $$\theta$$ be the acute angle between diagonals OP and AL.

$$\therefore$$ $$\cos \theta = {{{a_1}{a_2} + {b_1}{b_2} + {c_1}{c_2}} \over {\sqrt {a_1^2 + b_1^2 + c_1^2} \sqrt {a_2^2 + b_2^2 + c_2^2} }}$$

$$ = {{a \times ( - a) + a \times a + a \times a} \over {\sqrt {{a^2} + {a^2} + {a^2}} \sqrt {{{( - a)}^2} + {a^2} + {a^2}} }}$$

$$ = {{ - {a^2} + {a^2} + {a^2}} \over {\sqrt {3{a^2}} \sqrt {3{a^2}} }} = {{{a^2}} \over {a\sqrt 3 \times a\sqrt 3 }}$$

$$ \Rightarrow \cos \theta = {1 \over 3} \Rightarrow \theta = {\cos ^{ - 1}}\left( {{1 \over 3}} \right)$$

questions.title: BITSAT 2021
questions.question:
Consider the two lines

$${L_1}:{{x + 1} \over 3} = {{y + 2} \over 1} = {{z + 1} \over 2}$$ and $${L_2}:{{x - 2} \over 1} = {{y + 2} \over 2} = {{z - 3} \over 3}$$

The unit vector perpendicular to both the lines L1 and L2 is
questions.solution:
Given lines are

$${L_1}:{{x + 1} \over 3} = {{y + 2} \over 1} = {{z + 1} \over 2}$$ and $${L_2}:{{x - 2} \over 1} = {{y + 2} \over 2} = {{z - 3} \over 3}$$

Now, convert into vector form

$${L_1}:( - \widehat i - 2\widehat j - \widehat k) + \lambda (3\widehat i + \widehat j + 2\widehat k)$$

$${L_2}:(2\widehat i + 2\widehat j + 3\widehat k) + \mu (\widehat i + 2\widehat j + 3\widehat k)$$

Line L1 comparing with $${a_1} + \lambda {b_1}$$

and L2 comparing with $${a_2} + \mu {b_2}$$, then we have $${b_1} = 3\widehat i + \widehat j + 2\widehat k$$ and $${b_2} = \widehat i + 2\widehat j + 3\widehat k$$

Perpendicular to both b1 and b2 = $${b_1} \times {b_2}$$

$$ = \left| {\matrix{ {\widehat i} & {\widehat j} & {\widehat k} \cr 3 & 1 & 2 \cr 1 & 2 & 3 \cr } } \right|$$

$$ = \widehat i(3 - 4) - \widehat j(9 - 2) + \widehat k(6 - 1) = - \widehat i - 7\widehat j + 5\widehat k$$

$$\therefore$$ Required unit vector $$ = {{ - \widehat i - 7\widehat j + 5\widehat k} \over {\sqrt {{{( - 1)}^2} + {{( - 7)}^2} + {{(5)}^2}} }}$$

$$ = {{ - \widehat i - 7\widehat j + 5\widehat k} \over {\sqrt {75} }} = {{ - \widehat i - 7\widehat j + 5\widehat k} \over {5\sqrt 3 }}$$

questions.title: BITSAT 2021
questions.question: The distance between the line $$r = 2\widehat i - 2\widehat j + 3\widehat k + \lambda (\widehat i - \widehat j + 4\widehat k)$$ and the plane $$a\,.\,(\widehat i + 5\widehat j + \widehat k) = 5$$ is
questions.solution:
The given line is r = a + tb, where $$a = 2\widehat i - 2\widehat j + 3\widehat k$$, $$b = \widehat i - \widehat j + 4\widehat k$$ and given plane is r . n = d, where $$n = \widehat i + 5\widehat j + \widehat k$$, d = 5.

Since, $$b\,.\,n = 1 - 5 + 4 = 0$$

$$\therefore$$ Given line is parallel to given plane.

$$\therefore$$ The distance between the line and the plane is equal to length of the perpendicular from the point a = $${2\widehat i - 2\widehat j + 3\widehat k}$$ on the line to the given plane.

$$\therefore$$ Required distance $$ = \left| {{{(2\widehat i - 2\widehat j + 3\widehat k)\,.\,(\widehat i + 5\widehat j + \widehat k) - 5} \over {\sqrt {1 + 25 + 1} }}} \right|$$

$$ = \left| {{{2 - 10 + 3 - 5} \over {\sqrt {27} }}} \right| = {{10} \over {3\sqrt 3 }}$$

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14 November 2021
questions.question: The locus of a point P(x, y, z) which moves in such a way that z = 7 is a
questions.solution:
Given that the point P has coordinates $$P(x, y, z)$$ and the condition is $$z = 7$$, this means:

The z-coordinate is fixed at 7.

The x and y coordinates can be any real numbers.

This describes a plane where all points have a constant z-value of 7, which is parallel to the xy-plane.

Thus, the correct answer is:

Option D: plane parallel to xy-plane.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14 November 2021
questions.question:
Consider the following statements:

1. A-line in space can have infinitely many direction ratios.

2. It is possible for certain lines that the sum of the squares of direction cosines can be equal to the sum of its direction cosines.

Which of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.solution:
A line in space "can have infinitely many direction ratios."

The direction of a line is determined by a set of numbers (a, b, c) up to a non-zero scalar multiple. For example, if (a, b, c) are direction ratios of a line, then (ka, kb, kc) for any nonzero constant k are also direction ratios representing the same line.

This means there are infinitely many choices for direction ratios that correspond to the same line.

Therefore, Statement 1 is true.

"It is possible for certain lines that the sum of the squares of direction cosines can be equal to the sum of its direction cosines."

For any line in space, if the direction cosines are $$l, m, n$$, they satisfy the relation:

$$l^2 + m^2 + n^2 = 1.$$

The statement asks if it is possible that:

$$l^2 + m^2 + n^2 = l + m + n,$$

which, using the above fact, reduces to:

$$1 = l + m + n.$$

We need to check if there exists a set of direction cosines $$l, m, n$$ (satisfying the normalization and being within the range [–1, 1]) such that $$l + m + n = 1.$$ For example, consider the direction cosines:

$$l = 0,\, m = 0,\, n = 1.$$

Here, $$l^2 + m^2 + n^2 = 0^2 + 0^2 + 1^2 = 1$$ and $$l + m + n = 0 + 0 + 1 = 1.$$

Since such a set exists, Statement 2 is also true.

Since both statements are correct, the answer is:

Option C: Both 1 and 2.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14 November 2021
questions.question: The xy-plane divides the line segment joining the points (-1, 3, 4) and (2, -5, 6)
questions.solution:
Understanding the Problem

The xy-plane is defined by the equation $$z = 0$$. We need to find where the line segment connecting the points $$(-1, 3, 4)$$ and $$(2, -5, 6)$$ intersects this plane.

Steps to Solve

Section Formula: Assume the xy-plane divides the line segment internally in the ratio $$k : 1$$. The coordinates of the point of intersection can be found using the section formula:

$$(\frac{k \cdot 2 + 1 \cdot (-1)}{k+1}, \frac{k \cdot (-5) + 1 \cdot 3}{k+1}, \frac{k \cdot 6 + 1 \cdot 4}{k+1})$$

Using the xy-plane Property: Since the point lies on the xy-plane, its z-coordinate must be 0. Therefore:

$$\frac{6k + 4}{k+1} = 0$$

Solve for k:

$$6k + 4 = 0$$

$$6k = -4$$

$$k = -\frac{4}{6} = -\frac{2}{3}$$

Interpret the Result: Since $$k$$ is negative, the xy-plane divides the line segment externally in the ratio $$2 : 3$$.

Therefore, the correct answer is Option C: externally in the ratio 2 : 3

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14 November 2021
questions.question: The number of spheres of radius r touching the coordinate axes is
questions.solution:
1. Set up the conditions for the centre $(a,b,c)$

$ \begin{aligned} \text{Distance to $x$-axis}&=\sqrt{b^{2}+c^{2}}=r,\\[4pt] \text{Distance to $y$-axis}&=\sqrt{a^{2}+c^{2}}=r,\\[4pt] \text{Distance to $z$-axis}&=\sqrt{a^{2}+b^{2}}=r. \end{aligned} $

Squaring each equation:

$ \begin{cases} b^{2}+c^{2}=r^{2}\\ a^{2}+c^{2}=r^{2}\\ a^{2}+b^{2}=r^{2} \end{cases} \tag{1} $

2. Solve for $a,b,c$

Subtract the first two equations in (1):

$ b^{2}-a^{2}=0\;\;\Longrightarrow\;\;a^{2}=b^{2}. $

Similarly, subtract the last two equations:

$ c^{2}-b^{2}=0\;\;\Longrightarrow\;\;c^{2}=b^{2}. $

Hence $|a|=|b|=|c|$.

Put $a^{2}=b^{2}$ into the third equation of (1):

$ a^{2}+a^{2}=r^{2}\;\;\Longrightarrow\;\;a^{2}=\frac{r^{2}}{2} \;\;\Longrightarrow\;\;|a|=|b|=|c|=\frac{r}{\sqrt{2}}. $

3. Count the possible centres

Each coordinate can independently be $\pm \dfrac{r}{\sqrt{2}}$.

$ \bigl(\pm \tfrac{r}{\sqrt{2}},\ \pm \tfrac{r}{\sqrt{2}},\ \pm \tfrac{r}{\sqrt{2}}\bigr) $

There are $2 \times 2 \times 2 = 8$ such combinations, giving eight distinct spheres (one in each octant), all of radius $r$.

$ \boxed{8} $

Correct option: C

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14 November 2021
questions.question: ABCDEFGH is a cuboid with base ABCD. Let A(0, 0, 0), B(12, 0, 0), C(12, 6, 0) and G(12, 6, 4) be the vertices. If α is the angle between AB and AG; β is the angle between AC and AG, then what is the value of cos 2α + cos 2β?
questions.solution:
Given:

The vertices of the cuboid ABCDEFGH are:

A(0, 0, 0), B(12, 0, 0), C(12, 6, 0), and G(12, 6, 4).

Concept:

To find the angle θ between two vectors a and b, use the dot product formula:

$ \cos \theta = \frac{\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}}{|\vec{a}| \cdot |\vec{b}|} $

For a vector $\vec{p} = a\mathbf{i} + b\mathbf{j} + c\mathbf{k}$, its magnitude is:

$ |\vec{p}| = \sqrt{a^2 + b^2 + c^2} $

Calculation:

Position Vector of AB:

$ \overrightarrow{AB} = (\mathbf{12i} + \mathbf{0j} + \mathbf{0k}) - (\mathbf{0i} + \mathbf{0j} + \mathbf{0k}) = 12\mathbf{i} $

Magnitude:

$ |\overrightarrow{AB}| = \sqrt{12^2 + 0^2 + 0^2} = 12 $

Position Vector of AG:

$ \overrightarrow{AG} = 12\mathbf{i} + 6\mathbf{j} + 4\mathbf{k} $

Magnitude:

$ |\overrightarrow{AG}| = \sqrt{12^2 + 6^2 + 4^2} = 14 $

Position Vector of AC:

$ \overrightarrow{AC} = 12\mathbf{i} + 6\mathbf{j} $

Magnitude:

$ |\overrightarrow{AC}| = \sqrt{12^2 + 6^2} = \sqrt{180} $

Calculating Angles:

Angle between $\overrightarrow{AB}$ and $\overrightarrow{AG}$:

$ \cos \alpha = \frac{\overrightarrow{AB} \cdot \overrightarrow{AG}}{|\overrightarrow{AB}| \cdot |\overrightarrow{AG}|} = \frac{12 \cdot 12}{12 \times 14} = \frac{6}{7} $

Angle between $\overrightarrow{AC}$ and $\overrightarrow{AG}$:

$ \cos \beta = \frac{\overrightarrow{AC} \cdot \overrightarrow{AG}}{|\overrightarrow{AC}| \cdot |\overrightarrow{AG}|} = \frac{\sqrt{180}}{14} $

Result:

To find $\cos 2\alpha + \cos 2\beta$:

$ 2\cos^2 \alpha - 1 + 2\cos^2 \beta - 1 $

Using the given values:

$ 2\left(\frac{6}{7}\right)^2 - 1 + 2\left(\frac{\sqrt{180}}{14}\right)^2 - 1 $

$ 2 \times \frac{36}{49} - 1 + 2 \times \frac{90}{196} - 1 $

$ \frac{72}{49} + \frac{90}{49} - 2 $

Finally:

$ \cos 2\alpha + \cos 2\beta = \frac{64}{49} $

∴ The value of $\cos 2\alpha + \cos 2\beta$ is $\frac{64}{49}$.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 18 April 2021
questions.question: What is the angle between the two lines having direction ratios (6, 3, 6) and (3, 3, 0)?
questions.solution:
CONCEPT:

The angle $ \theta $ between two lines with direction ratios $ (a_1, b_1, c_1) $ and $ (a_2, b_2, c_2) $ is determined using the formula:

$ \cos \theta = \left| \frac{a_1a_2 + b_1b_2 + c_1c_2}{\sqrt{a_1^2 + b_1^2 + c_1^2} \cdot \sqrt{a_2^2 + b_2^2 + c_2^2}} \right| $

CALCULATION:

To find the angle between two lines with direction ratios $ (6, 3, 6) $ and $ (3, 3, 0) $:

Given:

$ a_1 = 6 $, $ b_1 = 3 $, $ c_1 = 6 $

$ a_2 = 3 $, $ b_2 = 3 $, $ c_2 = 0 $

Using the formula, we calculate:

$ \cos \theta = \left| \frac{6 \cdot 3 + 3 \cdot 3 + 6 \cdot 0}{\sqrt{6^2 + 3^2 + 6^2} \cdot \sqrt{3^2 + 3^2 + 0^2}} \right| $

$ = \left| \frac{18 + 9 + 0}{\sqrt{36 + 9 + 36} \cdot \sqrt{9 + 9 + 0}} \right| $

$ = \left| \frac{27}{\sqrt{81} \cdot \sqrt{18}} \right| $

$ = \frac{27}{9 \cdot \sqrt{18}} $

$ = \frac{3}{\sqrt{18}} $

$ = \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} $

Thus, $ \cos \theta = \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} $.

Therefore, $ \theta = \frac{\pi}{4} $.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 18 April 2021
questions.question: If l, m, n are the direction cosines of the line x - 1 = 2(y + 3) = 1 - z, then what is l<sup>4</sup> + m<sup>4</sup> + n<sup>4</sup> equal to?
questions.solution:
To find $ l^4 + m^4 + n^4 $, we first need to determine the direction cosines $ l $, $ m $, and $ n $.

The given line equation is:

$ x - 1 = 2(y + 3) = 1 - z $

This can be rewritten in the symmetric form as:

$ \frac{x - 1}{1} = \frac{y + 3}{\frac{1}{2}} = \frac{z - 1}{-1} $

From the symmetric form, the direction ratios of the line are $ (1, \frac{1}{2}, -1) $.

The direction cosines $ l $, $ m $, and $ n $ are given by:

$ l = \frac{1}{\sqrt{1^2 + \left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^2 + (-1)^2}} = \frac{1}{\sqrt{1 + \frac{1}{4} + 1}} $

$ m = \frac{\frac{1}{2}}{\sqrt{1^2 + \left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^2 + (-1)^2}} = \frac{\frac{1}{2}}{\sqrt{1 + \frac{1}{4} + 1}} $

$ n = \frac{-1}{\sqrt{1^2 + \left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^2 + (-1)^2}} = \frac{-1}{\sqrt{1 + \frac{1}{4} + 1}} $

Calculate the denominator:

$ 1 + \frac{1}{4} + 1 = \frac{9}{4} $

Hence, the magnitude is:

$ \sqrt{\frac{9}{4}} = \frac{3}{2} $

Therefore, the direction cosines are:

$ l = \frac{1}{\frac{3}{2}} = \frac{2}{3}, \quad m = \frac{\frac{1}{2}}{\frac{3}{2}} = \frac{1}{3}, \quad n = \frac{-1}{\frac{3}{2}} = -\frac{2}{3} $

Now, calculate $ l^4 + m^4 + n^4 $:

$ l^4 = \left(\frac{2}{3}\right)^4 = \frac{16}{81} $

$ m^4 = \left(\frac{1}{3}\right)^4 = \frac{1}{81} $

$ n^4 = \left(-\frac{2}{3}\right)^4 = \frac{16}{81} $

Adding these together:

$ l^4 + m^4 + n^4 = \frac{16}{81} + \frac{1}{81} + \frac{16}{81} = \frac{33}{81} = \frac{11}{27} $

Therefore, the answer is:

Option B:

$ \boxed{\frac{11}{27}} $

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 18 April 2021
questions.question: What is the number of possible values of k for which the line joining the points (k, 1, 3) and (1, -2, k + 1) also passes through the point (15, 2, -4)?
questions.solution:
Concept:

The direction ratios of the line joining two points $(x_1, y_1, z_1)$ and $(x_2, y_2, z_2)$ are given by:

$ a = x_2 - x_1, \quad b = y_2 - y_1, \quad c = z_2 - z_1 $

If $a$, $b$, and $c$ are the direction ratios of a line passing through the point $(x_1, y_1, z_1)$, then the equation of the line can be expressed as:

$ \frac{x - x_1}{a} = \frac{y - y_1}{b} = \frac{z - z_1}{c} $

Calculation:

Given: The line joining the points $(k, 1, 3)$ and $(1, -2, k+1)$ also passes through the point $(15, 2, -4)$.

The direction ratios of the line joining the points $(k, 1, 3)$ and $(1, -2, k+1)$ are:

$ a = 1 - k, \quad b = -3, \quad c = k - 2 $

The equation of the line using these direction ratios passing through the point $(k, 1, 3)$ is:

$ \frac{x - k}{1 - k} = \frac{y - 1}{-3} = \frac{z - 3}{k - 2} $

Since the line also passes through the point $(15, 2, -4)$, substitute $x = 15$, $y = 2$, and $z = -4$ into the equation:

$ \frac{15 - k}{1 - k} = \frac{2 - 1}{-3} = \frac{-4 - 3}{k - 2} $

From $\frac{15 - k}{1 - k} = \frac{1}{-3}$, we solve:

$ -45 + 3k = 1 - k $

$ 4k = 46 $

$ k = \frac{23}{2} $

From $\frac{1}{-3} = \frac{-7}{k - 2}$, we solve:

$ k - 2 = 21 $

$ k = 23 $

Thus, there are two possible values for $k$: $\frac{23}{2}$ and $23$.

Hence, the correct answer involves two possible values for $k$.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 18 April 2021
questions.question: The foot of the perpendicular drawn from the origin to the plane x + y + z = 3 is
questions.solution:
CONCEPT:

To find the perpendicular distance from a point $ P(x_1, y_1, z_1) $ to a plane given by the equation $ ax + by + cz + d = 0 $, use the formula:

$ d = \left| \frac{ax_1 + by_1 + cz_1 + d}{\sqrt{a^2 + b^2 + c^2}} \right| $

CALCULATION:

Let $ A(x, y, z) $ be the foot of the perpendicular from the origin $ (0, 0, 0) $ to the plane $ x + y + z = 3 $.

We need to find the perpendicular distance from the origin to the plane:

Substitute the coordinates of the origin into the distance formula for the plane $ x + y + z - 3 = 0 $:

$ d = \left| \frac{0 \cdot 1 + 0 \cdot 1 + 0 \cdot 1 - 3}{\sqrt{1^2 + 1^2 + 1^2}} \right| = \frac{3}{\sqrt{3}} = \sqrt{3} $

This calculation shows that the distance from the origin to the plane is $ \sqrt{3} $.

Next, check the distance from the origin to the potential point $ A $. If $ A = (1, 1, 1) $, then:

The distance from the origin $ (0, 0, 0) $ to $ A(1, 1, 1) $ is given by:

$ \sqrt{(1-0)^2 + (1-0)^2 + (1-0)^2} = \sqrt{1 + 1 + 1} = \sqrt{3} $

This confirms that the point $ A = (1, 1, 1) $ lies on the plane and matches the given perpendicular distance. Therefore, the correct choice is $ A = (1, 1, 1) $.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14 November 2021
questions.question: If sinθ and cosθ are the roots of the equation ax<sup>2</sup> + bx + c = 0, then which one of the following is correct?
options: -a2 + b2 + 2ac = 0
a2 - b2 + 2ac = 0
a2 + b2 + 2ac = 0
questions.solution:
Let's denote the two roots of the quadratic equation

$$ax^2 + bx + c = 0$$

as $ \sin \theta $ and $ \cos \theta $. We'll use Vieta's formulas and a trigonometric identity to derive the relationship.

Using Vieta's formulas:

The sum of the roots is

$$\sin \theta + \cos \theta = -\frac{b}{a}.$$

The product of the roots is

$$\sin \theta \cos \theta = \frac{c}{a}.$$

Now consider squaring the sum:

$$\left(\sin \theta + \cos \theta\right)^2 = \sin^2 \theta + 2\sin \theta \cos \theta + \cos^2 \theta.$$

But we know from the Pythagorean identity that

$$\sin^2 \theta + \cos^2 \theta = 1.$$

Therefore,

$$\left(\sin \theta + \cos \theta\right)^2 = 1 + 2 \sin \theta \cos \theta.$$

Substitute the value of the product from Vieta’s formulas:

$$\left(-\frac{b}{a}\right)^2 = 1 + 2\left(\frac{c}{a}\right).$$

This simplifies to:

$$\frac{b^2}{a^2} = 1 + \frac{2c}{a}.$$

Multiply both sides by $a^2$ to eliminate the fraction:

$$b^2 = a^2 + 2ac.$$

Rearranging the equation gives:

$$a^2 + 2ac - b^2 = 0,$$

which can be rewritten as:

$$a^2 - b^2 + 2ac = 0.$$

Comparing with the given options, this matches Option C.

Thus, the correct answer is:

Option C

$$a^2 - b^2 + 2ac = 0.$$

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14 November 2021
questions.question: If sin(A + B) = 1 and 2 sin(A - B) = 1, where 0 < A, B < $\frac{\pi}{2}$, then what is tan A ∶ tan B equal to?
questions.solution:
Given:

$\sin(A + B) = 1$

This implies:

$ \sin(A + B) = \sin \left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right) $

Thus:

$ A + B = \frac{\pi}{2} \quad \text{----(1)} $

Also given:

$2 \sin(A - B) = 1$

This implies:

$ \sin(A - B) = \frac{1}{2} $

Thus:

$ \sin(A - B) = \sin \left(\frac{\pi}{6}\right) $

Therefore:

$ A - B = \frac{\pi}{6} \quad \text{----(2)} $

Solving equations (1) and (2):

$ A = \frac{\pi}{3}, \; B = \frac{\pi}{6} $

To find the value of "tan A ∶ tan B":

$ \frac{\tan \frac{\pi}{3}}{\tan \frac{\pi}{6}} = \frac{\sqrt{3}}{\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}} = 3 : 1 $

Conclusion:

The value of "tan A ∶ tan B" is $3 : 1$.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14 November 2021
questions.question: Let sin x + sin y = cos x + cos y for all x, y ∈ ℝ. What is $\rm tan \left(\frac{x}{2}+\frac{y}{2}\right)$ equal to?
questions.solution:
We start with the given equation:

$$\sin x + \sin y = \cos x + \cos y.$$

A useful step is to apply the sum-to-product identities:

• For sine:

$$\sin x + \sin y = 2\sin\left(\frac{x+y}{2}\right)\cos\left(\frac{x-y}{2}\right),$$

• For cosine:

$$\cos x + \cos y = 2\cos\left(\frac{x+y}{2}\right)\cos\left(\frac{x-y}{2}\right).$$

So the equation becomes:

$$2\sin\left(\frac{x+y}{2}\right)\cos\left(\frac{x-y}{2}\right) = 2\cos\left(\frac{x+y}{2}\right)\cos\left(\frac{x-y}{2}\right).$$

Assuming $$\cos\left(\frac{x-y}{2}\right) \neq 0,$$ we can divide both sides by $$2\cos\left(\frac{x-y}{2}\right),$$ giving:

$$\sin\left(\frac{x+y}{2}\right) = \cos\left(\frac{x+y}{2}\right).$$

This immediately implies:

$$\tan\left(\frac{x+y}{2}\right) = 1.$$

Since $\tan\left(\frac{x}{2}+\frac{y}{2}\right)$ is just another way to write $\tan\left(\frac{x+y}{2}\right)$, we conclude that:

$$\tan\left(\frac{x}{2}+\frac{y}{2}\right) = 1.$$

Thus, the correct answer is Option A.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14 November 2021
questions.question: Let 4sin<sup>2</sup> x = 3, where 0 ≤ x ≤ π. What is tan3x equal to?
questions.solution:
Start with the equation:

$$4\sin^2 x = 3$$

Divide both sides by 4 to isolate $$\sin^2 x$$:

$$\sin^2 x = \frac{3}{4}$$

Take the square root of both sides. Since $$0 \le x \le \pi$$, the sine function is non-negative, so we only consider:

$$\sin x = \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}$$

The equation $$\sin x = \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}$$ has two solutions in the interval $$[0, \pi]$$:

$$x = \frac{\pi}{3}$$

$$x = \frac{2\pi}{3}$$

Now, compute $$3x$$ for each solution:

If $$x = \frac{\pi}{3}$$, then $$3x = \pi$$.

If $$x = \frac{2\pi}{3}$$, then $$3x = 2\pi$$.

Evaluate the tangent for both:

$$\tan(\pi) = 0$$

$$\tan(2\pi) = 0$$

Thus, in both cases, we find:

$$\tan 3x = 0$$

So, the correct answer is:

Option C: 0.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14 November 2021
questions.question: Let the equation sec x.cosec x = p have a solution, where p is a positive real number. What should be the smallest value of p?
questions.solution:
We start with the equation

$$ \sec x \cdot \csc x = p. $$

Step 1: Express the functions in terms of sine and cosine.

Recall that

$$\sec x = \frac{1}{\cos x},$$

$$\csc x = \frac{1}{\sin x}.$$

Substituting these, we get:

$$ \sec x \cdot \csc x = \frac{1}{\cos x} \cdot \frac{1}{\sin x} = \frac{1}{\sin x \cos x} = p. $$

Step 2: Rewrite the equation in terms of the product of sine and cosine.

Rearranging the equation gives:

$$ \sin x \cos x = \frac{1}{p}. $$

Step 3: Use a trigonometric identity.

Notice that

$$ \sin x \cos x = \frac{1}{2} \sin 2x. $$

Therefore, we have:

$$ \frac{1}{2} \sin 2x = \frac{1}{p} \quad \Longrightarrow \quad \sin 2x = \frac{2}{p}. $$

Step 4: Consider the range of the sine function.

The sine function always lies between -1 and 1. Since we are looking for positive $p$, we must have the right-hand side to be positive. Consequently, we focus on the maximum value of $$\sin 2x,$$ which is 1.

Setting $$\sin 2x = 1,$$ we obtain:

$$ \frac{2}{p} = 1 \quad \Longrightarrow \quad p = 2. $$

Thus, the smallest possible value of $p$ for which the equation has a solution is 2.

Final Answer: Option C, which is $$p = 2.$$

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14 November 2021
questions.question: For what value of θ, where 0 < θ < $\frac{\pi}{2}$, does sin θ + sin θ cos θ maximum value?
questions.solution:
We want to maximize the function

$$f(\theta) = \sin \theta + \sin \theta \cos \theta = \sin \theta (1 + \cos \theta)$$

for $$0 < \theta < \frac{\pi}{2}.$$

Here's a step-by-step solution:

Differentiate f(θ):

Since $$f(\theta) = \sin \theta (1 + \cos \theta),$$ use the product rule:

$$f'(\theta) = \cos \theta (1 + \cos \theta) + \sin \theta (- \sin \theta) = \cos \theta (1 + \cos \theta) - \sin^2 \theta.$$

Simplify the derivative:

Expand the expression:

$$f'(\theta) = \cos \theta + \cos^2 \theta - \sin^2 \theta.$$

Notice that $$\cos^2\theta - \sin^2\theta$$ is the double-angle formula for cosine:

$$\cos^2 \theta - \sin^2 \theta = \cos 2\theta.$$

Therefore, the derivative becomes:

$$f'(\theta) = \cos \theta + \cos 2\theta.$$

Set the derivative equal to zero:

To maximize the function, set:

$$\cos \theta + \cos 2\theta = 0.$$

Express cos 2θ in terms of cosθ:

Use the double-angle formula:

$$\cos 2\theta = 2\cos^2 \theta - 1.$$

Substitute into the equation:

$$\cos \theta + 2\cos^2 \theta - 1 = 0.$$

Solve for cosθ:

Let $$x = \cos \theta.$$ Then the equation becomes:

$$2x^2 + x - 1 = 0.$$

Solve this quadratic using the quadratic formula:

$$x = \frac{-1 \pm \sqrt{1^2 - 4(2)(-1)}}{2 \cdot 2} = \frac{-1 \pm \sqrt{1 + 8}}{4} = \frac{-1 \pm 3}{4}.$$

This gives two solutions:

$$x = \frac{-1 + 3}{4} = \frac{1}{2},$$

$$x = \frac{-1 - 3}{4} = -1.$$

Since $$\theta$$ is in the interval $$\left(0, \frac{\pi}{2}\right),$$ $$\cos \theta$$ is positive. Therefore, only $$x = \frac{1}{2}$$ is valid.

Determine θ:

If $$\cos \theta = \frac{1}{2},$$ then:

$$\theta = \frac{\pi}{3}.$$

Thus, the maximum value of $$\sin \theta + \sin \theta \cos \theta$$ for $$\theta$$ in $$\left(0, \frac{\pi}{2}\right)$$ occurs at

$$\theta = \frac{\pi}{3}.$$

The correct answer is Option B.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14 November 2021
questions.question: Let ABC be a triangle. If cos2A + cos2B + cos2C = -1 then which one of the following is correct?
options: sin A sin B sin C = 0
sin A sin B cos C = 0
cos A sin B sin C = 0
cos A cos B cos C = 0
questions.solution:
Given:

$ \cos 2A + \cos 2B + \cos 2C = -1 $

Rewriting this, we have:

$ \cos 2A + \cos 2B + (1 + \cos 2C) = 0 $

This can be expanded using our trigonometric identities:

$ 2 \cos(A + B) \cos(A - B) + 2 \cos^2 C = 0 \quad \text{----(i)} $

Using the identity for $\angle A + \angle B = 180^\circ - \angle C$:

$ \cos (A + B) = \cos (180^\circ - C) = - \cos C \quad \text{----(ii)} $

Substituting equation (ii) into equation (i):

$ 2(-\cos C) \cos(A - B) + 2 \cos^2 C = 0 $

Simplifying, we get:

$ -2 \cos C [\cos(A - B) - \cos C] = 0 \quad \text{----(iii)} $

From equation (ii) and equation (iii), it follows:

$ -2 \cos C [\cos(A - B) + \cos(A + B)] = 0 $

This reduces to:

$ -2 \cos C (2 \cos A \cdot \cos B) = 0 $

Thus, we conclude:

$ \boxed{ \cos A \cos B \cos C = 0 } $

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 18 April 2021
questions.question: If $\tan A=\dfrac{1}{7}$, then what is cos 2A equal to?
questions.solution:
Concept

The formula to find $\cos 2\theta$ in terms of $\tan \theta$ is as follows:

$ \cos 2\theta = \frac{1 - \tan^2\theta}{1 + \tan^2\theta} $

Calculation

Given that $\tan A = \frac{1}{7}$, we use the formula for $\cos 2A$:

$ \cos 2A = \frac{1 - \tan^2A}{1 + \tan^2A} $

Substituting the value of $\tan A$:

$ \cos 2A = \frac{1 - \left(\frac{1}{7}\right)^2}{1 + \left(\frac{1}{7}\right)^2} $

Calculating further, we find:

$ \cos 2A = \frac{\frac{49 - 1}{49}}{\frac{49 + 1}{49}} $

This simplifies to:

$ \cos 2A = \frac{48}{50} = \frac{24}{25} $

Therefore, $\cos 2A$ is $\frac{24}{25}$.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 18 April 2021
questions.question: If $\rm tan\:x=-\dfrac{3}{4}$ and x is in the second quadrant, then what is the value of sin x ⋅ cos x?
questions.solution:
Explanation

Concept:

$ \tan x = \frac{\text{Perpendicular}}{\text{Base}} = \frac{P}{B} $

$ \sin x = \frac{\text{Perpendicular}}{\text{Hypotenuse}} = \frac{P}{H} $

$ \cos x = \frac{\text{Base}}{\text{Hypotenuse}} = \frac{B}{H} $

Using the Pythagorean theorem:

$ \text{Hypotenuse}^2 = \text{Perpendicular}^2 + \text{Base}^2 $

Calculation:

Given:

$ \tan x = -\frac{3}{4} $

This implies $ P = 3 $ and $ B = -4 $, as $ x $ is in the second quadrant where the tangent function is negative.

Using the Pythagorean theorem:

$ H^2 = P^2 + B^2 = 3^2 + (-4)^2 $

$ H = 5 $

Therefore:

$ \sin x = \frac{3}{5} $

$ \cos x = \frac{-4}{5} $

Multiplying these values gives:

$ \sin x \cdot \cos x = \frac{3}{5} \cdot \frac{-4}{5} = -\frac{12}{25} $

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 18 April 2021
questions.question:
What is the value of the following?

$\rm cosec\left(\dfrac{7\pi}{6}\right)sec\left(\dfrac{5\pi}{3}\right)$
questions.solution:
Concept:

$\sec(360^\circ - \theta) = \sec \theta$

$\csc(180^\circ + \theta) = -\csc \theta$

Calculation:

To find the value of $\csc\left(\frac{7\pi}{6}\right)\sec\left(\frac{5\pi}{3}\right)$, we first convert the radian measures to degrees:

$\csc\left(\frac{7\pi}{6}\right) = \csc(210^\circ)$

$\sec\left(\frac{5\pi}{3}\right) = \sec(300^\circ)$

Next, use the identities mentioned:

$\csc(210^\circ) = \csc(180^\circ + 30^\circ) = -\csc(30^\circ)$

$\sec(300^\circ) = \sec(360^\circ - 60^\circ) = \sec(60^\circ)$

Substituting these identities, we have:

$ \csc(210^\circ) \cdot \sec(300^\circ) = -\csc(30^\circ) \cdot \sec(60^\circ) $

Now, substituting the values for the trigonometric functions:

$\csc(30^\circ) = 2$

$\sec(60^\circ) = 2$

Thus, the expression simplifies to:

$ -\csc(30^\circ) \cdot \sec(60^\circ) = -2 \cdot 2 = -4 $

The final value is $-4$.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 18 April 2021
questions.question:
What is the value of the following?

tan 31° tan 33° tan 35° _ _ _ _ _ tan 57° tan 59°
questions.solution:
The expression given is the product of tangent functions of angles measured in degrees from $31^\circ$ to $59^\circ$, increasing in steps of $2^\circ$. This means:

$$ \tan 31^\circ \cdot \tan 33^\circ \cdot \tan 35^\circ \cdot \ldots \cdot \tan 57^\circ \cdot \tan 59^\circ $$

Utilizing a known trigonometric product identity:

$$ \tan(1^\circ) \tan(2^\circ) \cdot \ldots \cdot \tan(44^\circ) \tan(45^\circ) \tan(46^\circ) \ldots \tan(89^\circ) = 1 $$

In this particular setup, the product involves pairs of tangents such that:

$$ \tan(x^\circ) \cdot \tan((90^\circ-x)^\circ) = 1 $$

It means:

$\tan 31^\circ \cdot \tan 59^\circ = 1$

$\tan 33^\circ \cdot \tan 57^\circ = 1$

$\tan 35^\circ \cdot \tan 55^\circ = 1$

$\tan 37^\circ \cdot \tan 53^\circ = 1$

$\tan 39^\circ \cdot \tan 51^\circ = 1$

$\tan 41^\circ \cdot \tan 49^\circ = 1$

$\tan 43^\circ \cdot \tan 47^\circ = 1$

$\tan 45^\circ = 1$

Hence, the entire product of the tangent functions is:

$$ 1 \cdot 1 \cdot 1 \cdot 1 \cdot 1 \cdot 1 \cdot 1 \cdot 1 = 1 $$

Thus, the value of the product is $1$.

The correct option is C: 1

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 18 April 2021
questions.question:
What is the value of the following?

(sin 24° + cos 66°)(sin 24° - cos 66°)
questions.solution:
Concept:

When dealing with trigonometric identities, remember:

$\cos(90^\circ - \theta) = \sin \theta$

$\sin(90^\circ - \theta) = \cos \theta$

Calculation:

To find the value of $(\sin 24^\circ + \cos 66^\circ)(\sin 24^\circ - \cos 66^\circ)$:

Substitute $\cos 66^\circ$ with $\sin(90^\circ - 66^\circ)$:

$ = (\sin 24^\circ + \sin 24^\circ)(\sin 24^\circ - \sin 24^\circ) $

Simplify inside the parentheses:

$ = (2\sin 24^\circ) \times 0 $

Result:

$ = 0 $

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 18 April 2021
questions.question: What is (1 + cot θ - cosec θ)(1 + tan θ + sec θ) equal to?
questions.solution:
Concept:

Trigonometry Formulas

$\cot \theta = \frac{\cos \theta}{\sin \theta}$

$\tan \theta = \frac{\sin \theta}{\cos \theta}$

$\csc \theta = \frac{1}{\sin \theta}$

$\sec \theta = \frac{1}{\cos \theta}$

Additionally, the fundamental identities:

$\sin^2 \theta + \cos^2 \theta = 1$

$(\sin \theta + \cos \theta)^2 = \sin^2 \theta + \cos^2 \theta + 2 \sin \theta \cos \theta$

Calculation:

We need to find the value of $(1 + \cot \theta - \csc \theta)(1 + \tan \theta + \sec \theta)$.

$ = \left(1 + \frac{\cos \theta}{\sin \theta} - \frac{1}{\sin \theta}\right) \left(1 + \frac{\sin \theta}{\cos \theta} + \frac{1}{\cos \theta}\right) $

Simplifying, we have:

$ = \frac{(\sin \theta + \cos \theta - 1)(\sin \theta + \cos \theta + 1)}{\sin \theta \cos \theta} $

Applying the identity for the difference of squares:

$ = \frac{(\sin \theta + \cos \theta)^2 - 1}{\sin \theta \cos \theta} $

Expanding $(\sin \theta + \cos \theta)^2$:

$ = \frac{\sin^2 \theta + \cos^2 \theta + 2 \sin \theta \cos \theta - 1}{\sin \theta \cos \theta} $

Given that $\sin^2 \theta + \cos^2 \theta = 1$, this simplifies further to:

$ = \frac{1 + 2 \sin \theta \cos \theta - 1}{\sin \theta \cos \theta} $

This reduces to:

$ = \frac{2 \sin \theta \cos \theta}{\sin \theta \cos \theta} = 2 $

Hence, the answer is 2.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 18 April 2021
questions.question: What is $\rm \frac{1+tan^2\theta}{1+cot^2\theta}-\left(\frac{1-tan\theta}{1-cot\theta}\right)^2$equal to?
questions.solution:
Concept

Trigonometry Formula

$ \sec^2 \theta = 1 + \tan^2 \theta $

$ \csc^2 \theta = 1 + \cot^2 \theta $

$ \cot \theta = \frac{1}{\tan \theta} $

$ \sec \theta = \frac{1}{\cos \theta} $

$ \csc \theta = \frac{1}{\sin \theta} $

Calculation

Given the expression:

$ \frac{1+\tan^2\theta}{1+\cot^2\theta}-\left(\frac{1-\tan\theta}{1-\cot\theta}\right)^2 $

Substitute the trigonometric identities:

$ = \frac{\sec^2 \theta}{\csc^2 \theta} - \left(\frac{1 - \tan \theta}{1 - \frac{1}{\tan \theta}}\right)^2 $

Express the secant and cosecant in terms of sine and cosine:

$ = \frac{\frac{1}{\cos^2 \theta}}{\frac{1}{\sin^2 \theta}} - \left(\frac{1 - \tan \theta}{\frac{\tan \theta - 1}{\tan \theta}}\right)^2 $

Simplify the fractions:

$ = \frac{\sin^2 \theta}{\cos^2 \theta} - \left(\frac{1 - \tan \theta}{-\frac{1 - \tan \theta}{\tan \theta}}\right)^2 $

Simplify further:

$ = \tan^2 \theta - (-\tan \theta)^2 $

The final simplification:

$ = \tan^2 \theta - \tan^2 \theta = 0 $

The expression simplifies to 0.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 18 April 2021
questions.question: If 7 sinθ + 24 cosθ = 25, then what is the value of (sin θ + cos θ)?
questions.solution:
Concept:

The formulas for sine and cosine in a right triangle are:

$ \sin \theta = \frac{\text{Perpendicular}}{\text{Hypotenuse}} $

$ \cos \theta = \frac{\text{Base}}{\text{Hypotenuse}} $

Additionally, an important trigonometric identity is:

$ \sin^2 \theta + \cos^2 \theta = 1 $

Calculation:

Given the equation:

$ 7 \sin \theta + 24 \cos \theta = 25 $

Divide both sides by 25:

$ \frac{7}{25} \sin \theta + \frac{24}{25} \cos \theta = 1 \quad \text{...(i)} $

From the identity:

$ \sin^2 \theta + \cos^2 \theta = 1 \quad \text{...(ii)} $

On comparing equations (i) and (ii), we deduce:

$ \sin \theta = \frac{7}{25} $

$ \cos \theta = \frac{24}{25} $

Now, we find $(\sin \theta + \cos \theta)$:

$ \sin \theta + \cos \theta = \frac{7}{25} + \frac{24}{25} $

$ = \frac{31}{25} $

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 18 April 2021
questions.question: If 3cosθ = 4sinθ, then what is the value of tan (45° + θ)?
questions.solution:
Given the equation $ 3\cos\theta = 4\sin\theta $, we want to find the value of $ \tan(45^\circ + \theta) $.

First, find the value of $ \tan\theta $:

Divide both sides by $ \cos\theta $ to get:

$ 3 = 4 \tan\theta $

Solving for $ \tan\theta $, we get:

$ \tan\theta = \frac{3}{4} $

Next, use the tangent addition formula to find $ \tan(45^\circ + \theta) $:

$ \tan(45^\circ + \theta) = \frac{\tan 45^\circ + \tan\theta}{1 - \tan 45^\circ \tan\theta} $

Since $ \tan 45^\circ = 1 $, the formula becomes:

$ \tan(45^\circ + \theta) = \frac{1 + \frac{3}{4}}{1 - 1 \times \frac{3}{4}} $

Simplify the expression:

$ \tan(45^\circ + \theta) = \frac{\frac{4}{4} + \frac{3}{4}}{\frac{4}{4} - \frac{3}{4}} $

$ \tan(45^\circ + \theta) = \frac{\frac{7}{4}}{\frac{1}{4}} $

$ \tan(45^\circ + \theta) = 7 $

Therefore, the value of $ \tan(45^\circ + \theta) $ is 7. The correct answer is Option B: 7.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 18 April 2021
questions.question: The number of integer values of k, for which the equation 2sinx = 2k + 1 has a solution, is
questions.solution:
Concept:

Minimum and Maximum Values of $ a \sin x + b \cos x $

The expression $ a \sin x + b \cos x $ can be bounded by:

$ -\sqrt{a^2 + b^2} \leq a \sin x + b \cos x \leq \sqrt{a^2 + b^2} $

Calculation:

Applying this concept to the given equation:

$ -\sqrt{2^2 + 0^2} \leq 2 \sin x + 0 \cos x \leq \sqrt{2^2 + 0^2} $

This simplifies to:

$ -2 \leq 2 \sin x \leq 2 $

Thus, for the equation $ 2\sin x = 2k + 1 $ to have a solution, it must satisfy:

$ -2 \leq 2k + 1 \leq 2 $

Breaking it down:

$ -3 \leq 2k \leq 1 $

Dividing the entire inequality by 2:

$ -1.5 \leq k \leq 0.5 $

Therefore, the integer values for $ k $ within this range are $ k = 0 $ and $ k = -1 $.

Hence, the number of integer values of $ k $ for which the equation has a solution is two.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 18 April 2021
questions.question: What is cot 2x cot 4x - cot 4x cot 6x - cot 6x cot 2x equal to
questions.solution:
Concept:

The formula for the cotangent of the sum of two angles is:

$\text{cot}(A + B) = \frac{\text{cot} A \cdot \text{cot} B - 1}{\text{cot} A + \text{cot} B}$

Calculation:

We need to evaluate:

$(\text{cot} 2x \cdot \text{cot} 4x) - (\text{cot} 4x \cdot \text{cot} 6x) - (\text{cot} 6x \cdot \text{cot} 2x)$

First, notice the expression:

$(\text{cot} 2x \cdot \text{cot} 4x) - \text{cot} 6x (\text{cot} 4x + \text{cot} 2x)$

By applying the sum of cotangents:

$(\text{cot} 2x \cdot \text{cot} 4x) - \text{cot}(2x + 4x)(\text{cot} 4x + \text{cot} 2x)$

Using the identity:

$\text{cot}(6x) = \frac{\text{cot} 2x \cdot \text{cot} 4x - 1}{\text{cot} 2x + \text{cot} 4x}$

Substituting the identity, the expression becomes:

$(\text{cot} 2x \cdot \text{cot} 4x) - \left(\frac{\text{cot} 2x \cdot \text{cot} 4x - 1}{\text{cot} 2x + \text{cot} 4x}\right) \times (\text{cot} 4x + \text{cot} 2x)$

Simplifying further, we have:

$(\text{cot} 2x \cdot \text{cot} 4x) - (\text{cot} 2x \cdot \text{cot} 4x - 1)$

This yields:

$(\text{cot} 2x \cdot \text{cot} 4x) - (\text{cot} 2x \cdot \text{cot} 4x) + 1$

Finally, the simplified result is:

$1$

questions.title: BITSAT 2021
questions.question: If $${\cos ^3}x\,.\,\sin 2x = \sum\limits_{m = 1}^n {{a_m}\sin mx} $$ is identity in x, then
questions.solution:
$${\cos ^3}x\,.\,\sin 2x = {{\cos 3x + 3\cos x} \over 4} \times \sin 2x$$

$$ = {1 \over 8}(\sin 5x - \sin x) + {3 \over 8}(\sin 3x + \sin x)$$

$$ = {1 \over 4}\sin x + {3 \over 8}\sin 3x + {1 \over 8}\sin 5x$$

Here, $$n = 5,{a_1} = {1 \over 4},{a_2} = 0,{a_3} = {1 \over 8},{a_4} = 0,{a_5} = {1 \over 8}$$

questions.title: BITSAT 2021
questions.question: Total number of solutions of $$\left| {\cot x} \right| = \cot x + {1 \over {\sin x}},x \in [0,3\pi ]$$ is equal to
questions.solution:
$$\left| {\cot x} \right| = \cot x + {1 \over {\sin x}}$$

Let $$\cot x > 0 \Rightarrow \cot x = \cot x + {1 \over {\sin x}} = 0$$

$$ \Rightarrow {1 \over {\sin x}} = 0$$ which is not possible.

Let $$\cot x \le 0 \Rightarrow - \cot x = \cot x + {1 \over {\sin x}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow - 2\cot x = {1 \over {\sin x}} \Rightarrow \cos x = - {1 \over 2} \Rightarrow x = {{2\pi } \over 3},{{8\pi } \over 3}$$

$$\therefore$$ The number of solutions is 2.

questions.title: BITSAT 2021
questions.question: If nth division of main scale coincides with (n + 1)th division of vernier scale. The least count of the vernier is (Given, one main scale division is equal to a units)
questions.solution:
(n + 1) divisions of vernier scale = n division of main scale

$$\therefore$$ 1 VSD = $${n \over {n + 1}}$$ MSD

Least count = 1 MSD $$-$$ 1 VSD

= 1 MSD $$-$$ $${n \over {n + 1}}$$ MSD = $${1 \over {n + 1}}$$ MSD

= $${1 \over {n + 1}}$$ $$\times$$ a units = $${a \over {n + 1}}$$ units [$$\because$$ given, 1 MSD = a units]

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2021 Paper 1 Online
questions.question: The smallest division on the main scale of a Vernier calipers is 0.1 cm. Ten divisions of the Vernier scale correspond to nine divisions of the main scale. The figure below on the left shows the reading of this calipers with no gap between its two jaws. The figure on the right shows the reading with a solid sphere held between the jaws. The correct diameter of the sphere is
questions.solution:
Least count of Vernier calipers (L.C) = $$\left( {1 - {9 \over {10}}} \right)0.1$$ = 0.01 cm

We know that main scale reading (MSR) is the first reading on the main scale immediately to the left of the zero of the Vernier scale. But there are no marks on the main scale before zero of the Vernier scale. We claim that MSR = −0.1 cm. The Vernier scale reading is VSR = 6 and the least count is LC = 0.01 cm.

Substitute these values to get,

Zero Error = MSR + VSR $$ \times $$ LC

= -0.1 + 6 $$ \times $$ 0.01 = -0.04 cm

Now in the second figure, the reading from main scale is 3.1 cm will be added to 1st matching division of vernier so,

Reading = 3.1 cm + 1 $$ \times $$ L.C

= 3.1 cm + 0.01

= 3.11 cm

So correct diameter of the sphere

= 3.11 - (Zero Error)

= (3.11 + 0.04) cm

= 3.15 cm

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2021 Paper 2 Online
questions.question: A physical quantity $$\overrightarrow S $$ is defined as $$\overrightarrow S  = (\overrightarrow E  \times \overrightarrow B )/{\mu _0}$$, where $$\overrightarrow E $$ is electric field, $$\overrightarrow B $$ is magnetic field and $$\mu$$<sub>0</sub> is the permeability of free space. The dimensions of $$\overrightarrow S $$ are the same as the dimensions of which of the following quantity(ies)?
questions.solution:
Dimension of electric field $$[E] = [M{A^{ - 1}}L{T^{ - 3}}]$$

Dimensions of magnetic field [B]

$$[B] = [M{A^{ - 1}}L{T^{ - 2}}]$$

Dimensions of magnetic permebility

$$[{\mu _0}] = [M{A^{ - 2}}{T^{ - 2}}L]$$

$$[S] = {{EB} \over {{\mu _0}}} = {{[M{A^{ - 1}}L{T^{ - 3}}][M{A^{ - 1}}{T^{ - 2}}]} \over {[M{A^{ - 2}}{T^{ - 2}}L]}} = [M{T^{ - 3}}]$$

questions.title: BITSAT 2021
questions.question: The points with position vectors $$10\widehat i + 3\widehat j$$, $$12\widehat i - 5\widehat j$$ and $$a\widehat i + 11\widehat j$$ are collinear, if a is
questions.solution:
To determine if the points with position vectors $$10\widehat{i} + 3\widehat{j}$$, $$12\widehat{i} - 5\widehat{j}$$, and $$a\widehat{i} + 11\widehat{j}$$ are collinear, we need to check if the vectors between these points are scalar multiples of each other. This is equivalent to checking if the determinant of the matrix formed by these points is zero.

Let's consider the vectors formed by the points:

  • Vector 1, $$\mathbf{AB}$$: from $$10\widehat{i} + 3\widehat{j}$$ to $$12\widehat{i} - 5\widehat{j}$$
  • Vector 2, $$\mathbf{AC}$$: from $$10\widehat{i} + 3\widehat{j}$$ to $$a\widehat{i} + 11\widehat{j}$$

We can find these vectors as follows:

$$\mathbf{AB} = (12 - 10)\widehat{i} + (-5 - 3)\widehat{j}$$

$$\mathbf{AB} = 2\widehat{i} - 8\widehat{j}$$

$$\mathbf{AC} = (a - 10)\widehat{i} + (11 - 3)\widehat{j}$$

$$\mathbf{AC} = (a - 10)\widehat{i} + 8\widehat{j}$$

For the points to be collinear, $$\mathbf{AB}$$ and $$\mathbf{AC}$$ must be linearly dependent, such that:

$$\mathbf{AB} = k \cdot \mathbf{AC}$$ for some scalar $$k$$

Equating the components:

$$2\widehat{i} - 8\widehat{j} = k \cdot \left((a - 10)\widehat{i} + 8\widehat{j}\right)$$

Separating the components gives us two equations:

1. $$2 = k(a - 10)$$

2. $$-8 = k \cdot 8$$

From the second equation:

$$k = -1$$

Substituting $$k = -1$$ into the first equation:

$$2 = -1 \cdot (a - 10)$$

$$2 = -a + 10$$

Solving for $$a$$:

$$a = 10 - 2$$

$$a = 8$$

Thus, the points will be collinear if $$a = 8$$.

questions.title: BITSAT 2021
questions.question: Let a, b, c be vectors of lengths 3, 4, 5 respectively and a be perpendicular to (b + c), b to (c + a) and c to (a + b), then the value of (a + b + c) is
questions.solution:
We have, | a | = 3, | b | = 4 and | c | = 5.

It is given that

a $$ \bot $$ (b + c), b $$ \bot $$ (c + a) and c $$ \bot $$ (a + b)

$$\Rightarrow$$ a . (b + c) = 0, b . (c + a) = 0 and c . (a + b) = 0

$$\Rightarrow$$ a . b + a . c = b . c + b . a = c . a = c . b = 0

or, a . b + b . c + c . a = 0

(adding all the above equations)

Now, | a + b + c |2 = | a |2 + | b |2 + | c |2 + 2 (a . b + b . c + c . a)

= 32 + 42 + 52 = 50

$$\therefore$$ | a + b + c | = 5$$\sqrt2$$

questions.title: BITSAT 2021
questions.question: For non-zero vectors a, b, c; |(a $$\times$$ b) . c| = |a| |b| |c| holds if and only if
options: a . b = b . c = c . a = 0
questions.solution:
We have, $$\left| {(a \times b).\,c} \right| = \left| a \right|\left| b \right|\left| c \right|$$

$$ \Rightarrow \left\| a \right\|\left\| b \right\|\left| c \right|\left. {\sin \theta \cos \alpha } \right| = \left| a \right|\left| b \right|\left| c \right|$$

$$ \Rightarrow \left| {\sin \theta } \right|\left| {\cos \alpha } \right| = 1 \Rightarrow \theta = {\pi \over 2}$$ and $$\alpha = 0$$

$$ \Rightarrow a \bot b$$ and $$c||\widehat n \Rightarrow a \bot b$$ and c $$\bot$$ both a and b.

$$\Rightarrow$$ a, b, c are mutually perpendicular.

$$\Rightarrow$$ a . b = b . c = c . a = 0

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2021 Paper 1 Online
questions.question:
Let $$\overrightarrow u $$, $$\overrightarrow v $$ and $$\overrightarrow w $$ be vectors in three-dimensional space, where $$\overrightarrow u $$ and $$\overrightarrow v $$ are unit vectors which are not perpendicular to each other and $$\overrightarrow u $$ . $$\overrightarrow w $$ = 1, $$\overrightarrow v $$ . $$\overrightarrow w $$ = 1, $$\overrightarrow w $$ . $$\overrightarrow w $$ = 4

If the volume of the paralleopiped, whose adjacent sides are represented by the vectors, $$\overrightarrow u $$, $$\overrightarrow v $$ and $$\overrightarrow w $$, is $$\sqrt 2 $$, then the value of $$\left| {3\overrightarrow u + 5\overrightarrow v } \right|$$ is ___________.
questions.solution:
Given: $|\vec{u}|=1,|\vec{v}|=1$

$\vec{u}$ is not perpendicular to $\vec{v}$ $\vec{u} \cdot \vec{v} \neq 0$

And $\vec{u} \cdot \vec{w}=1 \quad \vec{v} \cdot \vec{w}=1 \quad \vec{w} \cdot \vec{w}=4$

$$ \begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \vec{w} \cdot \vec{w}=|\vec{w}|^2=4 \\\\ & \Rightarrow|\vec{w}|=2 \end{aligned} $$

Volume of parallelepiped with $\vec{u}, \vec{v}$ and $\vec{w}$ as its sides,

$$ \begin{aligned} & =[\vec{u} \vec{v} \vec{w}]=\sqrt{2} \\\\ & \text { Now, }[\vec{u} \vec{v} \vec{w}]^2=\left|\begin{array}{ccc} \vec{u} \cdot \vec{u} & \vec{u} \cdot \vec{v} & \vec{u} \cdot \vec{w} \\ \vec{v} \cdot \vec{u} & \vec{v} \cdot \vec{v} & \vec{v} \cdot \vec{w} \\ \vec{w} \cdot \vec{u} & \vec{w} \cdot \vec{v} & \vec{w} \cdot \vec{w} \end{array}\right|=2 \\\\ & \Rightarrow\left|\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & \vec{u} \cdot \vec{v} & 1 \\ \vec{v} \cdot \vec{u} & 1 & 1 \\ 1 & 1 & 4 \end{array}\right|=2 \end{aligned} $$

$$ \begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow 1(4-1)-\vec{u} \cdot \vec{v}(4 \vec{u} \cdot \vec{v}-1)+1(\vec{u} \cdot \vec{v}-1)=2 \\\\ & \Rightarrow 3-4(\vec{u} \vec{v})^2+\vec{u} \vec{v}+\vec{u} \vec{v}-1=2 \\\\ & \Rightarrow-4(\vec{u} \vec{v})^2+2 \vec{u} \vec{v}+2=2 \\\\ & \Rightarrow-4(\vec{u} \vec{v})^2+2 \vec{u} \vec{v}=0 \\\\ & \Rightarrow 2 \vec{u} \vec{v}(-2 \vec{u} \vec{v}+1)=0 \\\\ & \Rightarrow \vec{u} \cdot \vec{v}=\frac{1}{2}, \vec{u} \cdot \vec{v} \neq 0 \\\\ & \text { Now, }|3 \vec{u}+5 \vec{v}|=\sqrt{9+25+30\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)} \\\\ & \Rightarrow|3 \vec{u}+5 \vec{v}|=\sqrt{49} \\\\ & \Rightarrow|3 \vec{u}+5 \vec{v}|=7 \end{aligned} $$

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2021 Paper 2 Online
questions.question: Let O be the origin and $$\overrightarrow {OA}  = 2\widehat i + 2\widehat j + \widehat k$$ and $$\overrightarrow {OB}  = \widehat i - 2\widehat j + 2\widehat k$$ and $$\overrightarrow {OC}  = {1 \over 2}\left( {\overrightarrow {OB}  - \lambda \overrightarrow {OA} } \right)$$ for some $$\lambda$$ > 0. If $$\left| {\overrightarrow {OB}  \times \overrightarrow {OC} } \right| = {9 \over 2}$$, then which of the following statements is (are) TRUE?
options: Area of the triangle OAB is $${9 \over 2}$$
Area of the triangle ABC is $${9 \over 2}$$
The acute angle between the diagonals of the parallelogram with adjacent sides $${\overrightarrow {OA} }$$ and $${\overrightarrow {OC} }$$ is $${\pi  \over 3}$$
questions.solution:
Given,

$$\overrightarrow {OA} = 2\widehat i + 2\widehat j + 2\widehat k$$

$$\overrightarrow {OB} = \widehat i - \widehat j + 2\widehat k$$

and $$\overrightarrow {OC} = {1 \over 2}(\overrightarrow {OB} - \lambda \overrightarrow {OA} )$$

Also, $$\left| {OB \times OC} \right| = 9/2$$ ..... (i)

Now, $$\overrightarrow {OB} \times \overrightarrow {OC} = \overrightarrow {OB} \times {1 \over 2}(\overrightarrow {OB} - \lambda \overrightarrow {OA} )$$

$$ = {{ - \lambda } \over 2}\overrightarrow {OB} \times \overrightarrow {OA} = {\lambda \over 2}(\overrightarrow {OA} \times \overrightarrow {OB} )$$

We need to find $$\overrightarrow {OA} \times \overrightarrow {OB} $$ for this,

($$\because$$ $$\overrightarrow a \times \overrightarrow b = - \overrightarrow b \times \overrightarrow a $$ and $$\overrightarrow a \times \overrightarrow a $$ = 0)

$$\overrightarrow {OA} \times \overrightarrow {OB} = \left| {\matrix{ {\widehat i} & {\widehat j} & {\widehat k} \cr 2 & 2 & 1 \cr 1 & { - 2} & 2 \cr } } \right|$$

$$ = 6\widehat i - 3\widehat j - 6\widehat k = 3(2\widehat i - \widehat j - 2\widehat k)$$

So, $$\overrightarrow {OB} \times \overrightarrow {OC} = {{3\lambda } \over 2}(2\widehat i - \widehat j - 2\widehat k)$$

From Eq. (i), $$\left| {{{3\lambda } \over 2}\sqrt {4 + 1 + 4} } \right| = 9/2$$

$$ \Rightarrow \left| {{{9\lambda } \over 2}} \right| = 9/2 \Rightarrow {9 \over 2}\left| \lambda \right| = {9 \over 2}$$

$$ \Rightarrow \left| \lambda \right| = 1 \Rightarrow \lambda = \pm 1$$

But $$\lambda$$ > 0

$$\therefore$$ $$\lambda$$ = 1

So, $$\overrightarrow {OC} = {1 \over 2}(\overrightarrow {OB} - \overrightarrow {OA} )$$ (By putting $$\lambda$$ = 1)

$$ \Rightarrow \overrightarrow {OC} = {1 \over 2}( - \widehat i - 4\widehat j + \widehat k) = - {1 \over 2}\widehat i - 2\widehat j + {1 \over 2}\widehat k$$

Option (a)

Projection of $${\overrightarrow {OC} }$$ and $${\overrightarrow {OA} }$$ $$ = {{\overrightarrow {OC} \,.\,\overrightarrow {OA} } \over {\left| {\overrightarrow {OA} } \right|}}$$

$$ = {{{1 \over 2}( - 2 - 8 + 1)} \over 3} = {{ - 3} \over 2}$$

Option (b)

Area of $$\Delta OAB = {1 \over 2}\left| {\overrightarrow {OA} \times \overrightarrow {OB} } \right| = {1 \over 2}\left| {\overrightarrow {OA} } \right|\left. {\overrightarrow {OB} } \right|\sin 90^\circ $$

$$ = {1 \over 2}\left| {3 \times 3} \right| = {9 \over 2}$$

Option (c)

Area of the $$\Delta ABC = {1 \over 2}\left| {\overrightarrow {AB} \times \overrightarrow {AC} } \right|$$

$$ = {1 \over 2}\left\| {\matrix{ {\widehat i} & {\widehat j} & {\widehat k} \cr { - 1} & { - 4} & 1 \cr { - 5/2} & { - 4} & {{{ - 1} \over 2}} \cr } } \right\|$$

$$ = {1 \over 2}\left| {6\widehat i - 3\widehat j - 6\widehat k} \right| = {1 \over 2} \times 3 \times 3 = {9 \over 2}$$

Option (d)

The acute angle between the diagonals of the parallelogram with adjacent sides $${\overrightarrow {OA} }$$ and $${\overrightarrow {OC} }$$ = $$\theta$$

$$ \Rightarrow {{(\overrightarrow {OA} + \overrightarrow {OC} )\,.\,(\overrightarrow {OA} - \overrightarrow {OC} )} \over {\left| {\overrightarrow {OA} + \overrightarrow {OC} } \right|\left| {\overrightarrow {OA} - \overrightarrow {OC} } \right|}} = \cos \theta $$

$$ \Rightarrow \cos \theta = {{\left( {{3 \over 2}\widehat i + {3 \over 2}\widehat k} \right)\,.\,\left( {{5 \over 2}\widehat i + 4\widehat j + {1 \over 2}\widehat k} \right)} \over {{3 \over 2}\sqrt 2 \times \sqrt {{{90} \over 4}} }}$$

$$ = {{18} \over {3\sqrt 2 \times \sqrt {90} }} = {1 \over {\sqrt 5 }} \ne {1 \over 2}$$ [$$\therefore$$ $$\theta$$ $$\ne$$ $$\pi$$/3]

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14 November 2021
questions.question:
Let $\rm \vec{a}, \vec{b}$ and $\rm\vec{c}$ be unit vectors such that $\rm\vec{a} \times \vec{b}$ is perpendicular to $\vec{c}$. If θ is the angle between $\rm\vec{a}$ and $\rm\vec{b}$, then which of the following is/are correct?

1. $\rm\vec{a} \times \vec{b} = sin ~\theta~ \vec{c} $

2. $\rm\vec {a} \cdot (\vec{b}\times \vec{c})=0$

Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.solution:
Given:

$|\vec{a}| = |\vec{b}| = |\vec{c}| = 1$ (All are unit vectors)

$\vec{a} \times \vec{b}$ is perpendicular to $\vec{c}$.

Concept:

For two vectors $\vec{a}$ and $\vec{b}$, their cross product is given by:

$ \vec{a} \times \vec{b} = |\vec{a}| |\vec{b}| \sin \theta \, \hat{n} $

where $\hat{n}$ is the unit vector indicating the direction of $\vec{a} \times \vec{b}$. The direction of $\vec{a} \times \vec{b}$ is perpendicular to both $\vec{a}$ and $\vec{b}$.

Scalar Triple Product:

$ [\overrightarrow{a} \, \overrightarrow{b} \, \overrightarrow{c}] = \vec{a} \cdot (\vec{b} \times \vec{c}) $

If any two vectors among $\vec{a}$, $\vec{b}$, and $\vec{c}$ are equal, the scalar triple product is zero.

Explanation:

Statement 1:

The cross product $\vec{a} \times \vec{b}$ is:

$ \vec{a} \times \vec{b} = |\vec{a}| |\vec{b}| \sin \theta \, \hat{n} $

Since $\hat{n}$ is a unit vector in the direction of $\vec{a} \times \vec{b}$, statement 1 stating $\vec{a} \times \vec{b} = \sin \theta \, \vec{c}$ is incorrect. This is because $\vec{c}$ and $\hat{n}$ are perpendicular to each other.

Hence, statement 1 is incorrect.

Statement 2:

Given the property of the scalar triple product:

$ [\vec{a} \, \vec{b} \, \vec{c}] = \vec{c} \cdot (\vec{a} \times \vec{b}) $

Since $\vec{a} \times \vec{b}$ and $\vec{c}$ are perpendicular, we have:

$ \vec{c} \cdot (\vec{a} \times \vec{b}) = 0 $

Thus, $\vec{a} \cdot (\vec{b} \times \vec{c}) = 0$.

So, statement 2 is correct.

Therefore, statement 2 is the correct option.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14 November 2021
questions.question: If $\rm\vec{a}+3\vec{b} = 3\hat{i}- \hat{j}$ and $\rm2\vec{a}+\vec{b} = \hat{i}- 2\hat{j}$, then what is the angle between $\rm\vec{a}$ and $\rm\vec{b}$
questions.solution:
Concept:

If θ is the angle between vectors $\vec{a}$ and $\vec{b}$, then

$ \cos \theta = \frac{\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}}{|\vec{a}| |\vec{b}|} $

Calculation:

Let θ be the angle between vectors $\vec{a}$ and $\vec{b}$.

Given:

$ \vec{a} + 3\vec{b} = 3\hat{i} - \hat{j} \quad \text{....(i)} $

$ 2\vec{a} + \vec{b} = \hat{i} - 2\hat{j} \quad \text{....(ii)} $

To find $\vec{b}$, multiply equation (i) by 2 and then subtract equation (ii):

$ 2(\vec{a} + 3\vec{b}) - (2\vec{a} + \vec{b}) = 2(3\hat{i} - \hat{j}) - (\hat{i} - 2\hat{j}) $

$ 2\vec{a} + 6\vec{b} - 2\vec{a} - \vec{b} = 6\hat{i} - 2\hat{j} - \hat{i} + 2\hat{j} $

$ 5\vec{b} = 5\hat{i} $

$ \vec{b} = \hat{i} $

Substituting $\vec{b} = \hat{i}$ back into equation (i), we find:

$ \vec{a} + 3\hat{i} = 3\hat{i} - \hat{j} $

$ \vec{a} = -\hat{j} $

Now, calculate the dot product $\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}$:

$ \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b} = (-\hat{j}) \cdot \hat{i} = 0 $

Using the concept:

$ \cos \theta = \frac{\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}}{|\vec{a}| |\vec{b}|} = 0 $

$ \theta = \cos^{-1}(0) $

$ \theta = \frac{\pi}{2} $

∴ The angle between $\vec{a}$ and $\vec{b}$ is $\frac{\pi}{2}$.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14 November 2021
questions.question: If $\rm(\vec {a} + \vec{b})$ is perpendicular to $\rm\vec {a}$ and magnitude of $\rm\vec {b}$ is twice that of $\rm\vec {a}$, then what is the value of $\rm(4\vec {a} + \vec{b})\cdot \vec{b}$ equal to?
questions.solution:
Concept:

If $(\vec{a} + \vec{b})$ is perpendicular to $\vec{a}$, then:

$ (\vec{a} + \vec{b}) \cdot \vec{a} = 0 $

Calculation:

From the equation above:

$ |\vec{a}|^2 + \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b} = 0 $

Given that the magnitude of $\vec{b}$ is twice that of $\vec{a}$:

$ |\vec{b}| = 2 \times |\vec{a}| $

This can be written as:

$ \frac{|\vec{b}|^2}{4} + \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b} = 0 $

Solving for $\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}$:

$ \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b} = -\frac{|\vec{b}|^2}{4} \quad \text{-----(1)} $

To find $(4\vec{a} + \vec{b}) \cdot \vec{b}$:

$ (4\vec{a} + \vec{b}) \cdot \vec{b} = 4(\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}) + |\vec{b}|^2 \quad \text{----(2)} $

Substituting equation (1) into equation (2):

$ (4\vec{a} + \vec{b}) \cdot \vec{b} = 4\left(-\frac{|\vec{b}|^2}{4}\right) + |\vec{b}|^2 = -|\vec{b}|^2 + |\vec{b}|^2 = 0 $

∴ The value of $(4\vec {a} + \vec{b})\cdot \vec{b}$ is equal to 0.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14 November 2021
questions.question:
Let $\rm\vec {a}, \vec{b}$ and $ \rm \vec {c}$ be three vectors such that  $\rm\vec {a}, \vec{b}$ and $ \rm \vec {c}$  are co-planar. Which of the following is/are correct?

1. $\rm(\vec{a}\times \vec{b})\times \vec{c}$ is co-planar with $\rm\vec {a}$ and $\rm\vec {b}$

2. $\rm(\vec{a}\times \vec{b})\times \vec{c}$ is perpendicular to $\rm\vec {a}$ and $\rm\vec {b}$

Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.solution:
Concept:

If vectors $\rm\vec {a}, \rm\vec {b}$, and $\rm\vec {c}$ are co-planar, then:

$ \rm \vec{a} \cdot (\rm \vec{b} \times \rm \vec{c}) = 0 $

The vector $\rm \vec{a} \times \rm \vec{b}$ is perpendicular to both $\rm\vec {a}$ and $\rm\vec {b}$.

Calculation:

Let's consider:

$ \rm(\vec{a}\times \vec{b})\times \vec{c} = \rm\vec {d} \quad (\text{Let}) $

$\rm\vec {d}$ is perpendicular to both $\rm \vec{a} \times \rm \vec{b}$ and $\rm \vec {c}$.

$\rm \vec{a} \times \rm \vec{b}$ is perpendicular to $\rm\vec {a}$ and $\rm\vec {b}$.

Therefore, $\rm\vec {d}$ or $\rm(\vec{a}\times \vec{b})\times \vec{c}$ is co-planar with vectors $\rm\vec {a}$ and $\rm\vec {b}$.

Conclusion:

Statement 1 is correct because $(\vec{a}\times \vec{b})\times \vec{c}$ is co-planar with $\rm\vec {a}$ and $\rm\vec {b}$.

Statement 2 is incorrect; for statement 2 to be true, vector $\rm\vec {d}$ must be parallel or anti-parallel to $\rm \vec{a}\times\rm \vec{b}$, which contradicts the explanation for statement 1.

Thus, the best choice is option 1.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14 November 2021
questions.question: If the position vectors of A and B are (√2 - 1)î - ĵ and î + (√2 + 1)ĵ respectively, then what is the magnitude of $\rm \vec{AB}$?
questions.solution:
Concept:

If $ A $ and $ B $ are points with position vectors $\vec{a}$ and $\vec{b}$ respectively, then $\vec{AB} = \vec{b} - \vec{a}$.

If $\vec{a} = a_1\hat{i} + a_2\hat{j} + a_3\hat{k}$ is a vector, then the magnitude of the vector is given by $|\vec{a}| = \sqrt{a_1^2 + a_2^2 + a_3^2}$.

Calculation:

Given:

Position vector of $ A = (\sqrt{2} - 1) \hat{i} - \hat{j} $

Position vector of $ B = \hat{i} + (\sqrt{2} + 1) \hat{j} $

To find $\vec{AB}$:

$ \vec{AB} = \vec{b} - \vec{a} = [\hat{i} + (\sqrt{2} + 1) \hat{j}] - [(\sqrt{2} - 1) \hat{i} + \hat{j}] $

Simplifying:

$ \vec{AB} = (-\sqrt{2} + 2) \hat{i} + (\sqrt{2} + 2) \hat{j} $

Now, calculate the magnitude of $\vec{AB}$:

$ |\vec{AB}| = \sqrt{(-\sqrt{2} + 2)^2 + (\sqrt{2} + 2)^2} $

Solving this:

$ |\vec{AB}| = \sqrt{((- \sqrt{2} + 2)^2) + ((\sqrt{2} + 2)^2)} = \sqrt{12} $

Thus:

$ |\vec{AB}| = 2\sqrt{3} $

Therefore, the magnitude of $\vec{AB}$ is $\mathbf{2\sqrt{3}}$.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 18 April 2021
questions.question: What is the projection of the line segment joining A(1, 7, -5) and B(-3, 4, -2) on y-axis?
questions.solution:
To find the projection of the line segment joining points $ A(1, 7, -5) $ and $ B(-3, 4, -2) $ on the y-axis, we need to look at the change in the y-coordinates of these points.

The y-coordinate of point $ A $ is 7, and the y-coordinate of point $ B $ is 4. The projection of the line segment on the y-axis is the absolute difference between these y-coordinates.

The formula to find this projection is:

$$ \text{Projection on y-axis} = |y_2 - y_1| $$

Substituting the given values:

$$ \text{Projection on y-axis} = |4 - 7| = |-3| = 3 $$

Thus, the projection of the line segment on the y-axis is 3.

Option C: 3

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 18 April 2021
questions.question: A vector $\vec r=a \hat i+b \hat j$ is equally inclined to both x and y axes. If the magnitude of the vector is 2 units, then what are the values of a and b respectively?
questions.solution:
The condition that a vector $\vec{r} = a \hat{i} + b \hat{j}$ is equally inclined to both the x and y axes implies that the coefficients $a$ and $b$ are equal, i.e., $a = b$.

Given that the magnitude of the vector $\vec{r}$ is 2 units, we use the formula for the magnitude of a vector:

$$ |\vec{r}| = \sqrt{a^2 + b^2} $$

Substituting $a = b$ into the magnitude equation:

$$ \sqrt{a^2 + a^2} = 2 $$

This simplifies to:

$$ \sqrt{2a^2} = 2 $$

Squaring both sides gives:

$$ 2a^2 = 4 $$

Dividing both sides by 2:

$$ a^2 = 2 $$

Taking the square root of both sides:

$$ a = \pm \sqrt{2} $$

Since $a = b$:

$$ b = \pm \sqrt{2} $$

Hence, the values of $a$ and $b$ are $\sqrt{2}$ and $\sqrt{2}$, respectively.

The correct option is:

Option C: $\sqrt{2}, \sqrt{2}$

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 18 April 2021
questions.question:
Consider the following statements in respect of a vector $\vec c=\vec a+\vec b$, where $|\vec a|=|\vec b|\ne0$:

1. $\vec c$ is perpendicular to $(\vec a-\vec b).$

2. $\vec c$ is perpendicular to $\vec a \times \vec b.$

Which of the above statement is/are correct?
questions.solution:
CONCEPT:

The scalar triple product of three vectors is zero if any two of the vectors are equal.

A vector $\vec{a}$ is perpendicular to another vector $\vec{b}$ if and only if $\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b} = 0$.

CALCULATION:

Given: $\vec{c} = \vec{a} + \vec{b}$, where $|\vec{a}| = |\vec{b}| \neq 0$.

Statement 1: $\vec{c}$ is perpendicular to $(\vec{a} - \vec{b})$.

To determine this, calculate $\vec{c} \cdot (\vec{a} - \vec{b}) = (\vec{a} + \vec{b}) \cdot (\vec{a} - \vec{b})$:

$ (\vec{a} + \vec{b}) \cdot (\vec{a} - \vec{b}) = |\vec{a}|^2 - \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b} + \vec{b} \cdot \vec{a} - |\vec{b}|^2 $

Since $\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b} = \vec{b} \cdot \vec{a}$, this simplifies to:

$ (\vec{a} + \vec{b}) \cdot (\vec{a} - \vec{b}) = |\vec{a}|^2 - |\vec{b}|^2 $

Given $|\vec{a}| = |\vec{b}|$, it follows that:

$ |\vec{a}|^2 - |\vec{b}|^2 = 0 $

Thus, $\vec{c} \cdot (\vec{a} - \vec{b}) = 0$, confirming that $\vec{c}$ is indeed perpendicular to $(\vec{a} - \vec{b})$. Therefore, statement 1 is true.

Statement 2: $\vec{c}$ is perpendicular to $\vec{a} \times \vec{b}$.

Calculate $\vec{c} \cdot (\vec{a} \times \vec{b}) = (\vec{a} + \vec{b}) \cdot (\vec{a} \times \vec{b})$:

$ (\vec{a} + \vec{b}) \cdot (\vec{a} \times \vec{b}) = \vec{a} \cdot (\vec{a} \times \vec{b}) + \vec{b} \cdot (\vec{a} \times \vec{b}) $

According to the properties of the scalar triple product, this is zero, as any scalar triple product with two of the same vectors is zero:

$ (\vec{a} + \vec{b}) \cdot (\vec{a} \times \vec{b}) = 0 $

Hence, statement 2 is also true.

Both statements are correct.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 18 April 2021
questions.question: If $\vec a \:and\: \vec b$ are two vectors such that $|\vec a + \vec b|= |\vec a - \vec b|=4,$ then which one of the following is correct?
options: $\vec a \:and\: \vec b$ must be unit vectors.
$\vec a$ must be parallel to $\vec b.$
$\vec a$ must be perpendicular to $\vec b.$
$\vec a$ must be equal to $\vec b.$
questions.solution:
Given two vectors $ \vec{a} $ and $ \vec{b} $, the conditions $ |\vec{a} + \vec{b}| = 4 $ and $ |\vec{a} - \vec{b}| = 4 $ imply specific properties about the relationship between $ \vec{a} $ and $ \vec{b} $.

First, consider the equation:

$ |\vec{a} + \vec{b}|^2 = 16 \rightarrow (\vec{a} + \vec{b}) \cdot (\vec{a} + \vec{b}) = 16 $

Expanding the dot product:

$ \vec{a} \cdot \vec{a} + 2(\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}) + \vec{b} \cdot \vec{b} = 16 $

Similarly, consider:

$ |\vec{a} - \vec{b}|^2 = 16 \rightarrow (\vec{a} - \vec{b}) \cdot (\vec{a} - \vec{b}) = 16 $

Expanding this dot product gives:

$ \vec{a} \cdot \vec{a} - 2(\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}) + \vec{b} \cdot \vec{b} = 16 $

Now, let's subtract the second equation from the first one:

$ [\vec{a} \cdot \vec{a} + 2(\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}) + \vec{b} \cdot \vec{b}] - [\vec{a} \cdot \vec{a} - 2(\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}) + \vec{b} \cdot \vec{b}] = 0 $

Simplifying:

$ 4(\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}) = 0 $

This gives:

$ \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b} = 0 $

The condition $\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b} = 0$ indicates that the vectors $ \vec{a} $ and $ \vec{b} $ are perpendicular to each other. Thus, the correct option is:

Option C: $ \vec{a} $ must be perpendicular to $ \vec{b}. $

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 18 April 2021
questions.question: If $\vec a, \vec b\:and \: \vec c$ are coplanar, then what is $(2\vec a\times 3\vec b)\cdot4\vec c+(5\vec b\times 3\vec c)\cdot6\vec a$ equal to?
questions.solution:
For vectors $ \vec{a}, \vec{b}, $ and $ \vec{c} $ to be coplanar, the scalar triple product $ \vec{a} \cdot (\vec{b} \times \vec{c}) = 0 $ holds true. Given the expression $(2\vec{a} \times 3\vec{b}) \cdot 4\vec{c} + (5\vec{b} \times 3\vec{c}) \cdot 6\vec{a}$, let's simplify it:

The expression can be rewritten using the distributive property of the dot and cross products:

Simplify $(2\vec{a} \times 3\vec{b}) \cdot 4\vec{c}$:

$ (2\vec{a} \times 3\vec{b}) \cdot 4\vec{c} = 2 \cdot 3 \cdot 4 (\vec{a} \times \vec{b}) \cdot \vec{c} = 24 (\vec{a} \times \vec{b}) \cdot \vec{c} $

Simplify $(5\vec{b} \times 3\vec{c}) \cdot 6\vec{a}$:

$ (5\vec{b} \times 3\vec{c}) \cdot 6\vec{a} = 5 \cdot 3 \cdot 6 (\vec{b} \times \vec{c}) \cdot \vec{a} = 90 (\vec{b} \times \vec{c}) \cdot \vec{a} $

Now, consider the property of scalar triple product stating that:

$ (\vec{a} \times \vec{b}) \cdot \vec{c} = \vec{a} \cdot (\vec{b} \times \vec{c}) $

Therefore, both expressions simplify to zero:

$ 24 (\vec{a} \times \vec{b}) \cdot \vec{c} = 24 \cdot \vec{0} = 0 $

$ 90 (\vec{b} \times \vec{c}) \cdot \vec{a} = 90 \cdot \vec{0} = 0 $

Adding these results gives:

$ 24 (\vec{a} \times \vec{b}) \cdot \vec{c} + 90 (\vec{b} \times \vec{c}) \cdot \vec{a} = 0 + 0 = 0 $

Thus, the expression evaluates to 0. Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option C: 0

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 18 April 2021
questions.question:
Consider the following statements:

1. The cross product of two unit vectors is always a unit vector.

2. The dot product of two unit vectors is always unity.

3. The magnitude of sum of two unit vectors is always greater than the magnitude of their difference.

Which of the above statements are not correct?
questions.solution:
Concept:

The cross product and dot product are operations that can be performed on vectors. Understanding these concepts will help us evaluate the statements.

Cross Product of Two Vectors:

If $\vec{a}$ and $\vec{b}$ are vectors, their cross product is given by:

$ \vec{a} \times \vec{b} = |\vec{a}| \cdot |\vec{b}| \cdot \sin \theta \cdot \hat{n} $

Here, $\theta$ is the angle between the vectors, and $\hat{n}$ is the unit vector perpendicular to both $\vec{a}$ and $\vec{b}$.

Dot Product of Two Vectors:

If $\vec{a}$ and $\vec{b}$ are vectors, their dot product is given by:

$ \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b} = |\vec{a}| \cdot |\vec{b}| \cdot \cos \theta $

Unit Vectors:

If $\vec{a}$ is a unit vector, then $|\vec{a}| = 1$.

Calculations:

Statement 1: "The cross product of two unit vectors is always a unit vector."

For $\vec{a}$ and $\vec{b}$ as unit vectors ($|\vec{a}| = |\vec{b}| = 1$), the magnitude of their cross product is:

$ |\vec{a} \times \vec{b}| = \sin \theta $

Since $\sin \theta$ ranges from -1 to 1, the cross product's magnitude can vary and is not always 1. Therefore, statement 1 is false.

Statement 2: "The dot product of two unit vectors is always unity."

For unit vectors $\vec{a}$ and $\vec{b}$, the dot product is:

$ |\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}| = \cos \theta $

Here, $\cos \theta$ also ranges from -1 to 1, meaning the dot product is not always 1. Therefore, statement 2 is false.

Statement 3: "The magnitude of the sum of two unit vectors is always greater than the magnitude of their difference."

Consider vectors $\vec{a} = \hat{i}$ and $\vec{b} = \hat{j}$, both unit vectors.

$ |\hat{i} + \hat{j}| = \sqrt{2} \quad \text{and} \quad |\hat{i} - \hat{j}| = \sqrt{2} $

In this case, the magnitude of the sum equals the magnitude of the difference, so statement 3 is false.

Thus, each statement provided is not correct.

questions.title: BITSAT 2021
questions.question: Decay is an $$\underline {immutable} $$ factor of human life.
questions.solution:
Option C :

unchangeable

Explanation :

The word 'immutable' means unchanging over time or unable to be changed. When used in the sentence "Decay is an immutable factor of human life," it emphasizes that decay (or aging, deterioration over time) is a constant, unchanging aspect of human life that we cannot alter or avoid.

Let's examine the other options :

Option A : Important - While decay might be considered an important factor in human life due to its inevitability, this doesn't capture the meaning of 'immutable,' which has more to do with being unchangeable rather than being important.

Option B : Unique - 'Unique' means being the only one of its kind, unlike anything else. This doesn't fit with the meaning of 'immutable.'
